entries.description: Moderato. One of the least difficult of the waltzes. A touch of melancholy is found throughout the wandering melodic line. Primarily single-note writing in the A section against an oom-pah-pah accompaniment. Chopin published several editions of the waltzes simultaneously, with occasional slight differences between them. Level 8.

entries.title: Waltz in F Minor, Op. 70, No. 2, Op. Posth.
entries.description: Tempo giusto. Much expansion and contraction in the right hand melody. Suitable for the pianist who can inflect a long line. One of the easiest although not the most interesting of the Chopin waltzes. Level 9.

entries.title: Waltz in E Minor, Op. Posth.
entries.description: Vivace. Sounds more difficult than it is. Effective and brilliant in performance. Needs a fluid right-hand technique. Level 10.

entries.title: Waltz in A Minor, Op. Posth.
entries.description: Lento. One of the best choices for a first Chopin waltz assignment, perhaps appropriate for a performer who has already played some of the easier Haydn or Schubert dances. The large leaps and occasional ornaments need attention. ABA form. Level 7.

entries.title: Waltz in E-flat Major, Op. Posth.
entries.description: Sostenuto. Subtitled "To Emile Galliard" and discovered in 1941. Much shorter than any other waltz by Chopin and appropriate as a first Chopin waltz. Should not be confused with another, substantially more difficult, posthumous E-flat Major Waltz with many octave reaches in the right hand. Level 7.

entries.title: Polonaise in C-sharp Minor, Op. 26, No. 1
entries.description: Allegro. Not as difficult as it sounds. In the form of a nocturne with three sections-the polonaise proper, a trio in a slowed tempo, and finally a repetition of the first part. The passionate opening octaves and full chords need arm weight. The central contrasting meno mosso requires careful attention to voicing and legato, and for many this may be the most difficult part to learn. Requires a performer with a strong temperament who can also work out details. Effective in performance and one of the best of the accessible polonaises. Level 10.

entries.title: Polonaise in E-flat Minor, Op. 26, No. 2
entries.description: Maestoso. A somber work with suppressed rumblings. For a student with a large hand span and the ability to inflect small units. The double notes, chords and awkward key provide the primary problems here. Level 10.

entries.title: Polonaise in A Major, Op. 40, No. 1
entries.description: Allegro con brio. Often referred to as the "Military Polonaise." Deserving of its popularity because of its high-spirited character and strongly characteristic polonaise rhythms. Full chords need appropriate use of the arm to achieve rich sounds. A triumphant work, at times bombastic. Level 10.

entries.title: Polonaise in C Minor, Op. 40, No. 2
entries.description: Allegro maestro. Left-hand octave melody in the opening needs careful voicing and matching of melodic tones. Strong fingers are needed for the double notes at the end of the first part. The A-flat lyrical section requires careful voicing above the thick accompaniment. Level 10.

entries.title: Polonaise in G Minor, Op. Posth.
entries.description: Allegro non troppo. A short and accessible work with many different ideas closely juxtaposed. Careful attention to the various rhythmic values is essential. An intermediate teaching piece, although it should be played by a careful student who can avoid its many possible pitfalls. Level 8.

entries.title: Polonaise in B-flat Major, Op. Posth.
entries.description: Allegro maestoso. Right-hand passagework above left-hand broken-chord accompaniment. The trio contains interesting changes of harmony and texture. Composed when Chopin was seven years old. Sounds more difficult than it is. Level 7.

entries.title: Trots Nouvelles Etudes (1839)
entries.description: These three pieces were composed for the instruction book of Moscheles and Fetis. No. 1 in F Minor features a right-hand melody in triplets over a left-hand accompaniment in sweeping ascending and descending broken chords. No. 2 in A-flat also works two-against-three with the melody in the top right-hand chords played against the bottom left-hand eighths. No. 3 in D-flat combines advanced voicing within a hand and is the most difficult of the three. (Many standard editions.) Level 10.

entries.title: Album Leaf in E Major, KK IVb/12
entries.description: One-page piece with a relatively thick texture. Appropriate for the performer able to play the waltzes but desiring a shorter Chopin work. Level 8.

entries.title: Two Bourrees, KK VIIb 1/2
entries.description: Historically conceived though lacking in profundity. Level 7.

entries.title: Cantabile in B-flat Major, KK IVb/6
entries.description: Beautiful, lyrical writing, in a work similar to an abbreviated nocturne. One of the best and most inspired of the short pieces. Level 7.

entries.title: Contradanse in G Major, KK Anh. la/4
entries.description: Good for the performer who plays chordal pieces well. Some potentially awkward voicings and chordal spacings. Two printed pages. Level 7.

entries.title: Three Ecossaises, Op. 72c (Posth.)
entries.description: Light pieces with idiomatic technical passages. Composed when Chopin was 16. Little-known works of limited pedagogical interest. Level 8.

entries.title: Ecossaise No. I
entries.description: The best-known of the Three Pcossaises, featuring many large skips about the keyboard. Dance-like character and brilliant in places. The complex, bouncing rhythms require accuracy in counting to avoid playing triplets. Finger dexterity is required. Level 7.

entries.title: Largo in E-flat Major, KK IVb/5
entries.description: A solemn piece in 4/4 in which one can almost detect a religious character. Primarily chordal, and slightly more difficult than the Chopin Prelude in C Minor, Op. 28, No. 20, but in the same vein. Level 7.

entries.title: Sostenuto in E-flat Major, KK IVb/10
entries.description: Short work that may precede the study of a pianist's first Chopin waltzes. Lyrical melody in single notes. More accessible than many of the preludes. Effective and rewarding. Levels 6-7.

entries.title: Valse Melancolique, KK Anh. la/7
entries.description: Unusual work with an especially beautiful B section. Manageable for a late-intermediate or early-advanced performer. A slow work in a dancelike idiom. The key of F-sharp Minor and Major may seem intimidating. Level 8.

entries.title: Fantasie-Impromptu, Op. 66
entries.description: Not published in Chopin's lifetime yet now an audience favorite. Composed in ABA-Coda form with the well-known three against four passages in the A sections creating a whirlwind effect. The music fits the hand exceptionally well. The beautiful melody of the B section has been transcribed frequently for other idioms and taken out of context. Requires strong fingers and some facility. (Many standard editions.) Level 10.

entries.title: Chopin: An Introduction to His Piano Works, ed. Palmer
entries.description: Provides an excellent crosssection of the most accessible piano works of Chopin and includes some little-known works. Extensive prefatory material on Chopin's style, ornamentation, and pedaling as well as on the concept of rubato. (Alfred.) Levels 7-10.

entries.title: Chopin: The First Book for Pianists, ed. Palmer
entries.description: Contains the easiest selections from Chopin: An Introduction to His Piano Works, ed. Palmer. Three easiest preludes, three mazurkas, Albumleaf, Cantabile in B-flat Major, Largo in E-flat Major and other similar works. (Alfred.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Chopin: Selected Favorites, ed. Palmer
entries.description: Includes mazurkas, preludes, waltzes, polonaises, nocturnes, etudes and the Fantasie-Impromptu. (Alfred.) Levels 7-10.

entries.title: Dances of Chopin, ed. Hinson
entries.description: Miscellaneous works found here include the Waltz in C-sharp Minor, Op. 64, No. 2, Polonaise in A Major, Op. 40, No. 1, Mazurka in F Major, Op. 68, No. 3, Three Pcossaises, Op. 72, No. 3 and Tarantella in A-flat Major, Op. 43. (Alfred.) Levels 8-10.

entries.title: At the Piano With Chopin, ed. Hinson
entries.description: The first 22 pages of this volume consist of extensive background material on Chopin and on the pieces included here. (Alfred.) Levels 7-10.

entries.title: Chopin: An Introductory Album, ed. Jones
entries.description: A fine student introductory edition. Some of the most important and accessible solo works of Chopin are found here. Included are such works as Waltz in A Minor, Op. 34, No. 2, Nocturne in G Minor, Op. 15, No. 3, Prelude in D-flat, Op. 28, No. 15, Albumleaf, Op. Posth. and Largo, Op. Posth. Well-edited, the variety and rather uniform level of difficulty of the pieces included is desirable. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music.) Levels 7-10.

entries.title: Klavierunterricht mit Fryderyk Chopin, Sechs Leichte Mazurkas, ed. Walter
entries.description: Six of the easiest mazurkas. Included are those in F Major, Op. 68, No. 3; B Major, Op. 7, No. 1; G Minor, Op. 24, No. 1; A Major, Op. 24, No. 3; C Major, Op. 33, No. 3; and F Minor, Op. 7, No. 3. (Breitkopf.) Level 9.

entries.title: Klavierunterricht mit Fryderyk Chopin, Leichte Klavierwerke, ed. Walter
entries.description: In addition to the three commonly taught Preludes from Op. 28, in E Minor, B Minor, and A Minor, this volume contains two very easy waltzes, a nocturne, and the "Rain-drop" prelude. (Breitkopf.) Levels 8-10.

entries.title: Chopin: Master Series for the Young, ed. Hughes
entries.description: Unfortunately this volume extracts portions of pieces to serve as the easiest selections in this volume. That practice and the extremely wide range in level of difficulty of the pieces included makes this volume impractical. (G. Schirmer.) Levels 6-10.

entries.title: Chopin: Easiest Piano Pieces, ed. Scholtz
entries.description: The selections here are printed in large type, and are obviously intended for very young talented students. Included are the Preludes in B Minor, A Major, and C Minor, Mazurkas in A Minor, Op. 7, No. 2 and E Minor, Op. 17, No. 2, and Waltz in A Minor, Op. 34, No. 2. (Peters.) Levels 7-10.

entries.title: Masters for the Young: Chopin
entries.description: Small print, some questionable alterations from originals, and a lack of sources for the pieces appearing in original form. Included are two waltzes, two mazurkas, and the Polonaise in A Major, Op. 40, No. 1, here transposed to C Major. (Kalmus.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Introduction to the Cortot Editions of Chopin Works
entries.description: This fine collection contains some of the best and most accessible works of Chopin from the preludes, mazurkas, polonaises, nocturnes, waltzes and ecossaises. The works included have extensive prefatory material by Alfred Cortot, including practice suggestions. Valuable for the combination of selections included and for Cortot's notes. (Salabert.) Levels 8-10.

entries.title: Chopin: Selected Easy Pieces, ed. Paderewski
entries.description: An excellent collection containing some of the most accessible works from the preludes, mazurkas, polonaises and waltzes. A valuable anthology of Chopin's works. (Marks.) Levels 8-10.

entries.title: Chopin: Piano Music Inspired by Women in His Life, ed. Hinson
entries.description: An interesting variety of music is provided. Especially notable is the historical background provided about Louise Chopin, Emily Elsner, George Sand, Celina Symanowska, Maria Wodzfnska, Baroness Charlotte de Rothschild and others. (Alfred.) Levels 7-10.

entries.title: No. 25 (Allegro brillante)
entries.description: Requires clean and facile passagework. Filled with comfortable patterns. The phrasing requires attention to the long line. Level 7.

entries.title: 15 Studies in Style and Expression, Op. 25
entries.description: Substantially more difficult than the Op. 24 pieces. All begin with a short prelude and are then followed by a more extended characteretude. The latter contain valuable practice in playing technical idioms of the standard Romantic repertoire. The selections are given titles such as The Robin Redbreast, The Swallow and Triumphal March. (Many standard editions.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: 24 Brilliant Preludes, Op. 37
entries.description: These are actually etudes in all the Major and Minor keys. They are easy to read and provide the pianist with practice in perfecting typical passagework such as scales and broken chords. The Preludes are short, often only 12 or 16 measures long. Valuable for study by both serious students and amateur pianists. (Many standard editions.) Level 8.

Many editions with varying numbers of etudes are available. In the last edition appearing during Cramer's life, 16 Nouvelles Etudes were added, making 100. Among the editions available are Cramer-Bulow 60 Selected Studies (Peters), Cramer 84 Studies (Kalmus, Schott and others) and Cramer-Billow 50 Selected Studies. (Many standard editions.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Marche in E-flat
entries.description: A Russian counterpart to a Sousa march. Two pages. Full chords, square writing. Might be motivating for a student whose interest in piano study is waning. Level 8.

entries.title: Francaise
entries.description: Prevalent oom-pah-pah chords in the left hand dominate the march-like flavor of this highly patterned piece. Rich, full sounds. Level 8.

entries.title: Valse Melancolique
entries.description: Rich in national spirit and rhythmic verve. This writing is more entertaining than profound. Level 8.

entries.title: Valse in E-flat
entries.description: Sentimental, appealing writing. This could provide a good introduction to heart-on-sleeve Romantic style for those ready to play Schubert waltzes. Level 7.

entries.title: Valse Brillante
entries.description: Notable mostly for its general flair. Oom-pah-pah left hand and right hand repeated notes and chord passages. Might be a student's introduction to the Romantic-style brilliant playing. Level 7.

entries.title: Three Preludes
entries.description: Thin textures with flowing arpeggios or repeated figurations. Harp-like, within an impressionistic framework. (Oxford.) Level 8.

entries.title: Five Piano Pieces
entries.description: Short selections in a moderately contemporary idiom. (Boosey and Hawkes.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Allegro in F
entries.description: A somewhat pretentious 10-page sonata-allegro movement containing many typical figurations and flourishes. Might be enjoyed by the adult piano enthusiast who knows the standard literature well and wants to play something unusual. (Boccaccini and Spada, Peters.) Level 10.

entries.title: Adagio and Allegro in G Major
entries.description: Dramatic writing with many typical figurations. Fits the hand well. (Boccaccini and Spada.) Level 10.

entries.title: Allegro in C Major
entries.description: Barcarolle character and sentimental mood. Requires legato octaves for the melody. (Boccaccini and Spada.) Level 10.

entries.title: La Ricordanza
entries.description: Sentimental, lyrical adagio movement. Easier than any of the Chopin nocturnes. (Boccaccini and Spada.) Level 8.

entries.title: Larghetto in A Minor (Una Furtlva Lacrima)
entries.description: A two-page nocturne on the famous aria Una Furtiva Lacrima. Melody in octaves, thirds and single notes over an arpeggiated left hand. (Boccaccini and Spada.) Level 9.

entries.title: Capriccio in Sinfonia
entries.description: Dramatic, operatic writing. Appropriate for the opera buff or as sightreading for accomplished pianists. (Boccaccini and Spada.) Level 10.

entries.title: Sinfonia in C Major
entries.description: Displays many cliches of orchestral reductions, although composed for piano. Not for serious study. (Boccaccini and Spada.) Level 10.

entries.title: Sinfonia in D Major
entries.description: According to Pietro Spada, "It seems to be the rapid skeleton of an overture for a comic opera and is concluded by the faster ritual 'Coda' with repeated cadenzas and conclusive formulas then in use in the theater." (Boccaccini and Spada.) Level 10.

entries.title: 24 Studies in all Major and Minor Keys, Op. 100
entries.description: Romantic both in texture and in harmonic vocabulary. Many are too long or too repetitive to be practical. Figurations used are typical ones found in some of the best romantic period writing. See especially the Studies in B Minor and C-sharp Minor. Book 1 contains the studies in the keys of C, A Minor, G Major and on around the circle of fifths through G-sharp Minor. The remainder are in Book 2. (G. Schirmer and others.) Levels 7-10.

entries.title: No. 6 in B Minor
entries.description: The hands continually alternate in eighths throughout. Melodic line is played by both hands. Effective and impassioned. Worth investigating. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 10 in C-sharp Minor
entries.description: This work clearly reflects the volatile spirit of Romanticism, here reflected in the sweeping right-hand broken chords and arpeggios. The performer should strive for clear voicing of the left-hand melody above the tempestuous broken chords. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Chaconne, Op. 62
entries.description: The typical pianistic figurations found here will help develop facility. The A sections feature a legato single-note right hand over a staccato accompaniment. Somewhat repetitive musically. (G. Schirmer.) Level 6.

entries.title: Valse in E-flat, Op. 83
entries.description: An exceedingly popular and effective selection. The flourishes and figurations fit the hand to maximize the virtuosic effect with surprisingly little effort. A piece to motivate the right student. (Boston, G. Schirmer.) Level 8.

entries.title: Valse in A-flat, Op. 86
entries.description: Requires agile fingerwork, but rewarding for the student with a facile technique. The performer needs a natural feeling for rubato. The fast scale passages sound more difficult than they are. (G. Schirmer.) Level 8.

entries.title: The School of Mechanism, Op. 120
entries.description: These 15 short studies were written to precede Czerny's School of Velocity. They embrace traditional pianistic figures-scales, arpeggios and sequential patterns-that occur in standard literature. These studies are generally two pages long and are some of the better studies available for the intermediate student who plays at the level of Kuhlau sonatinas. (Many standard editions.) Level 7.

entries.title: Elementary Studies, Op. 176
entries.description: Twenty-five excellent and little-known studies for the elementary pianist. These pieces could take the place of some of the less interesting Czerny etudes. They deal with the development of a cantabile tone, legato playing and a kinesthetic feeling for chord playing, as well as with the development of velocity. (Many standard editions.) Level 4.

entries.title: No. 24 Etude
entries.description: Excellent study in parallel thirds and sixths, especially helpful in developing a flexible wrist. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 1
entries.description: An allegro feroce opening and closing surround a central allegretto grazioso which displays nationalistic overtones. Effective writing with strong contrast. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 2
entries.description: Lyrical and highly Romantic writing requiring voicing of inner parts. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 4
entries.description: Marked vivace, this selection is good for the spirited pianist. The infectious rhythms and nationalistic overtones of the middle section combine to make this an appealing piece for both listener and audience. The dramatic octaves in this selection can be motivating. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 5
entries.description: This selection could be a useful one for developing a kinesthetic sense for playing chords. It is a spirited presto with some chord changes. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 7
entries.description: A good precursor to full-sounding Rachmaninoff works. The patterns in this vigorous dance-like piece lie well under the hand, and the central section offers energetic octave passagework. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 8
entries.description: Staccato passages and characteristic five-note flourishes lend a sophisticated and playful character to this charming three-page work. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 12
entries.description: Marked "allegro feroce," this five-page work is another for a student who has flair. Parts of it sound Lisztian and yet the figurations fit the hand well. Big and full romantic sounds. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 3
entries.description: Marked "poco allegro," this selection is reminiscent of the Schumann Novelettes. Lively rhythms and unabashedly Romantic sounds. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 4
entries.description: A charming Chopinesque melody is featured above a waltz accompaniment. This lively selection fits the hand well so that the performer receives maximum return for his or her effort. Winsome writing. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 5
entries.description: Reminiscent of the Chopin Waltz in C-sharp Minor. Figurations are kinesthetic and the writing is highly patterned. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 7
entries.description: Light and flowing. Romantic. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 8
entries.description: Allegro vivace. This movement requires some passion and agility but the music is not difficult to comprehend. Especially good for the student who likes "big" pieces. Level 10.

entries.title: Albumleaf No. 2 (from Four Albumleaves)
entries.description: Miniature Romantic tone-poem. Requires adept voicing of accompaniment and inner voices. (Peters.) Level 8.

entries.title: Albumleaf No. 3 (from Four Albumleaves)
entries.description: A good choice for a pianist not yet ready to play the Brahms Rhapsody in G Minor, Op. 79, No. 2 and needing a piece of the same spirit. Rich sonorities, effective writing. (Peters.) Level 8.

entries.title: Humoreske, Op. 101, No. 7
entries.description: The composer's best-known piano piece. Filled with grace-notes and Bohemian flavor. Needs a dance-like feeling and a rhythmic lilt. (Many standard editions.) Level 10.

entries.title: Dvorak Selected Works
entries.description: Contains some of Dvoiak's best pieces for the early-advanced performer, including selections from the waltzes, silhouettes, mazurkas and humoreskes. Would be a valuable source from which to gain an overview of Dvoiak's piano music. (Schott.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Easy Original Pieces for Piano, ed. Roggenkamp
entries.description: Contains some of the shorter and easier pieces. Valuable collection. (Universal.) Levels 6-10.

entries.title: Spinning Song, Op. 14, No. 4
entries.description: One of the most loved of all early-intermediate teaching pieces. The ascending scalewise finger patterns and the syncopations lend strong character to this effective solo. (Many standard editions.) Level 4.

entries.title: Romances Sans Paroles (Romances Without Words), Op. 17
entries.description: Three lyrical works with patterned accompaniment. These pieces comprise some of the best of the scant amount of literature available at the upper-intermediate level for students who want to study French music of this era. See especially No. I (Andante quasi allegretto) with the melody in single notes, sustained note accompaniment, and off-beat chords and No. 3 (Andante moderato), a work in two voices and the most accessible of the set. No. 2 (Allegro molto) features a beautiful melody with broken left-hand chords, a good piece to teach longer phrases and tone quality. Should be better known. (International.) Level 8.

entries.title: No. 1 Capriccio in E-flat Major
entries.description: Little-known work that is accessible to the early-advanced performer. Features flowing, broken arpeggios divided between the hands. A miniature impromptu. One of the best of Faure's easier works. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 2 Fantasie in A-flat Major
entries.description: Displays a sensitive, dreamy and calm nature. Contains many broken chords and flowing arpeggiations. The many accidentals make the score difficult to read. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 3 Fugue in A Minor
entries.description: An academic study with lush harmonies. A good choice for a student who prefers literature from the Romantic period and needs to develop basic skills in contrapuntal playing. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 4 Adagietto in E Minor
entries.description: Contains a march-like rhythm that gives it a noble feeling. Requires a performer with the ability to sustain a melodic line. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 5 Improvisation in C-sharp Minor
entries.description: A highly expressive two-page work with lyrical melodies, the Improvisation is one of the best of the shorter pieces of Faure. The score is difficult to read initially, but the writing is of high quality. Should be heard more frequently. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 6 Allegrese
entries.description: Marked allegro giocoso, this effective work is comprised primarily of broken chords arpeggiated between the hands and a lyrical melody. Typical of the florid arpeggiated style which many associate with the works of Faure. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 7 Fugue in E Minor
entries.description: More difficult than No. 3 in this set and somewhat less profound. Three-voice writing, but homophonic as well as contrapuntal. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 8 Nocturne in D-flat Major
entries.description: This work in one continuous section was initially called a "Prelude" by Faure and could easily have become another of his Barcarolles. Flowing sixteenths throughout with substantial fingerwork required. This adagio non troppo work in D-flat Major needs skillful control of the melodic line. Level 10.

entries.title: Impromptu No. 2 in F Minor, Op. 31
entries.description: Probably the most accessible and effective of the Impromptus. Rippling eighth-note triplets occur throughout this flowing work. For the performer with strong fingers and a strong structural sense. A fine alternative to a Chopin nocturne. Some brilliant writing in a charming work. (Many standard editions.) Level 10.

entries.title: Nocturne No. 10, Op. 99
entries.description: Large central climax. Lyrical writing that calls for playing a legato melody over accompaniment in the same hand. The performer needs a good sense of voicing and pacing. (Heugel.) Level 10.

entries.title: Nocturne No. 11, Op. 104, No. 1
entries.description: Plaintive, lyrical writing in a sophisticated harmonic framework. For the musician who can hear and project harmonic changes with finesse. Four pages, with periodic thick passagework. This nocturne was composed upon the sudden death of the wife of Pierre Lalo, a music critic of Le Temps and son of the famous composer. A poignant but restrained elegy. (Durand.) Level 10.

entries.title: Barcarolle No. 4, Op. 44
entries.description: Lush harmonies and rich arpeggiated accompanimental figures in this effective sicilienne. (Hamelle.) Level 10.

entries.title: Barcarolle No. 10, Op. 104, No. 2
entries.description: Yearning mood with a Dorian-mode theme that is treated to several variations. This piece could strengthen a student's ability to project melody over a thicker texture and to spin forward a long melodic line. Nostalgic and lyrical. Five pages. (Durand.) Level 9.

entries.title: Album of Piano Pieces
entries.description: Contains some of the most widely played barcarolles and nocturnes, along with one of the Romances Without Words, the Third Impromptu and Improvisation, Op. 84, No. S. (G. Schirmer.) Level 10.

entries.title: Selected Piano Pieces
entries.description: This volume contains perhaps the largest number of miscellaneous pieces of any of the collections. It also contains many frequently played works. (Cramer.) Level 10.

These short Romantic pieces are slow and lyrical, and often filled with rich textures, calling for careful voicing. Especially appropriate for the expressive, lyrically inclined student. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music.) Level 9.

entries.title: Nocturne No. 1 in E-flat
entries.description: Sustained melody above triplet broken-chord figuration. Fluid writing in one of the better of the accessible nocturnes. Level 9.

entries.title: Nocturne No. 2 in C Minor
entries.description: Florid right-hand passages with skips in the bass render this nocturne more difficult than No. 1. Level 10.

entries.title: Nocturne No. 3 in A-flat
entries.description: This work contains busy inner voices and thick textures. More difficult than some Chopin nocturnes. Level 10.

entries.title: Nocturne No. 5 in B-flat
entries.description: The most popular of the Field nocturnes, perhaps due to its brevity. Contains an effective central section and an A section with a beautiful cantilena that soars above the arpeggiated accompaniment. Level 9.

entries.title: Nocturne No. 7 in A Major
entries.description: An appropriate piece to help improve rapid leggiero playing in the right hand. Lyrical writing interspersed with florid passagework. Level 10.

entries.title: Nocturne No. 9 in E Minor
entries.description: Soft and melancholy writing in this short adagio. Contains a triplet accompaniment that supports the unadorned melody. One of the best from the opus. Level 8.

entries.title: Selected Piano Works, ed. Branson
entries.description: This volume contains some minor works of Field such as Marche Triomphale, Andante Inedit and Nocturne "The Troubadour" not often found in other sources. Also found here are a sonata movement and another nocturne. (Carl Fischer.) Level 9.

entries.title: Field: Nocturnes and Other Short Pieces, ed. Barsham
entries.description: Interesting volume containing seven nocturnes and various other works that are hard to find such as Marche Triomphale, Sehnsuchts-Waltzer, Waltz in A Major, Polonaise and Rondo: 12 O'clock. The valuable introduction to the book discusses interpretation, fingering, pedaling, phrasing and dynamic marks, text, ornamentation and tempo in Field's music. This introduction along with the textural commentary on each piece make this volume especially valuable. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: Complete Piano Music, ed. List
entries.description: A delightful collection of Americana. The melodies are folk tunes, and the settings often feature oom-pah-pah left-hand accompaniments. Cheerful, sometimes rousing. See especially Old Folks at Home (Theme and Variations), The Old Folks Quadrilles and Santa Anna's Retreat From Buena Vista. (G. Schirmer.) Levels 6-8.

entries.title: Three Earliest Compositions (1835)
entries.description: Three small contrapuntal pieces taken from Franck's book of exercises in counterpoint and fugue. Of historical significance. (Dover.) Levels 5-6.

entries.title: Twenty-Five Short Pieces from L'Organiste
entries.description: Franck wrote 59 miniatures for the harmonium, an organ-like instrument without pedals. Many of them commonly appear in anthologies of piano teaching music since they also sound pleasing on the piano. Twenty-five of the best pieces were included in this practical teaching edition. The writing is highly idiomatic for the keyboard. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Forty-Six Short Pieces, ed. Agay
entries.description: In this volume are 46 of the pieces from L'Organiste, all of which were composed by Franck for the harmonium. Originally the pieces were intended to be used as preludes, postludes, voluntaries and offertories. These selections could be used by the upper-intermediate pianist to cultivate legato playing. (Presser.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Danse Lente
entries.description: A slow, sentimental waltz, with three repetitions of an eight-measure theme. (Dover.) Level 7.

entries.title: The Doll's Lament (Les Plaintes dune Poupee)
entries.description: A simple work with a legato melody above an eighth-note accompaniment. Calls for refined voicing and phrasing. (Dover.) Level 4.

entries.title: Polka
entries.description: Composed by Franck to curb his wife's interest in dancing, this two-page solo (also arranged for piano duet) is a playful, shallow piece. Level 7.

entries.title: Children's Pieces from Op. 32 and 47
entries.description: Selections from two of his albums for children. The pieces are charming in their simplicity and unashamed Romanticism. They parallel the intermediate pieces from the Schumann Album for the Young, Op. 68. Generally one page long, suitable for teaching Romantic phrasing and voicing. Titles of the works include Morning Song, Soldier's March, Chatterbox, A Great Mystery and The Little Trumpeter. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music.) Levels 5-7.

entries.title: No. 1 Elegie
entries.description: Features a soaring melody with a Chopin-like bass. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 2 Scherzo
entries.description: Grazioso writing with some left-hand stretches and many accidentals. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 5 Barkarole
entries.description: Strong Romantic flavor with rolling broken chords divided between the hands. Lyrical and sweeping writing with some octave passages. Excellent preparation for the playing of Chopin. Level 8.

entries.title: Children's Christmas Eve, Op. 36
entries.description: Enticing set of character pieces for the intermediate performer. Includes two introductory pieces, Christmas Chimes and Christmas Psalm (without number), three pieces in a suite called The Christmas Tree, and a concluding movement Good Night. The three pieces in The Christmas Tree, No. 1 Entrance March, No. 2 The Boys' Round Dance, and No. 3 The Little Girls' Dance are the best. Little-known. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music, Kalmus and other standard editions.) Level 7.

entries.title: No. 2 from The Christmas Tree, The Boys' Round Dance
entries.description: Lively piece that fits the hand well. Several ideas are present, linked by a single rhythmic element. Employs many chords and is effective in performance. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 3 from the Christmas Tree, The Little Girls' Dance
entries.description: Highly reminiscent of Chopin and excellent preparation for his waltzes. Rondo form in E Major with alternate sections in B and A Major. Enticing passagework conveys the feeling of dancing and whirling. Graceful, lyrical dance. In some respects, reminiscent of an easier version of the Chopin "Minute Waltz." Level 7.

entries.title: Good Night
entries.description: Needs facility in moving about the keyboard and in playing chords. Not as effective as the second and third selections in this opus. The rhythmic complexity needs careful attention. Level 6.

entries.title: Aquarellen and Other Pieces, ed. Salter
entries.description: Contains works taken from albums that Gade published between about 1850 and 1880 including selections from Aquarellen, Op. 19 and Children's Christmas Eve, Op. 36. This volume provides an interesting and varied overview of his piano compositions. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music.) Levels 7-9.

entries.title: Le Secret
entries.description: At one time, this pleasant, unassuming selection appeared in all of the popular piano anthologies. Oom-pah-pah accompaniment and simple eighth-note melody. (G. Schirmer.) Level 8.

entries.title: Studies for Children (Etudes Enfantines), Op. 149, Book 1
entries.description: Godard was influenced by Schumann; in fact he orchestrated Schumann's Scenes from Childhood, Op. 15 in 1876. Perhaps better described as character pieces, these 10 selections for upper-intermediate students follow predictable harmonic patterns. The ideas are simple, but charming. See especially Marche des Garconnets, Marche des Fillettes, Taquinerie and Peur de I'enfer. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music, Simrock.) Level 7.

entries.title: Funeral March of a Marionette
entries.description: This well-known work based on a single tuneful short motive was first published by Gounod as a piano solo and later orchestrated. ABA form with full solid chords in the middle section. (G. Schirmer.) Level 7.

entries.title: Marche Pontificale
entries.description: Pompous and chordal, but rather ordinary writing. It could be used by a student who needs to work on the kinesthetic feeling of diatonic chords, or who needs to stress rhythmic surety. (G. Schirmer.) Level 6.

entries.title: Funeral March of a Marionette and Other Pieces, ed. Jones
entries.description: Although Gounod wrote little for piano solo, 10 interesting selections are included in this volume. The music is strongly Romantic and sometimes sentimental. See especially Barcarolle, The Evening, May Morning and Children's Dance. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music.) Level 7.

entries.title: No. 1 Dedication
entries.description: One-page work dedicated to Granados' son. Lyrical and in three voices with melody and triplet accompaniment in the right hand. The texture is similar to Schumann's About Strange Lands and People, Op. 68, No. 1, but this work is slightly more difficult. It can provide a fine study in finger substitution. Level 5.

entries.title: No. 3 May Song
entries.description: Enchanting melody above an extended broken-chord accompaniment in a work that may be appropriate for those who are not yet ready to play Chopin nocturnes. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 5 Coming from the Fountain
entries.description: Reflective and tranquil work with a lyrical melody and an arpeggiated left hand in a Chopin-like style. The double notes in the right-hand melody make this piece seem more difficult than it initially appears. Level 8.

entries.title: Valses Poeticos
entries.description: One of Granados' best-known teaching works, this composition consists of a series of seven waltzes with an introduction and a concluding waltz. Preferably, the entire set should be played together. (Dover, Kalmus International, Union Musical and other standard editions.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: 12 Spanish Dances, Op. 5
entries.description: Effective set of pieces for the student of Spanish piano music. The titles are: No. 1 Minuetto; No. 2 Oriental; No. 3 Zarabanda; No. 4 Villanesca; No. 5 Andaluza or Playera; No. 6 Iota or Rondalla aragonesa; No. 7 Valenciana; No. 8 Asturiana; No. 9 Mazurka; No. 10 Danza triste; No. 11 Zambra; and No. 12 Arabesca. See especially Nos. 1, 2, 5 and 6. (Dover, International and other standard editions.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: No. I Minuetto
entries.description: Broad chords and a catchy rhythmic motive may appeal to those who like works of Rachmaninoff. For the pianist able to play resonant and full chords. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 5 Andaluza or Playera
entries.description: Popular work, featuring a sparkling theme and a left-hand accompaniment depicting the Spanish dance idiom and castanets. Level 10.

entries.title: Six Expressive Studies
entries.description: Primarily lyrical pieces. Pleasant, almost naive music. See especially La Ultima Pavana and Pastoral. (Masters Music, Union Musical.) Level 8.

entries.title: Bocetos
entries.description: Set of four solos for upper-intermediate pianists with characteristic Spanish rhythms. See especially Vals muy lento, notable for its strong character, and La Campana de la Tarde with its native melody above a simple accompaniment. (Union Musical.) Level 8.

entries.title: Escenas Poeticas, Book 1
entries.description: Three movements that reflect Granados' interest in the music of Grieg, Schumann and Liszt. Inviting music, filled with Granados' unique modulations and melodic style. Titles include Berceuse, Eva and Walter and Dance of the Rose. See especially the second and third of these pieces. (Dover.) Level 9.

entries.title: Escenas Romanticas
entries.description: Little-known set of six pieces that sounds less Spanish than most of Granados' works. Resembles Chopin in emotional content, and occasionally in figuration; all but two of these pieces are advanced. See especially No. 4 Allegretto. (International and other editions.) Levels 8-10.

entries.title: No. 4 Allegretto
entries.description: Only 19 measures long, the shortest in the set, featuring dance-like figurations in the melody and numerous arpeggiations. This work would pair well with No. 6 Epilogue. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 6 Epilogue
entries.description: Nocturne-like work that features a beautiful lyrical melody in single notes and a left-hand accompaniment of arpeggiated chords. For a student who is not yet ready to play a nocturne. Level 8.

entries.title: Sketches
entries.description: Four enjoyable, brief upper-intermediate pieces. See especially No. 2 El Hada y el Nino. (Masters Music.) Level 8.

entries.title: Carezza, Op. 38
entries.description: Especially appropriate for the performer who enjoys full sonorities. Pianistic writing throughout. (Masters Music.) Level 9.

entries.title: My First Granados
entries.description: An interesting and useful mixture of selections from five of Granados' major works. Included are Evening Bell, Marguerite's Song, The Hunger's Awakening, Oriental and more. (Ricordi.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: Arietta, Op. 12, No. I
entries.description: Features three layers of sound, with the middle voice in sixteenths split between the hands. Appropriate for control of the right-hand thumb. Expressive and beautiful writing. Good preparation for the Mendelssohn Songs Without Words. Level 6.

entries.title: Waltz, Op. 12, No. 2
entries.description: Excellent study in articulation and in inflection of melody. Righthand melody calls for independence of fingers and the work needs a light, dance-like character throughout. Suitable preparation for Chopin waltzes. (Alfred.) Level 6.

entries.title: Watchman's Song, Op. 12, No. 3
entries.description: Composed by Grieg after he attended a performance of Shakespeare's Macbeth. The ghosts in the night and the watchman's shout are depicted in the central mysterious intennezzo ("Spirits of the Night"), which should prompt the student's imagination. The A sections are primarily chordal. Deserving of its popularity. Level 5.

entries.title: Dances of the Elves, Op. 12, No. 4 (Elfin Dance)
entries.description: A finger piece with running eighth-note figures and staccato chords. Contains two basic rhythmic motives. This mysterious work could be especially good to bring out an inhibited student. Needs right-hand dexterity to execute the runs. Hand positions are relatively stable. Level 6.

entries.title: Norwegian Melody, Op. 12, No. 6
entries.description: Based on a drone bass and an accented melody, with elements of a Nordic folk dance. Requires rhythmic precision. Top needs to be voiced over two- or three-note chords split between the hands. More difficult than the Arietta. Hand skips that are sometimes awkward. Level 7.

entries.title: Album-leaf, Op. 12, No. 7
entries.description: Shows Grieg's propensity for nationalistic elements in his writing. Melody with oom-pah-pah accompaniment. Requires rapid movement about the keyboard. Playful, sectional work. Level 6.

entries.title: Birdling, Op. 43, No. 4
entries.description: Light, graceful character piece containing short trills notated in thirty-second notes. Requires rhythmic accuracy and finger dexterity. Much use of the upper register of the keyboard. Level 7.

entries.title: March of the Dwarfs, Op. 54, No. 3
entries.description: A favorite that often proves more difficult than expected. Constant repetition of broken fifths in the bass. Inherent energy and strongly contrasting themes. Requires strong octaves. Levels 8-9.

entries.title: Notturno, Op. 54, No. 4
entries.description: An elegant, highly expressive work for the advancing performer who is not yet ready to play Chopin nocturnes. A central pia mosso passes through several different keys to build to a large climax. Needs sensitivity to subtle dynamic changes. Level 8.

entries.title: Bell Ringing, Op. 54, No. 6
entries.description: Imaginative and unique. Depicts clanging bells, with ample use of damper pedal. Dynamics range from ppp to ff. Performer needs the ability to control the gradual intensity of sound. Level 6.

entries.title: Wedding Day at Troldhaugen, Op. 65, No. 6
entries.description: Well-known work that begins with a memorable rhythmic drone, perhaps depicting ringing bells. Filled with fast dramatic broken octaves and large sweeping arpeggiated passages. Intense work. Level 10.

entries.title: Sailor's Song, Op. 68, No. I
entries.description: Dramatic writing with big dynamic contrasts. Needs rich sound without harshness. A good study in playing chords, octaves and in executing syncopated pedaling. Short and march-like. Level 5.

entries.title: Puck, Op. 71, No. 3
entries.description: Mischievous, sprightly writing. A repeated four-note motive travels through different keys. The double-note passages and rapid skips help strengthen the fingers. The title refers to the famous sprite in Shakespeare's Midsummer Night's Dream. Level 6.

entries.title: Poetic Tone Pictures, Op. 3
entries.description: Six character pieces published early in Grieg's life. See especially Nos. 1, 2 and S. (Many standard editions.) Level 8.

entries.title: No. I Tone Picture
entries.description: Beautifully lyrical piece with many voices working independently. Due to the contrapuntal writing, this work is difficult to play well. Needs attention to appropriate fingering and to the forward flow of the melodic line. Level 9.

entries.title: Grieg Easier Favorites
entries.description: Amply fingered volume that contains many favorites of Grieg's works including Notturno, March of the Dwarfs and Puck. (Heinrichshofen.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: Grieg: An Introduction to His Piano Works, ed. Halford
entries.description: Contains a fine foreword on Grieg's life and musical style. Some of the easier pieces. (Alfred.) Levels 5-8.

entries.title: Grieg: The First Book for Young Pianists, ed. Halford
entries.description: Eight of the Lyric Pieces are found here. (Alfred.) Levels 5-6.

entries.title: Grieg: Lyric Pieces and Poetic Tone-Pictures, Op. 12 and Op. 3, ed. Morrison
entries.description: A practical and scholarly edition containing the complete Op. 12 and Op. S. The first volume of Lyric Pieces, Op. 12 was published in 1867 when the composer was 24. It contains some of the easiest pieces that Grieg composed. The Poetic Tone-Pictures were published two years earlier and are slightly more difficult. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: Grieg: Selected Works for the Piano, ed. Levine
entries.description: Contains 38 of the Lyric Pieces. (Alfred.) Levels 5-10.

entries.title: Six Short Pieces, Op. 48
entries.description: These pieces were possibly composed as teaching pieces for his daughter, and are works that the young, precocious student might play well. Surprising and interesting writing. See especially Little March and Tarantella. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music.) Level 7.

entries.title: Six Sonatinas, Op. 54
entries.description: Lesser-known Romantic sonatinas, mostly in three movements. (Alfred and others.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: No. I Sonatina
entries.description: See especially the slow movement, which is chordal and expressive. The final Allegretto is animated and effective, and has a big climax. Level 6.

entries.title: No. S Sonatina
entries.description: The third movement, Vivace, is one of the best in this set. The dancelike rhythm has strong appeal. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 2 Morning Prayer
entries.description: Three-part chorale emphasizing voicing and phrase shaping. Hymnlike, peaceful. Good prelude to four-voice chorales. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 8 The Fair
entries.description: This light, elementary piece provides a good workout for the right hand through the rapid circular sixteenth-note patterns. Especially appropriate to help develop inflection of short melodic ideas. One of the best in this collection. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 12 The Little Wanderer
entries.description: Fragmented melody with sudden stops written in the score, as if one is lost and starting over again. The B section features staccato thirds in both hands. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 13 Grandfather's Birthday
entries.description: A fanfare. Extensive use of dotted-quarter sixteenth-note rhythm. The thick chords make this sound impressive. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 14 Valse Noble
entries.description: A cheerful waltz in miniature, only two eight-bar phrases in length. Effective contrast between the A section grazioso and the B section scherzando. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 18 Sunday
entries.description: Excellent piece for four-part harmony. The lento chorale-like selection helps develop a feeling for diatonic triads and voicing to the top. The A section features some right-hand movement in parallel thirds while the B section in 3/2 is in chorale style. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 19 Hunting Song
entries.description: Helps develop a feeling for dramatic playing especially through the accelerando to the climax and the fermata over the silence. Level 6.

entries.title: No. I (Allegretto)
entries.description: Inspiring and lyrical. Occasional left-hand descending octaves lend a touch of excitement to this two-page piece. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 2 (Andantino con moto)
entries.description: Expressive work with the melody generally in the left hand and offbeat syncopated chords above. Needs attention to voicing. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 3 (Allegretto scherzando)
entries.description: One of the easiest scherzos in this collection. The passagework fits the hand well, and does not last long. Effective writing. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 4 (Allegretto grazioso)
entries.description: Flowing, broken-chord passagework divided between the hands permeates this piece. An excellent study in matching melodic tones and in shaping the texture. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 5 (Allegro)
entries.description: A passionate work that calls for flair and facility. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 6 (Con moto, quasi Allegro)
entries.description: Another impassioned piece. The passagework is highly idiomatic and creates a big and resonant sound. The left hand can assist in achieving the strong crescendos and diminuendos. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 7 (Moderato grazioso)
entries.description: A graceful waltz with thin textures. The ascending and descending arpeggiations in the B section provide drama and sweep. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 10 (Con moto)
entries.description: An effective character piece with a thick texture and periodic syncopations. Tuneful, somewhat dark. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 11 (Allegretto scherzando)
entries.description: Repeated chord figures form the accompaniment for idiomatic double-note right-hand passages. A playful and effective two-page work. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 12 (Allegro capriccioso)
entries.description: A three-page leggiero scherzo featuring some double-note passages. Easier than it sounds. Level 8.

entries.title: The First Lessons, Op. 117
entries.description: Delightful little teaching pieces spanning elementary to intermediate levels. Many are frequently found in anthologies, but some are unfamiliar gems. See especially The Clappermill, Gavotte, The Return, The Chase and Cradle Song. (Kalmus.) Levels 2-6.

entries.title: No. 33 The Mill
entries.description: Effective work in three sections calling for even right-hand passagework. Listen for the repeated figures representing the mill wheel. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 34 Song Without Words
entries.description: A broken-chord study, also effective in performance. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 1 March
entries.description: Rhythmic and pompous work with four-voice chords alternating with three-voice chords. Differing articulations in each hand. Rhythmic precision is needed especially in the dotted eighth and sixteenth figures. Careful articulation is required especially in measure two. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 2 Morning Song
entries.description: Could serve as a student's first study in voicing melody over other voices in a single hand. Limited number of measures to learn in this lyrical, chordal selection. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 3 Bright is the Sky
entries.description: Grazioso writing with a lyrical melody over Alberti bass accompaniment. Predictable phrase lengths. The performer has the opportunity to display a true singing tone. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 4 In the Garden
entries.description: Singing melody in the left hand accompanied by syncopated righthand chords. Appropriate for students who are able to voice between the hands. One of the best in the set. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 8 The Music Box
entries.description: Entire piece is written in treble register, simulating a music box. Charming and engaging writing that fits the hand well. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 10 The Little Norwegian
entries.description: Could be an appropriate repertoire selection to emphasize clarity in passage playing. Spirited and vigorous work. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 11 Longing
entries.description: Expressive phrasing of the long lyrical lines is needed as the right hand features the melody throughout. This dreamy work calls for careful right-hand voicing and an especially soft thumb. Good preparation for more advanced, Schumannesque repertoire. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 13 The Little Wanderer
entries.description: Predictable phrases in a simple tuneful work featuring grazioso writing with an inviting melodic contour. Melody in right hand with simple accompaniment in the left. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 17 Scherzo
entries.description: Playful work featuring chordal playing in each hand. The accent occurs on the third beat throughout until the final left-hand statement, when the accent reverts to the "proper" downbeat. May help a student learn to move freely about the keyboard. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 18 Serenade
entries.description: A fine study in phrasing, inflection and matching of tones. Brokenchord accompaniment. Level 4.

entries.title: No. I [Wild Mignonette]
entries.description: One-page piece with a melody featuring a strong rhythmic motive. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 2 [Harebell]
entries.description: Simple work in ABB form. The texture generally consists of continuous eighth notes in one hand against quarter notes in the other. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 3 [Lady's Smock]
entries.description: Charming waltz. One of the best in this set. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 4 [Ragged Robin]
entries.description: Suitable preparation for easier Chopin and Schubert waltzes and effective in performance. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 8 [Rose Rock]
entries.description: Perpetual-motion piece with continuous sixteenth notes in the right hand. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 12 [Dandelion]
entries.description: Marked allegro con fuoco, this piece is especially suitable for a student with energy and flair, and one who plays broken four-note chords adeptly. Level 6.

entries.title: A First Book
entries.description: Simple character pieces, many of which appear in modern anthologies for elementary-level students. Some of the best teaching music for the early-level student. The music in this book is more uniform in level than in Gurlitt's The First Lessons, Op. 117. The selections are titled. (Belwin.) Levels 4-5.

entries.title: 24 Easy Melodious Studies, Op. 50
entries.description: Tuneful, rather predictable character pieces or etudes. Not lengthy and generally highly patterned. Valuable etude material. (G. Schirmer.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: No. 5 (Allegretto)
entries.description: Broken-chord study that calls for matching tone quality between the hands. Provides an opportunity to listen to harmonic progressions and to interpret accordingly. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 6 (Allegretto)
entries.description: Triads appear in triplets and broken between the hands to create a harp-like piece. The chords should roll from hand to hand seamlessly with the first bass note serving as a harmonic foundation. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 14 (Con moto)
entries.description: Triplet figuration in the right hand with the melody note heard as the first of the triplets against a single-note accompaniment mostly in eighths. Lyrical and yet exciting writing. Should be better known. Level 7.

entries.title: The First Steps of the Young Pianist, Op. 82
entries.description: One hundred pieces in two volumes, progressive in difficulty. These are character etudes, relatively short, and of strong value for the young pianist in developing the playing of passagework. Although the writing is not harmonically adventurous, the pieces contain common patterns and writing that is highly idiomatic to the Romantic period. (G. Schirmer.) Levels 3-8.

entries.title: No. 65 (Allegro non troppo)
entries.description: This passionate piece calls for a strongly defined bass that sings out above the repeated-note accompaniment in the right hand. Effective and exciting. Level 4.

entries.title: The Easiest Studies in Velocity, Op. 83
entries.description: These pieces fall into the category of pedantic studies, with many repetitions of ideas within the etude. (G. Schirmer.) Level 6.

entries.title: 25 Easy Studies Without Octaves, Op. 130
entries.description: Little-known collection that contains many interesting character pieces. Most are one page or shorter in length. The writing is enticing. See especially Morning Song, Hymne, Rustic Dance and Sunshine. (G. Schirmer.) Levels 6-7.

entries.title: 24 Melodious Studies, Op. 131
entries.description: Some character pieces of surprisingly high quality are found here. Most are short and are more difficult than the selections in Album for the Young, Op. 140. Generally on the level of difficulty with the Burgmuller 25 Easy and Progressive Studies, Op. 100. It is surprising that these are not better known. See especially No. 8, Pleasures of the Chase; No. 13, A Steadfast Resolve; and No. 20, Evening Peace. (G. Schirmer.) Levels 6-7.

entries.title: No. 13 A Steadfast Resolve
entries.description: The mood throughout is passionate and driving in this brokenchord piece. Effective sweep and brilliance. Worth investigating. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: No. 20 Evening Peace
entries.description: Andantino work with a triplet accompaniment and a lyrical righthand melody. The left hand presents an interesting countermelody in this highly expressive and effective piece. Level 7.

entries.title: Novellettes, Op. 148
entries.description: Subtitled "12 Little Salon Pieces." All are titled and some are of musical value. The most appealing are No. 1, Morning Greeting; No. 4, Idyl; and No. 6, Impromptu. (G. Schirmer.) Level 8.

entries.title: Etude in D Minor
entries.description: A melody is stated in the low register of the keyboard against a repeated chord right-hand accompaniment that adds tension and excitement to this dramatic character piece. Level 5.

entries.title: Hunting Song in F Major, Op. 210, No. 5
entries.description: Elementary piece with an infectious hunting-horn motive as the main theme. Level 3.

entries.title: Scherzo in D Minor
entries.description: Descending arpeggio patterns using different inversions of chords in successive order. The cross-rhythms are effective. The B section poses problems in voicing and legato. Motivating for most students and especially effective in recital and contest. Level 7.

entries.title: 25 Studies, Op. 45
entries.description: Perhaps the best-known and most frequently played etudes are found here, works that are highly Romantic and sometimes sentimental. See especially No. 1, The Brook (Etude); No. 2, The Avalanche; No. 7, Determination; No. 9, Celestial Voices; No. 10, Evening Prayer (Study); No. 14, Sailor's Song; No. 15, Warrior's Song; No. 22, The Harp; and No. 23, Sailing Along. (Many standard editions.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: No. 1 The Brook
entries.description: Right-hand sixteenth-note figuration throughout with a simple quarter-note accompaniment. A technical workout for fingers four and five of the right hand. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 2 The Avalanche
entries.description: Perhaps the most popular selection from this opus, an energetic piece that is effective in performance. Attention should be paid to producing even eighth notes in the ascending passages, and to playing resonant chords. The phrasing can become monotonous unless it is carefully worked out. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 3 A Challenge
entries.description: Primarily a finger study, with some twisting passages in the right hand accompanied by descending scale passages in the left. For the performer wanting to develop agile finger technique. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 5 Song of May
entries.description: The strong rhythm of the melody makes it memorable. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 7 Determination
entries.description: A bold work consisting of octaves and chords in both hands. Especially appropriate for the performer with large hands, wanting to perform a work that sounds big and yet is not technically involved. Short and effective in performance. Although the playing of four-note chords is required, no stretches of over an octave are present. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 9 Celestial Voices
entries.description: Undoubtedly inspired by the Chopin Etude Op. 25, No. 1 "Harp." The melody is played with the thumb of the right hand and the rapid broken-chord accompanimental notes are played by the other fingers. Accompanimental notes are printed in small print as in the Chopin "Harp" etude. More difficult than it appears. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 14 Sailor's Song
entries.description: Appears to be influenced by the Mendelssohn Songs Without Words, reflected in the tuneful writing with incessant repeated chords in the right hand. This work is immediately appealing but the technical difficulties of the rapid repeated chords in the right hand are significant. This piece would be especially appropriate for the student with large hands who is adept at playing chordal works. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 15 Warrior's Song
entries.description: Majestic chords that need arm and body weight. Features frequent movement between the registers of the piano and the playing of fournote chords in a hand. Effective writing that sounds more difficult than it is. Perhaps appropriate for the student wanting to play the Rachmaninoff Prelude in C-sharp Minor but who is not yet at that level. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 16 Il Penseroso
entries.description: A primarily lyrical work that features a singing melody in the left hand with leggiero arpeggiated chords in the right hand. Somewhat monotonous phrasing. Especially good for the student wanting to play Chopin nocturne-like works but not ready for that level of repertoire. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 22 Song of the Harp
entries.description: Features broken-chord arpeggiation divided between the hands, with the left hand crossing over the right to play many of the melody notes. Requires rapid movement about the keyboard. This is an etude of high musical quality, but one which is also more difficult than it initially appears. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 25 Epilogue
entries.description: A brilliant and effective work in which the central section features passagework from the opening selection of this volume, The Brook. Especially good for a student who plays chords and octaves with ease. Sounds more difficult than it is. Level 8.

entries.title: 30 Studies, Op. 46
entries.description: Although the subtitle by Heller indicates that these pieces are preparatory to the Melodious Studies, Op. 45, they appear to be approximately equal in difficulty (if not more difficult). Often students who play these selections also could perform some of the easiest of the Mendelssohn Songs Without Words. See especially No. 2, The Anvil; No. 6, The Nodding Violet; No. 7, Petite Tarantella; No. 8, Song of Courage; No. 9, The jester; No. 14, Song of the Sea; and No. 22, The Queen Dances. (Many standard editions.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: 25 Studies, Op. 47
entries.description: Generally much less difficult than the studies in Op. 46. See especially No. 1, Scampering; No. 3, Autumn Song; No. 6, The Bagpipe; No. 8, The Rivulet; No. 10, The Unknown Soldier; No. 11, In the Highlands of Scotland; No. 15, Grief; No. 17, Whirling Leaves; No. 19, Lullaby; No. 20, Tragedy; No. 21, Love-Song; No. 23, Quiet Happiness. As noted earlier, titles are not by the composer but have been associated with these pieces by tradition. (Many standard editions.) Levels 6-8.

entries.title: No. I [Scampering]
entries.description: Broken chords divided between the hands present easy coordination and natural phrasing according to the harmonic progression. Flowing and appealing to the young student. One of the most accessible and most musical of the etudes. A good piece to help sharpen the listening skills of a student. This should be better known. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 3 [Autumn Song]
entries.description: This busy piece develops pedaling, listening and voicing over thick chords. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 14 [In Venice]
entries.description: A beautiful barcarolle, similar to some of the Mendelssohn Songs Without Words but significantly less difficult. Calls for playing some double-third passages in the right hand. Requires careful voicing of the top notes in a hand as well as finesse in phrasing. Appropriate for the student not yet ready for Chopin. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 19 [BerceuseJ
entries.description: One of the most accessible of the etudes. The melody is doubled a third below in the left hand to create a Schumannesque lyricism. Level 4.

entries.title: The Art of Phrasing, Op. 16
entries.description: Heller's studies Opp. 45, 46, 47, 90, 125 and 138 were written as preparatory studies to this opus, which consists of two volumes, with 12 pieces in the first volume and 14 in the second. The works are stylistic studies with characteristic titles. See especially No. 1, Canzonette; No. 8, Eclogue; and No. 9, Rondo from Volume I; and No. 1, Toccatina from Volume II. (G. Schirmer.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: 24 Preludes, Op. 81
entries.description: Untitled pieces, nearly all of which are monothematic and quite short. Thick romantic textures and lush sonorities. Not of particular intrigue although the best are Preludes Nos. 2, 3, 4, 6, 7, 12, 16, 17 and 24. (G. Schirmer.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Flower, Fruit and Thorn Pieces, Op. 82 (Nuits blanches)
entries.description: Subtitled, "Restless Nights." These 18 intermediate character pieces contain some of Heller's best writing. The works display the essence of Romanticism, and have slightly stronger character and more substance than many of the etudes. This is a collection that deserves investigation. These works were inspired by the German novelist jean Paul Richter, who also inspired Schumann. Note the flavor of Hungarian rhythm in Nos. 9 and 14. (G. Schirmer.) Levels 8-10.

entries.title: 32 Preludes (for Lilly), Op. 119
entries.description: Heller referred to these preludes in a letter as "mostly simple pleasant pieces written for children, either in a facile or characteristic manner, as indicated clearly by the title." They are somewhat reminiscent of children's fairy tales. See especially Preludes Nos. 2, 5, 6, 9, 10, 16, 19, 23, 29 and 32. (Many standard editions.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: No. 25
entries.description: An enchanting chordal work that provides an opportunity to hear the rich quality of various full harmonies. Does not require substantial finger independence. Level 6.

entries.title: 24 Studies for Rhythm and Expression, Op. 125
entries.description: Short, untitled character pieces, progressive in difficulty and especially suited for sight-reading. Romantic textures. See especially Nos. 1, 5, 7, 8, 10, 12 and 21. (Many standard editions.) Levels 7-9.

entries.title: No. 8 (Allegretto)
entries.description: Titled Tolling Bell in some editions due to the hand crossings to play treble notes, presumably representing bells. Effective writing in a short piece that calls for overlapping legato. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 22 (Lento)
entries.description: Highly expressive, lento work. The melody is usually played one sixteenth-note before the strong beats, providing a syncopated effect throughout. Should be better known. Level 8.

entries.title: Album for the Young, Op. 138
entries.description: Among the 25 pieces found here are several sets including five Songs Without Words (Nos. 1-5) and Gypsies (Nos. 16-20). Strong character and somewhat easier than most of the Etudes, Op. 45, 46 and 47. The wellknown Curious Story is No. 9 in this set. Other gems await discovery. Also see especially No. 5, Barcarolle; No. 6, Etude; No. 14, At Evening; Nos. 9 and 20 from Gypsies; and No. 22, Oberon's Horn. (Many standard editions.) Levels 5-8.

entries.title: Abenddammerung, Op. 138, No. 3
entries.description: Calls for projection of single-line melody over a repeated-note chordal accompaniment with melody appearing in both hands. Needs sensitivity to the harmonic changes. Not complex rhythmically and not difficult to read. Levels 6-7.

entries.title: Curious Story, Op. 138, No. 9
entries.description: Catchy rhythms help make this piece eminently appealing. Popular work for teaching. Humorous and light-hearted. Most of the melodic material is in the right hand. Level 6.

entries.title: Ses camarades le consolent, Op. 138, No. 11
entries.description: Built on repeated chords and scale passages. Interesting writing with an economy of material. Level 6.

entries.title: Tziganyi (Bohemiens), Op. 138, No. 17
entries.description: This is the second of a set of five pieces contained in Op. 138, No. 17. This piece calls for a flexible tempo. The performer needs substantial flair and an assertive musical spirit. This work should be better known, and sounds much more difficult than it is. Level 6.

entries.title: Tarantella in A-flat Major, Op. 85, No. 2
entries.description: Rhythmically exciting, a student favorite, but also relatively difficult. The 6/8 feeling may possibly become monotonous. Highly energetic and intense. Calls for strong finger facility and much energy in the performance. Level 8.

entries.title: The Heller Collection, ed. Hinson
entries.description: Contains 34 etudes drawn from Heller's many sets of etudes. Valuable introductions by Hinson are provided for the interesting works found here. (Universal.) Levels 7-9.

entries.title: Heller: 23 Miscellaneous Pieces, ed. Alexander
entries.description: A notable collection of some of Heller's easier and more accessible works. Most are little-known selections. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music.) Levels 6-8.

entries.title: Heller Rediscovered, ed. Rowley
entries.description: A series of five volumes of Heller's works, with the pieces grouped according to their themes. The groupings for the various volumes are Book 1, Miniatures; Book 2, Preludes; Book 3, Mood Pictures; Book 4, Dances; and Book 5, Nature Sketches. Unfortunately opus numbers are not given in the volumes and titles are of dubious nature. The lack of sources makes this volume less useful than it might be otherwise. (Alfred, Lengnick.) Levels 5-8.

entries.title: Selected Progressive Etudes for the Piano, ed. Olson
entries.description: A selection of untitled etudes from Op. 138, 45, 46, 47, 119 and 81. A well-conceived selection. (Alfred.) Levels 5-9.

entries.title: Ausgewahlte Klavierwerke-Charakterstiicke, ed. Kersten
entries.description: An interesting and unusual collection of character pieces by Heller. Contains some selections that are virtually impossible to find elsewhere such as those from Op. 79, 124, 128, 134 and 150. (Henle.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Twenty Miscellaneous Studies, ed. Alexander
entries.description: Includes an interesting array of pieces from Op. 45, 46, 47, 81, 90 and 125. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music.) Levels 6-8.

entries.title: Il Saltarello Romano
entries.description: Students who enjoyed playing tarantella-like pieces may be attracted to this work. Passagework can be challenging and the writing sometimes seems long-winded. Level 10.

entries.title: Notturno
entries.description: All cadenza-like sections are metered here and no polyrhythms (two against three, and so on) exist. Perhaps a good choice to precede Chopin nocturnes. Rich Romantic harmonies make reading somewhat difficult. Fingering needs careful work in the long, winding melodic lines. Some large chords need a span of over an octave. Level 10.

entries.title: Selected Piano Pieces, ed. Elvers/Theopold
entries.description: Published in 1986, this edition contains works previously unknown. Several hold strong interest for the performer and should be better known. (Henle.) Level 10.

entries.title: At the Piano with Felix and Fanny Mendelssohn, ed. Hinson
entries.description: Interesting combination of pieces by brother and sister is found here. The prefatory material is especially valuable, as is the accessibility of much unknown music by Fanny Mendelssohn. (Alfred.) Levels 7-10.

entries.title: Petite Valse, Op. 28, No. 1
entries.description: Salon-style grazioso work. Calls for care in voicing the counter-melody in the alto, making this selection appropriate for the student who needs to work on melodic lines. (Musical Scope Publishers.) Level 10.

entries.title: Scales and Exercises
entries.description: Many of the exercises are of the Pischna type, with some notes to be sustained while other fingers play. Additional selections feature patterns to be repeated up and down the scale, or double-note exercises. A substantial number of the activities begin and end around C Major position. Scales and arpeggios in written-out forms are also included. (Alfred, G. Schirmer.) Levels 8-10.

entries.title: Leaves From My Diary, Books 1-3
entries.description: Twelve titled works in a salon-type style. Not especially profound. (G. Schirmer.) Level 8.

entries.title: H. Hofmann: 17 Miscellaneous Pieces, ed. Johnson
entries.description: Interesting writing. More of these intermediate teaching pieces should make their way into the piano repertoire. See especially Melodie, Op. 77, No. 5; In the Evening, Op. 88, No. 2; Country Waltz, Op. 117, No. 3; Elves, Op. 77, No. 17; and By the Mountain Torrent, Op. 37, No. 2. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music.) Levels 7-9.

entries.title: On an Overgrown Path
entries.description: A set of 15 seldom-heard character pieces expressing memories of the happy years of Janacek's youth. The writing is often lyrical and expressive. A single selection would be suitable for a high school student who enjoys expressive literature in the Romantic style. Groups of several pieces also would be effective in performance. (Masters Music Publications and other standard editions.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Popular Moravian Dances
entries.description: Folk song settings, similar in concept to those done by Bartok. Czech words are included for some selections. Traditional harmonies, often spirited settings and pianistic writing. (Ricordi.) Level 7.

entries.title: Wariacje
entries.description: This upper-intermediate set of variations on an opera melody "Nel cor pid non mi sento" (translated as "Hope told a flattering tale") is less difficult than Beethoven's variation set on the same theme. This set, slightly longer, features strong character changes between variations (one is in the style of a polka) and idiomatic keyboard figurations. Traditional harmonic framework. (PWM.) Level 9.

entries.title: Scenes of Travel, Op. 17
entries.description: Romantic character pieces that could be played by students not yet ready for the Chopin nocturnes. The writing is idiomatic although not particularly imaginative. See especially No. 3, The Mill and No. 7, Peaceful Afternoon. (Many standard editions.) Level 8.

entries.title: 25 Etudes, Op. 32, Books 1, 2 and 3
entries.description: Jensen followed the Romantic tradition in writing etudes that also became distinct character pieces. These etudes are of high quality, feel idiomatic on the piano, and might benefit a talented young pianist. They are both better and easier than those by Cramer and Moszkowski. These works can serve as repertoire pieces as well as technical studies. Most are two pages long. (Peters, Presser.) Level 9.

entries.title: Seven Miniatures, Op. 10
entries.description: Upper-intermediate to early-advanced teaching pieces, essentially lyrical in character. See especially the first, Souvenir, a good predecessor to the Chopin nocturnes. Additional titles include Petite Valse, Reproche, Intermezzo, Scherzino, Impromptu and Humoresque. (Boston Music.) Level 9.

entries.title: Album for the Young, Op. 25
entries.description: Eight titled character pieces, generally two pages long. Interesting repertoire choices that should be better known. See especially No. 2, Game of Patience; No. 5, Prayer; No. 6, By the Brook; and No. 8, Russian Dance. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music.) Levels 6-7.

entries.title: No. 6 By the Brook
entries.description: The image of a brook trickling over rocks is portrayed through this perpetual-motion piece. Two-note figurations alternate between the hands. Effective writing. Level 7.

entries.title: Albumblatter, Op. 7
entries.description: Nine untitled selections. Not particularly significant writing. (G. Schirmer.) Level 8.

entries.title: Skizzen, Op. 11
entries.description: These eight character pieces in the Heugel edition were extracted from the complete Op. 11 of Kirchner. They are shorter than many of Schumann's pieces from Op. 12 but are in the same style. (Heugel.) Level 9.

entries.title: Spielsachen, Op. 35
entries.description: These 14 pieces are shorter and somewhat easier than those in Op. 11 by Kirchner but are slightly more difficult than the works in Burgmiiller Op. 100. The Kirchner selections are in a Schumannesque style and highly Romantic, featuring thicker textures that require voicing of inner melodies and a refined sense of phrasing. For a student with a romantic flair, or for one needing to work on Romantic playing. High-quality writing. (Schott.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: New Scenes of Childhood, Op. 55
entries.description: The title reflects Kirchner's admiration for Schumann's works. First published in 1881, the 25 one-page pieces in this collection are charming, and could serve as a substitute for the frequently used Album for the Young, Op. 68 of Schumann. A group of them would make an interesting recital offering. See especially Nos. 7, 17, 23 and 25. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music.) Levels 6-7.

entries.title: No. 2 (Con moto)
entries.description: Lyrical work that approaches the style of the Chopin nocturnes and Mendelssohn Songs Without Words. Active accompaniment which requires careful voicing. One of the best of the set. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 5 (Ziemlich langsam, ausdrucksvoll)
entries.description: Highly expressive writing in which each hand needs musical independence to project the character. Phrases require strong definition. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 7 (Nicht zu schnell, aber lebhaft)
entries.description: Landler-like feeling pervades this lively work that is short and full of sequences. The wide jumps and intervallic reaches require careful listening. The pedal needs special attention to avoid blur in this quick tempo. This work sounds impressive yet requires minimum effort. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: No. 9 (Allegretto, poco agitato)
entries.description: This passionate work reflects the essence of the Romantic spirit. The melody is found in the low bass register. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 13 (Poco Allegro)
