Country Pageant contains four intermediate works: Merry Andrew's Procession, Kings and Queens, There was a most beautiful lady and The Mummers' Dance. A Little Book of Dances is slightly more difficult, with all pieces in dance forms, including Minuet, Pavane, Galliard and Jig. Conservative harmonic vocabulary. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music.) Levels 7-9.

entries.title: 10 Piano Pieces for Children, Op. 56b
entries.description: Well-written kinesthetically for the piano. Moderately contemporary. See especially Puffing Bill, Ball Game and Dance in the Moonlight. (Simrock.) Levels 5-6.

entries.title: Sonatina
entries.description: Dissonant linear writing. The last of the three movements is decidedly the most difficult. (Simrock.) Level 7.

entries.title: Bagatelles
entries.description: Six brief, titled pieces for the elementary pianist. Occasional sections of bitonality are cleverly handled. Interesting and different. (Novello.) Level 4.

entries.title: Children's Pieces for Adults
entries.description: Mildly contemporary solos with titles such as A Stuffed Lion Called Jonathan, Italian Pony Cart, The Toy Donkey and Traffic. (Peer International.) Level 8.

entries.title: Le petit dne Blanc (The Little White Donkey)
entries.description: Humorous depiction. Left-hand staccato needs clean definition. Suitable piece to work on finger independence and articulation. A student favorite and the best-known of the set. Level 9.

entries.title: A Giddy Girl
entries.description: Visually difficult to read, but less difficult to play. Highly patterned and idiomatic to the keyboard. Marked to be played, "In the style of a sentimental English romance." Students ready for their first Debussy preludes might find this selection to be a good alternative. Humorous and playful writing. Level 8.

entries.title: La cage de cristal (The Crystal Cage)
entries.description: Animated, with several tempo changes in a good-natured selection featuring light glissando-like figures and lyrical melodic lines and light chordal accompaniment. Level 9.

entries.title: La marchande d'eau fraiche
entries.description: Witty writing featuring a rhythmic ostinato with fragmented melody. The hands are positioned on top of each other much of the time. Worth investigating. Level 10.

entries.title: Petite Suite en Quinze Images (1943)
entries.description: High-quality writing in 15 intermediate pieces that are substantially easier than those from Histoires and generally shorter. See especially Le gai vigneron, Le cavalier sans-souci, Parade, Romance, La machine a coudre and Premier bal. These selections should be better known. Level 8.

entries.title: Scherzetto
entries.description: Spirited and patterned, built primarily on the same theme throughout. The economy of material makes this a delightful piece for recreational study. (Leduc.) Level 9.

entries.title: Tableaux de Voyage, Op. 33
entries.description: Thirteen travel portraits. Pleasant and appealing within a neo-Romantic idiom. See especially En Marche, Paturage, Le Glas, Halte, au Soir and Lermoos. (Leduc.) Level 9.

entries.title: Invention
entries.description: Two-page work with large reaches. Often one voice moves in sixteenths against eighths in the other two voices. (Hinshaw.) Level 8.

entries.title: 12 Pieces for Children, Op. 62, Books 1 and 2
entries.description: Interesting writing. (Associated.) Levels 3-5.

entries.title: Improvisation on a Mormon Hymn "Come, come, ye saints"
entries.description: This well-known Mormon hymn is given a two-page setting that features full chords and effective use of the piano sonority. Several hymn adaptations are found here. (Oxford.) Level 7.

entries.title: Four Abstractions
entries.description: Brief atonal sketches, easier than Schoenberg Six Little Piano Pieces, Op. 19 and featuring thin textures and wide melodic leaps. For the contemporary music enthusiast who plays at the intermediate level. (Elkan.) Level 5.

entries.title: Im Ferienlager, Op. 3/86
entries.description: Set of six medium-length pieces. The writing is more highly contemporary than in many of Kabalevsky's pieces. Mature character pieces, strongly motivic. (Sikorski.) Level 8.

entries.title: Four Preludes, Op. 5
entries.description: Thick textures, and short in length. Interesting neo-Romantic pieces that foreshadow the later Preludes, Op. 38. Could be considered as an introduction to the two Sonatinas, Op. 13. Infrequently heard. (G. Schirmer, MCA.) Level 9.

entries.title: Sonatina in A Minor, Op. 13, No. 1 (1930)
entries.description: This sonatina should not be confused with the one-movement Sonatina in A Minor, Op. 27, No. 18. Both the first and third movements are based on sonata-allegro form. The first movement is energetic, opening with three fanfare-like chords and ending with a splash. The second movement, Andantino, is an ABA song-form. The third movement is a real showpiece, requiring fast movement in 6/8 meter. Contains a great deal of triplet passagework. (Alfred, Kalmus, MCA, International and G. Schirmer.) Level 8.

entries.title: Sonatina in G Minor, Op. 13, No. 2 (1933)
entries.description: Four movements in a work that is longer and more serious than the Sonatina in A Minor, Op. 13, No. 1. See especially the vigorous and marchlike first movement, which requires fine rhythmic control of triplets and the playing of double notes. The fourth movement is also notable for its brilliant opening and passagework. Calls for the playing of consecutive thirds. (Alfred, MCA, Kalmus.) Level 9.

entries.title: No. 1 Waltz
entries.description: A study in the playing of two-note slurs that overlap between the right and left hands. An expressive, cantabile piece. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 2 A Little Song
entries.description: Beautiful, lyrical melody, which is set against a sustained doublenote accompaniment in slower note values. Appropriate for developing evenness of tone and finesse in tonal control at the early elementary level. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 3 Etude in A Minor
entries.description: Dazzling in performance. The primary difficulties are in the righthand descending B-flat scale and left-hand coordinating figuration. Especially good for the student with fast fingers. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 4 At Night on the River
entries.description: Simple lyrical work, similar in texture to No. 2, but slightly more complex. Enchanting writing that is seldom heard. Needs skill in pacing the rubato. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 5 Playing Ball
entries.description: Repeated notes in rapid passagework. Alternate hands play throughout and a rhythmic ostinato unifies the work. Appropriate for the student able to play fast passages. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 6 A Sad Story
entries.description: Tender piece in which the right hand plays a melody above a chordal accompaniment. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 7 An Old Dance
entries.description: Reminiscent of a Baroque minuet. The quick trills and varied articulations may pose problems. One of the least frequently heard pieces in this set. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 8 Cradle Song (Lullaby)
entries.description: Descending broken seventh chords, perhaps simulating a rocking motion. The right and left hand exchange melody and accompaniment. Programmatic writing in a cantabile work. This selection can help develop a feeling for seventh chords as well as a balance of melody and accompaniment. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 9 Little Fable
entries.description: Generally the hands play in unison, calling for strong inflection of the melody. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 11 Rondo
entries.description: Generally in two voices, occasionally with some parallel legato thirds in the right hand. Kabalevsky surprises the listener by varying the melody each time it returns. Mysterious in character. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 12 Toccatina
entries.description: An excellent work for developing the feeling of first inversion triads in the right hand. Study in legato melody in the left hand and staccato chords in the right hand. Requires balance of left-hand melody over right-hand accompaniment. Perhaps the most popular selection in this collection. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 14 Scherzo
entries.description: Calls for voicing of melody and the playing of a scherzando accompaniment. Requires a leggiero touch and strong fingers. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 15 March
entries.description: Primarily staccato work in march tempo. Contains ascending and descending passages in eighths with hands in parallel motion. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 16 Lyric Piece
entries.description: Calls for a mature performer to shape the expressive lines in this neo-Romantic work. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 17 Meadow Dance
entries.description: Continuous left-hand triads skip between octaves under a lyrical melody in the upper register. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 18 Sonatina
entries.description: One-movement work in A Minor, march-like with dotted rhythms throughout. Three distinct key areas. The left hand provides blocked chord accompaniment. Snappy rhythms. Level 5.

entries.title: No. 19 Warlike Dance
entries.description: Aggressive writing, effective in performance, and requiring a performer with strong fingers and the ability to play double notes. Strong rhythms. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 20 Fairy Tale (A Short Story)
entries.description: Melody with left-hand broken chord accompaniment, often in second inversion chords broken from the top. Sensitive writing, for the mature performer. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 21 The Chase
entries.description: A 6/8 work filled with triplets and accidentals, with some surprising modulations. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 22 A Tale
entries.description: Seldom heard, perhaps due to the many accidentals. Interesting modulations create unusual harmonic tension. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 23 Snow Storm
entries.description: A presto work built on two- or three-note slurs with the passagework alternating between the hands. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 24 Etude
entries.description: A splendid F-Major study in the playing of arpeggios and in expansion and contraction of the hand. Rapid arpeggios are played noteagainst-note against rapid descending/ascending scale passages. Effective in performance. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 25 Novelette
entries.description: One of the most popular works in this collection, perhaps due to the haunting melody and the effective building of tension through the harmonic writing. Marked molto sostenuto, this work could serve as a student's first study in playing right hand double-note passages. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 26 Etude
entries.description: This A-Major work is the least well-known of the three works titled "Etude" in this volume. Contains simulated "horn calls" and requires strong fingers. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 27 Dance
entries.description: A study in double thirds, this Russian dance features staccato passages which alternate between the hands. Some unpredictable harmonic turns. Good for developing finger strength. An energetic piece. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 28 Caprice
entries.description: Much of the piece consists of sustained and tied half notes with staccato passagework played above or below the held note written in a single hand. Requires finger independence. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 29 Cavalry Gallop (Song of the Cavalry)
entries.description: Persistent rhythms portray the charge of a cavalry brigade. Requires refined voicing. Off-beat chords pervade the thick texture. Written primarily on the black keys. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 30 Dramatic Episode
entries.description: Slow, sober work, perhaps intended as a satiric commentary on serious music. The dotted rhythms and resonant chords in the middle section create a pompous conclusion to this opus. Level 7.

entries.title: Jugendleben, Op. 14 (1931/1968)
entries.description: These two-page character pieces are filled with fresh ideas and musical vitality. Titles include A Brisk Game, The Drummer, In the Gymnasium and Soldiers' March. (MCA, Sikorski.) Level 7.

entries.title: No. 1 (Andantino)
entries.description: Displays a lyricism that reveals full-blown Romanticism. Much of the accompaniment is comprised of repeated chords. One of the best of the Preludes. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 2 (Scherzando)
entries.description: Probably the most popular Prelude in the set, this scherzo-like work in A Minor requires a crisp touch and a strong rhythmic sense. Light staccato triads, fast leggiero passagework, biting chords and dissonant effects are found throughout. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 8 (Andante non troppo. Semplice e cantando)
entries.description: Features a slow accompaniment in quarter notes above which a cantilena melody is heard. Somewhat reminiscent of Satie. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 12 (Adagio)
entries.description: An adagio work in arch form ABCBA, highly dramatic and arresting in its intensity. Segments of a folk-tune are separated by original interludes. Level 10.

entries.title: No. 15 (Allegretto marcato)
entries.description: Similar to a Prokofiev march, with clockwork rhythms and daring harmonic progressions. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 20 (Andantino semplice)
entries.description: A deep work with some harmonic ambiguity. Level 9.

entries.title: 24 Pieces for Children, Op. 39
entries.description: Some of the easiest music by a major composer. Each selection presents a particular technical problem, and many can be played in the first or second year of study. Worthy of an integral place in the standard teaching repertoire. (Alfred, International, Kalmus, MCA and others.) Levels 1-6.

entries.title: No. I A Little Tune
entries.description: Melody in quarter and half notes over whole-note accompaniment. Level 1.

entries.title: No. 2 Polka
entries.description: Legato quarter-note melody in the left hand and off-beat staccato chords played by the right hand. Level 1.

entries.title: No. 5 Playing (A Game)
entries.description: A staccato piece using primarily quarter notes. Emphasizes large motions and alternation between the hands. Should be played with one pulse per measure rather than three. Level 1.

entries.title: No. 6 A Little Joke
entries.description: Highly patterned piece in which both hands remain in relatively stationary positions on the keyboard. Level 2.

entries.title: No. 7 Funny Event
entries.description: Sometimes titled Conversation, quite appropriately, since this piece is largely a dialogue between the hands. Humorous and light piece filled with patterns. Level 1.

entries.title: No. 8 Song
entries.description: Lyrical contoured melody, played in unison at the distance of two octaves between the hands. Appropriate to teach inflection and phrasing of a musical line in the first year. Level 1.

entries.title: No. 9 Dance
entries.description: Staccato passagework and chords, with little movement of hands on the keyboard. Appealing dance for beginning students who have strong hands. Level 1.

entries.title: No. 10 March
entries.description: Snappy rhythms lend a flair that can appeal to beginning students. The passagework is based totally on broken chords. Level 2.

entries.title: No. I 1 Song of Autumn
entries.description: The hands play in unison at the distance of two octaves. Lyrical writing with some hand shifts. Good for the development of phrasing. Level 2.

entries.title: No. 12 Scherzo
entries.description: A pattern piece that can easily be taught by rote early in a student's study. Much easier than it sounds, and effective in performance. Level 1.

entries.title: No. 13 Waltz
entries.description: Graceful writing with a simple right-hand melody, and left-hand accompaniment punctuated by double thirds. Level 1.

entries.title: No. 14 A Fable
entries.description: Three-part piece in the form AA'A with the central A' section being a minor version of the two outer A sections. Easier than it appears and one which should be better known. Level 2.

entries.title: No. 15 lumping
entries.description: An appealing piece in which both hands play the same notes an octave apart, although one hand lags behind the other to create musical interest. Level 1.

entries.title: No. 16 A Sad Tale
entries.description: Two-voice writing, with a lyrical melody and an accompaniment moving in quarter notes. Level 1.

entries.title: No. 17 Folk Dance (Country Dance)
entries.description: Playful, canonic work with constantly shifting five-finger patterns and rapid staccato eighths. A light touch is needed. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 18 Galop
entries.description: Buoyant work in which the large right-hand leaps, though effective, make this more difficult than some of the earlier selections. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 19 Prelude
entries.description: This busy piece features five-finger patterns and scales, often in G Minor and C Minor keys. Requires independence between the hands. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 20 Clowns
entries.description: The continual shift from major to minor and back to major and the inversion of the melody throughout are all types of musical clowning. A light left-hand touch is needed for this deservedly popular selection. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 22 A Short Story
entries.description: Serious work that uses the high register of the piano. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 24 A Happy Outing
entries.description: Interesting writing with some jump-bass left-hand passages. More difficult than most of the pieces in this collection and seldom heard. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 1 Variations
entries.description: Continuous set of 12 character variations beginning with a fourmeasure introduction and concluding with a Coda. The theme is based on a descending D Major scale. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 2 Variations
entries.description: Theme and five variations in A Minor. Little-known work which displays a sadness and simplicity throughout. Not his most inspired writing. Level 8.

entries.title: Five Sets of Variations on Folk Themes, Op. 51 (1952)
entries.description: Strong writing that needs a performer with flair and the ability to achieve sharp contrast. All based on folk songs. Students who enjoy energetic and driving literature will enjoy playing these works. Available separately. (MCA, Masters Music and others.) Levels 4-8.

entries.title: No. 1 Variations on the Theme of a Russian Folk Song
entries.description: Sometimes known as Five Happy Variations on a Russian Folk Song, this is the most accessible variation set in this collection. Thin textures here call for skillful inflection of the melodic lines and careful detail to articulation. Robust and effective ending. Three pages. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 2 Variations of the Theme of a Russian Folk Song
entries.description: Sometimes known as Merry Dance Variations on a Russian Folk Song, this set contains an eight-measure theme and six variations. The rhythmic values and the coordination between hands are more complex. Three pages. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 3 Variations on the Theme of a Slovak Folk Song
entries.description: Titled Gray Day Variations on a Slovakian Folk Song in several collections, this set is one of the most effective in Op. 51. The lyrical theme that opens this set is soon displaced by lively and animated variations that build to a sparkling climax in variation five before the return of the opening cantabile theme in variation 6. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 4 Variations on the Theme of a Ukrainian Folk Song
entries.description: In some collections this set is titled Seven Good-Humored Variations on a Ukrainian Folk Song. A scherzando theme opens this lively set of seven variations. Requires a student with flair for the virtuoso sound. Five printed pages. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 5 Variations on the Theme of a Ukrainian Folk Song
entries.description: Sometimes known as Six Variations on a Ukrainian Folk Song. Opening with a cantando theme, this little-known variation set grad ually builds to an effective peak before subsiding at the close of the set. Perhaps the most lyrical of the variation sets. More difficult than it appears. Level 7.

entries.title: Variations on Folk Themes, Op. 87
entries.description: Three seldom-heard variation sets on themes of America, France and Japan. More difficult than the earlier sets. (Masters Music.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: No. 1 Variations on an American Folk Song (1966)
entries.description: Six variations on "All the Pretty Little Horses." Spunky writing in a version that is not childish. Note the tranquillo restatement of the theme at the conclusion. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 2 Variations on a French Folk Theme (1968)
entries.description: A sustained theme opens this work in 4/4 and closes it in 3/4. A good-natured selection with effective writing filled with capricious passagework. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 3 Variations on a Japanese Folk Theme (1969)
entries.description: The most technically involved of the sets in this opus and perhaps the most effective. Broken-chord passagework is found throughout. Pesante ending. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 1 March
entries.description: The March presents the theme in sixths in the right hand with a punctuating accompaniment low in the bass. The appeal stems in part from the catchy dotted rhythm of the main theme. Martial character, enjoyable to play. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 2 Dance
entries.description: A lilting work in 3/8 with buoyant rhythms. This amiable and graceful movement needs a light elegant touch. The lively staccatos and the predictable rhythms give it the dance-like character. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: No. 3 Song
entries.description: Requires a rich, singing tone in the melody. Plaintive melody with triadic passages needs skilled inflection. Kabalevsky says that this piece should remind one of a lullaby. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: No. 4 Toccata
entries.description: Spirited and humorous, and highly effective in performance. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Preludes and Fugues, Op. 61
entries.description: Six neo-Baroque preludes and fugues with thin textures. The preludes are relatively short and all are accessible. These selections could be played prior to some of the Bach Sinfonias or fugues from the Well-Tempered Clavier. Especially suitable for developing contrapuntal sight-reading. The set contains one four-voice fugue, with the remaining fugues composed in two or three voices. See especially No. 2, with its gigue-like fugue, as well as No. 6. (International, Leeds and others.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Spring Games and Dances, Op. 81
entries.description: A free-form suite in a playful, dance-like character with frequent return to the main theme, thus imparting the character of a rondo. Should be played continuously as a set. (MCA, VAAP.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Six Pieces for Piano, Op. 88 (Children's Dreams)
entries.description: These works, composed relatively late in Kabalevsky's life, show the continuation of his interest in writing music for children. The pieces display less rhythmic vitality than some of his earlier works. Titles include Dreams, Who'll Win the Argument?, Tale of an Old Organ-Grinder, Contrast, Queer Waltz and Naughty Boys. (Zen-On.) Levels 6-7.

entries.title: Children's Adventures, Op. 89 (1972)
entries.description: In many respects this collection of 35 easy pieces can serve as a parallel collection to 24 Pieces for Children, Op. 39. Found here are very easy pieces, some only 16 measures long. Significant collection. See especially No. 8, A Little Porcupine; No. 15, The Trumpeter and the Echo; No. 20, The Trumpet and the Drum; No. 21, The Little Juggler; No. 26, A Merry Tune; No. 34, A Melancholic Rain; and No. 35, At the River. (Sikorski.) Levels 2-3.

entries.title: Lyric Tunes, Op. 91
entries.description: Slavic music consisting of a series of movements connected by attacas. Opens with a prelude, followed by a waltz, variations and Coda. Improvisatory writing that requires supple phrasing. Should be better known. (Hal Leonard.) Level 9.

entries.title: Kabalevsky: An Introduction to His Piano Works, ed. Palmer
entries.description: A practical teaching collection containing some of Kabalevsky's most accessible works from Children's Pieces, Op. 27 and 24 Pieces for Children, Op. 39. (Alfred.) Levels 1-8.

entries.title: Kabalevsky: The First Book for Young Pianists, ed. Palmer
entries.description: Presents the most accessible pieces from the book Kabalevsky: An Introduction to His Piano Works, ed. Palmer. (Alfred.) Levels 1-6.

entries.title: At the Piano with Kabalevsky, ed. Hinson
entries.description: An interesting variety of pieces is found here including Four Rondos, Op. 60; A Brisk Game, Op. 14, No. 1; selections from Op. 27; and two variation sets. (Alfred.) Levels 3-8.

entries.title: 25 Piano Pieces by Dmitri Kabalevsky, ed. T. Dubrovskaya and M. Kabalevskaya
entries.description: Contains a wide variety of pieces by Kabalevsky including selections from Opp. 27, 30, 39, 51, 61, 88 and 89. Edited by Kabalevsky's daughter in consultation with Tatiyana Dubrovskaya, a teacher at the Moscow Central Musical School. Worth investigating. (Hal Leonard.) Levels 2-9.

entries.title: Epigrams, Op. 3 (1923-34)
entries.description: Contains eight short titled pieces. The writing is folk-like and highly expressive, calling for dramatic mood changes. Should be better known. Some selections use alternating triads, harmonics, indeterminate repetitions and gypsy-like idioms. (Editio Musica.) Levels 3-5.

entries.title: Al Fresco, Op. 11/a
entries.description: Three short pieces, highly reminiscent of Bart6k. Driving rhythms, folk vitality and strong characterization mark these movements. Movement titles are Capriccio in modo ongharese, Allegro robusto and Allegro giocoso. Level 10.

entries.title: Sonatina, Op. 11/b
entries.description: In two movements. The first movement with biting dissonance, rhythmic vitality, and shifting meters is akin to the music of Bartok. The brief finale spans a dynamic range from pq' to ppp. (Boosey.) Level 9.

entries.title: Folk Song Suite, Op. 21
entries.description: Strong and vibrant rhythms in a work that should be better known. Folk song flavor, similar in many respects to the Bart6k Suite, Op. 14. (Boosey.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: Five Studies, Op. 23/f
entries.description: Rarely do we find contemporary etudes written by contemporary composers for the upper-intermediate to early-advanced student. Short and musically inspired. (EMB.) Level 8.

entries.title: 55 Small Piano Pieces, Volumes 1-2
entries.description: Similar in concept to the Bart6k Mikrokosmos, these are fine progressive teaching pieces, of folk inspiration. Less dissonant than some Bart6k. Reflects the native folk idiom of Hungary. Volume 1 contains 31 pieces and volume two contains 24 works. Should be better known. (EMB.) Levels 1-7.

entries.title: No. 25 Toccatina
entries.description: A perpetual motion, motto vivo, in eighths, effective and little known. Requires evenness and steady rhythm but generally remains in fivefinger patterns. Sounds more difficult than it is. Level 4.

entries.title: Snapshots, Op. 69 (1971)
entries.description: Five short pieces featuring frequent meter changes and much dissonance. Strong writing for the mature performer. (EMB.) Level 8.

entries.title: No. 3 (Tempo diminuetto)
entries.description: Excellent writing in the contemporary idiom. Needs strong changes of character. Level 7.

entries.title: Sonatina in B flat
entries.description: In one movement, four pages long. Features thin textures, linear writing and biting dissonance. The phrase structure is not readily evident. Requires much use of forearm staccato. (EMB.) Level 4.

entries.title: 24 Easy Technical Etudes
entries.description: Intriguing and accessible etudes in a contemporary Hungarian folk idiom, for the early-intermediate performer. The music is readable and the studies are relatively short (often less than one page). (Boosey.) Level 4.

entries.title: Sonatine fur Klavier
entries.description: Short contemporary work in four movements featuring writing that is predominantly rhythmic. Effective, highly pianistic. Good for a performer with small hands. (Doblinger.) Level 7.

entries.title: 24 Piano Pieces for Children
entries.description: Continues in the strong Hungarian tradition of producing worthy modernist teaching materials for young pianists. The pieces here are progressive in difficulty and filled with spirited rhythms, modal folk writing, and sparkling passagework. Somewhat reminiscent of BartOk For Children. (EMB.) Levels 2-4.

entries.title: Etude Impressioniste
entries.description: Beautiful writing in a two-page solo that is especially appropriate for the student not yet ready to play Debussy Preludes but wanting to play Impressionist literature. (Lemoine.) Level 7.

entries.title: Four Inventions
entries.description: Neo-Baroque writing, especially effective performed as a set. Andante moderato is lyrical and contrapuntal with gracious melodic contours. Scherzando in 5/8 is a humorous invention, full of gaiety. Larghetto features expanding intervals and strongly melodic writing. The last invention, Allegro, is full of energy, surprising syncopations and sweep. Grows to a dramatic climax. (MCA.) Level 9.

entries.title: The Donkey Walks Along the Beach
entries.description: Nine pieces in a rhythmic and highly dissonant contemporary idiom with melodic writing that communicates well. (Peters.) Levels 4-5.

entries.title: Three Preludes (1938)
entries.description: A staple in the contemporary teaching repertory. The first is a playful, humorous Allegro scherzando in which the passages in double notes need attention. The brief second Prelude is in the style of a modern chorale. The concluding Prelude, Allegro con fuoco, is a loud and vigorous toccata with repeated octaves. Deservedly popular. (G. Schirmer.) Level 9.

entries.title: Two Preludes (1951)
entries.description: Neo-Romantic works with thick textures and chromatic writing. These have not gained the popularity of the first set of preludes. (G. Schirmer.) Level 10.

entries.title: No. 2 No Walking Today
entries.description: Could be a student's first introduction to double-note playing in one hand. Fine writing for the child committed to serious study. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 3 Ivan Is Sick
entries.description: Interesting harmonies in a primarily chordal setting. Good for voicing the tops of chords and for studying contemporary harmonic procedures. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 4 The Birthday
entries.description: Dance-like and festive in character. The unpredictable sounds and the movement about the keyboard render this piece harder than it sounds. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 5 Etude
entries.description: Refined coordination between the hands is required in this exciting and effective selection. Precision is needed to play the repeated notes and alternating figures. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 6 A Musical Picture
entries.description: A beautiful and seldom-heard movement. Those with an affinity toward Romantic music will enjoy the lush sounds and powerful climaxes. The phrasing needs natural pacing. A gem still waiting to be discovered. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 7 Invention
entries.description: Somewhat academic. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 8 Fugue
entries.description: One of the few two-voice fugues in the standard literature. Highly dissonant. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 9 Horseman's March
entries.description: A repeated rhythmic figure provides the feeling of a horse galloping. A relaxed arm is necessary for the repeated figures. Appealing. Level 5.

entries.title: Children's Album, Volume 2 (1964)
entries.description: These pieces are less well known than those in Volume 1, but the quality of the writing is high. (Peters, VAAP.) Levels 3-9.

entries.title: No. 4 Little Leopard on the Swing
entries.description: Impressionistic writing in a selection that would provide excellent preparation for the more advanced music of Debussy. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: No. 5 Snare Drum
entries.description: Straightforward work filled with humor and strong character. "Wrong-note" writing. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 6 Two Ladies Gossiping
entries.description: Joking writing in a dance-like, toccata framework. Calls for quick right-hand playing of passagework. One of the most difficult pieces in this volume, but a real showpiece. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 9 Toccata
entries.description: This work is as exciting as the much more famous Toccata of Khachaturian, and equally difficult. Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Two Pieces (1926)
entries.description: The movements are Valse-Caprice and Dance, both energetic. For many years Dance was a popular examination piece for the Trinity College in England. (Kalmus and others.) Level 8.

entries.title: Sonatina (1959)
entries.description: Brilliant and rhythmic writing that students enjoy. The first movement is the most popular due to the mild clashes and tuneful writing in the melody over broken or blocked left-hand octaves. The seldom-played second movement features a sad, singing melody over a basically chordal accompaniment. The last movement suffers from being too long to balance the other movements. It is nevertheless virtuosic and exciting. Level 8.

entries.title: I. Allegro giocoso
entries.description: Exciting movement calling for fast fingers and a strong sense of rhythm. Left-hand octaves, broken and blocked, need to be detached and never heavy. The central cantabile section is more difficult than many performers realize. Level 7.

entries.title: II. Andante con anima, rubato
entries.description: Expressive work in 3/4 with long lines and many changes of character. Dissonances lend a contemplative quality. Requires musical maturity. Level 7.

entries.title: III. Allegro mosso
entries.description: By far the most difficult movement of the Sonatina. Containing many meter changes, this is a toccata in rondo form that requires strict rhythmic control and evenness. Harp-like sixteenth-note passages between the hands demand rapid hand shifts and fine coordination. An extended movement with an effective ending. (Alfred, Kalmus, MCA, Peters and others.) Level 9.

The passages contain idiomatic and athletic patterns that lie well in the hand. The propulsive rhythms enhance the drama and flair required. Strong fingers are needed. The main pitfall appears in the middle section with the cross-rhythms that are difficult to count. (Alfred, Kalmus, MCA, Peters and others.) Level 9.

entries.title: Little Suite for Solo Piano (1949)
entries.description: Short one-page sketches featuring no particular tonal framework. Changing meters and lyrical writing. Movement titles are I Prelude, II Song, III Toccata, IV Fantasy and V Epilogue. (Mercury.) Level 8.

entries.title: Neun Klavierstiicke fiir Sonja, Op. 76
entries.description: Nine untitled, highly contemporary movements for piano. For the sophisticated performer. More difficult than they appear on the page. (Barenreiter.) Level 8.

entries.title: From the Country
entries.description: Subtitled Pictures for the Young Pianist, these pieces are mildly contemporary and contain thin textures. They would be good for the young student who does not read well since the spacing on the page is clean. Fine supplementary material for elementary students. Titles include A Beautiful Morning, The Moon Rises, Snow Falling and Rondino for a Quacking Duck. (General Music Publications, Inc.) Level 2.

entries.title: Nine Piano Pieces, Op. 3 (1910)
entries.description: Kodaly's most important work for piano students. For its day, it presented a novel blend of folk elements and progressive sounds. Best suited for the performer with a sophisticated musical ear and a feeling for improvised folk music. (MCA and others.) Level 10.

entries.title: Valsette (1907)
entries.description: Originally a part of the Nine Piano Pieces, Op. 3. A short work with introduction in the form ABA' and Coda. Contains an ostinato accompaniment, hemiola and some emphasis on the interval of the third. Modal writing. (EMB, Masters.) Level 8.

entries.title: 24 Little Canons on the Black Keys
entries.description: Originally composed as part of the Kodaly Choral Method in 1945. The first 16 canons include the solfege syllable abbreviations written under the rhythmic notation. Only one line is provided and the student plays the second hand in canon an octave higher or lower, reading the solfege indications and note values. Canons 17-24 are written on two staves and are to be played a semitone higher. These pieces would likely be used most successfully by a Kodaly-method teacher. (Boosey.) Levels 4-5.

entries.title: 1. (Allegretto)
entries.description: Binary form work with ostinato rhythms in melody and accompaniment, which consists of blocked fifths and thirds. Level 1.

entries.title: 2. (Allegretto cantabile)
entries.description: Imitative work with variation. Both hands move in eighth-note motion. Level 2.

entries.title: 3. (Vivace)
entries.description: Quartal sounds and parallelism are present. Level 2.

entries.title: 4. (Moderato cantabile)
entries.description: Left hand melody. Contains large chords with added seconds, metric changes and chordal melodies. Level 3.

entries.title: 5. (Allegro moderato, poco rubato)
entries.description: Chords with added seconds and a strongly syncopated accompaniment, which lends excitement. Thicker texture than in the preceding movements. Level 3.

entries.title: 6. (Vivace Hornpipe)
entries.description: Ostinato accompaniment and drone writing in one of the most immediately inviting selections in this collection. Level 3.

entries.title: 7. (Vivace, quasi marcia)
entries.description: Off-beat chordal accompaniment, with melody appearing in both hands. Level 3.

entries.title: 8. (Friss)
entries.description: Work in ternary form with an exciting presto Coda. The term "friss" refers to the faster of two dances paired in the Hungarian "csardas." Ostinato accompaniment of broken chords. Worth investigating. Level 4.

entries.title: 9. (Allegro marcato)
entries.description: Emphasis on unison playing and on fourths and fifths. Strong rhythmic drive and fortissimo ending. Level 4.

entries.title: 10. (Allegretto leggiero)
entries.description: Off-beat accompaniment and chords spanning the octave with the third omitted. Strong rhythmic vitality. Level 4.

entries.title: 12 Little Pieces (1973)
entries.description: Very easy pieces, most in eight measures, for the beginner. Some of the left-hand accompaniments feature two-note chords while others are linear. All except two (which center around D) are notated using the white keys but are to be played on the black keys only. Very simple pieces with pentatonic sounds. (Boosey.) Level 1.

entries.title: 12 Petites Pieces Faciles, Op. 208
entries.description: Most are contrapuntal, primarily two voices, although several homophonic pieces are included. The writing is sometimes modal. This is serious music by a major composer that is accessible to the elementary student. (Heugel.) Level 3.

entries.title: Nouvelles Sonatines, Op. 87
entries.description: Four sonatinas, each in three movements and published separately. These pieces are filled with subtle nuances. (Salabert.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: Easy Animal Pieces
entries.description: Subtitled "A First Book for the Piano," found here are 20 very easy pieces. Some are bitonal and would provide young students with a good introduction to contemporary sounds. The rhythmic values are basic and the textures relatively thin. Titles include Two Zebras, The Lion (with an effective drone bass), The Hungry Aardvark (an inverted canon in two keys), Monkeys at Play and Rabbits. (General Music Publishing.) Level 1.

entries.title: Easy Pieces for Piano
entries.description: A collection of avant-garde pieces featuring traditional notation. Textures are generally thin, with much disjunct movement. (Universal.) Level 8.

entries.title: 12 Short Piano Pieces, Op. 83 (1938)
entries.description: Twelve-tone pieces, all composed on the same tone row, which is stated with its various permutations at the beginning of the volume. An excellent introduction to serial technique. Titles include Glass Figures and The Sailing Boat Reflected in the Pond. (G. Schirmer.) Level 9.

entries.title: Echoes from Austria (1958)
entries.description: Seven pieces within a tonal framework. (Rongwen.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: Celebrations and Epilogue
entries.description: Set of 10 short, titled and highly contemporary pieces. Dissonant harmonies, strong rhythmic character. Most appropriate for the mature student. (Southern Music.) Level 9.

entries.title: Improvisations for Piano (1963)
entries.description: Short avant-garde pieces for young people featuring such devices used as indeterminate notation, clusters, free repetitions, spatial notation, use of harmonics, free ordering of sections of a piece and others. Colorful drawings add interest to the volume, although the scores themselves will be fascinating to many. The brevity of these selections makes their study feasible. (PVM.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: 14 Canonic Inventions for Young Composers
entries.description: Presented with a detailed analysis of each canon given on the same page. The analytical material includes scrutiny of the contrapuntal methods used. (General Music.) Level 7.

entries.title: Sonatina No. 1
entries.description: Boisterous and spirited writing in a mildly contemporary idiom. Effective and easy to read. A student able to play the Khachaturian Sonatina (1959) likely would be drawn to this work also. Worth investigating. (Leeds.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Second Impromptu
entries.description: Two-page work filled with large full chords in a texture similar to that of the Chopin Prelude in C Minor, Op. 28, No. 15. Large hand span required. Level 7.

entries.title: Four Variations on a Theme by Paganini
entries.description: Deserving of its popularity. The variations are filled with vitality, strongly idiomatic figures and mildly modern sounds. (Carl Fischer.) Level 8.

entries.title: Child's Play
entries.description: A cycle of seven avant-garde pieces featuring new notation, clusters and other devices. The texture is not thick, and interesting sonorities are elicited here. A good introduction to avant-garde literature and a fascinating score to decipher. All instructions are in German. (Breitkopf.) Level 9.

entries.title: Copycats
entries.description: Canons in five-finger positions, providing fine preparation for contrapuntal study. All of the canons are at the octave, except for one that is at the fifth. (Summy-Birchard.) Level 3.

entries.title: Questioning
entries.description: A two-page solo, adapted by the composer from his Sonata for Flute Solo. Thin texture. Sophisticated, though not difficult. (Oxford.) Level 4.

entries.title: 12 Relationships, Op. 10
entries.description: Each of the canons is at a different interval. Most are one page long and all, except the canon at the octave, are bitonal. Titles include Bold and Plain, Teasing, Sprightly, Piquant and Spirited. (Carl Fischer.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: A Child's Picture Book
entries.description: Significant contemporary writing for the intermediate student. Interesting sonorities in a contemporary idiom that features generally free use of all 12 tones. Titles include Story for a Rainy Day (tender writing calling for a singing tone), lack Frost (calling for crisp staccato and featuring sforzando) and The Giant Has a Hobby-Horse. (Broude.) Level 7.

entries.title: No. 1 Even Coolies Watch the Sunset
entries.description: A serene piece permeated by open fifths and seconds with some twopart writing in the right hand. The left hand has large leaps to navigate. Attractive. Levels 6-7.

entries.title: No. 3 Story for a Rainy Day
entries.description: Expressive work with hidden subtleties. A work for a sensitive student. Should be better known. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 5 Pageant
entries.description: Vigorous and dramatic work. The many accidentals make the chords somewhat difficult to read. Levels 6-7.

entries.title: Toccata, Op. 1
entries.description: Driving work in 6/8, built on many open fifths, with hand-alternating passagework and a bravura ending. Some awkward sections, but effective and motivating. (Broude.) Level 9.

entries.title: Pieces minuscules pour les enfants, Op. 16
entries.description: Contemporary teaching literature of exceptionally high quality and strong characterization in these vignettes, often 16 to 20 measures. All selections are untitled. Two books of six pieces each. Would provide a refreshing addition to contest lists. (Durand.) Level 7.

entries.title: Granada
entries.description: Smaller hands could handle this, and the musical interpretation is not involved. (Marks.) Level 9.

entries.title: Andalucia
entries.description: From his Spanish suite of the same name, this work features a simple melody, full chords and appealing Spanish rhythms. Deserving of its popularity. (Marks.) Level 10.

entries.title: Danzas Afro-Cubanas
entries.description: Six highly rhythmic and colored pieces. Not easy. See especially the last, La Comparsa, which is the best known in this volume. (Marks and others.) Level 10.

entries.title: 19th-Century Cuban Dances
entries.description: Set of 10 pieces on Cuban dance rhythms, most of which are two pages long and slightly easier than the Danzas Afro-Cubanas. Would be good for high school students who may need motivation. See especially La Mulata. (Marks.) Level 9.

entries.title: Little Things, Op. 9, No. 1
entries.description: Twenty-four very short pieces with thin textures for beginners. Some mild dissonances, and often humorous. The printed score is clean and well-spaced. Fine pedagogical writing for the early elementary student, which hopefully will make its way into the standard teaching repertoire. Printed in the same volume with Mixed Pages by the same composer. (Ricordi.) Level 2.

entries.title: Mixed Pages, Op. 9, No. 3a
entries.description: Eighteen excellent short pieces, with strong character. Most are one page long and follow Little Things in level of difficulty. No fingering. (Ricordi.) Levels 3-4.

entries.title: Kaleidoscopes (1959)
entries.description: Subtitled "10 Pieces for Students," found here are excellent contemporary selections for the talented intermediate student. Most are one page long, and a group of several programmed jointly is effective. The writing is energetic, dissonant and musical. Significant contemporary teaching literature by an American composer. See especially Nos. 2, 3, 5, 6, 7, 9 and 10. (Boosey.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: No. 3 (Risoluto)
entries.description: Brilliant writing, perhaps a good recital opener. The large chords call for a performer with a wide reach. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 9 (Alla marcia)
entries.description: Exciting work that makes full use of octaves, accents and changing meters. Good for the student who enjoys the rich sonorities of the piano. Performer needs accomplishment in octave playing. Level 8.

entries.title: Fantasia (1954)
entries.description: Striking, driving work, bold and dissonant. Pianistic writing, appropriate for students who play bravura writing well. (Boosey.) Level 10.

entries.title: Trois Preludes
entries.description: These works, while not profound, could serve as effective repertoire study for students wanting to play modern Impressionistic literature. The music is patterned, easy to read and pianistic. Each of the preludes is two pages long. The full chords provide resonant passages, for effective contrast with the more lyrical writing. (Musicales Transatlantiques.) Level 8.

entries.title: Sonatina No. 1
entries.description: Lyrical writing, often featuring repeated chords in the accompaniment. The movements may feel drawn out, and consequently require endurance. Three movements. (Lengnick.) Level 7.

entries.title: Sonatina No. 2
entries.description: Extended work permeated with dissonance that resolves. (Lengnick.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Household Pets, Op. 86
entries.description: Character pieces with titles such as Cat's Lament, Bird in Cage, Squeaky Guinea-Pig and Animal Heaven. Highly contemporary, expressive writing that requires a mature pianist. (Novello.) Level 8.

entries.title: Episodes
entries.description: Five contrasting pieces exhibiting jazz influence as well as strong rhythms and vibrant characters. Inherently lyrical writing. Should be better known. (Elkan-Vogel.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Alea, Music by Change
entries.description: Excellent collection of short elementary chance pieces using a variety of imaginative notational systems (many resemble graphs of geometric figures). The printed score is aesthetically appealing and is easy to interpret. An interesting and unique introduction to chance music for the elementary performer. Level 2.

entries.title: Intervals, Op. 37
entries.description: Outstanding contemporary writing for the upper-intermediate pianist. Creative and resourceful selections with some biting dissonance. Pianistic works built on different intervals from a second through an octave. (Leeds.) Level 8.

entries.title: Dance Piece
entries.description: Rhythms frequently alternate between 5/8, 6/8 and 7/8. Mild dissonances and bitonal writing. This would be an appropriate piece to familiarize a student with alternating meters. Requires a strong sense of rhythm. (Presser.) Level 8.

entries.title: 10 Pieces for Five Fingers
entries.description: Pieces for beginners featuring predictable phrase lengths and a tonal framework that is more conservative than in other works by this composer. Excellent writing for beginners who can coordinate the playing of both hands together. Level 1.

entries.title: Eight Preludes
entries.description: Dissonant writing with a variety of textures in the various one-page pieces. For the sophisticated musician who is interested in 20th-century music. (Merion.) Level 10.

entries.title: Folk Melodies (1945)
entries.description: Although conceived primarily as teaching pieces, these Folk Melodies have made their way into the concert repertoire. Fine native settings that deserve to be heard more often. (PWM.) Level 6.

entries.title: Invention (1968)
entries.description: Two voices with lyrical writing and shifting meter in almost every measure. Two pages. (PWM.) Level 7.

entries.title: Bucolics (1952)
entries.description: Evidences of folk music elements abound in these five pieces. Sparkling rhythms and melodic writing in a set that should be better known. Reminiscent in some ways of the Bartok Rumanian Folk Dances. (PWM.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Three Pieces for the Young (1953)
entries.description: Titled Four Finger Exercise, An Air and March, these pieces have strong pedagogical value. Appropriate for the sophisticated musician. (PWM.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: Afternoons and Afterward
entries.description: Set of contemporary character pieces. Solid writing. See especially Swans at Stratford and Forlane. (Novello.) Level 7.

entries.title: Five Bagatelles (1964)
entries.description: Serial writing especially appropriate for the sophisticated musician. (Elkin.) Level 10.

entries.title: Flower Pieces
entries.description: Mildly contemporary selections, one page or less in length, which describe flowers such as the Golden Marguerite, Butterfly Weed, Japanese Anemone and Japanese Pearly Everlastings. (Broude.) Level 3.

entries.title: Australia's Animals
entries.description: Pianistic and colorful writing in a sophisticated contemporary idiom for the elementary pianist. Highly recommended. Titles include Sleepy Koala, Wandering Wombat, Ponderous Platypus, Silver Swan, Capering Kangaroo and Elegant Emu. (G. Schirmer.) Level 2.

entries.title: Forecasts
entries.description: Four short pieces without meter and without key signatures. Clouds features clusters, while Feathery Fog is written in free notation. (G. Schirmer.) Levels 2-3.

entries.title: Images d'enfants
entries.description: Interesting contemporary writing in this short set of seven pieces. The music is filled with color and features biting harmonic language. See especially Le Chinois en porcelaine and Arlequin. (Durand.) Level 10.

entries.title: Martha's Vineyard
entries.description: A set of seven titled pieces with fascinating sounds and myriad contemporary approaches including the use of clusters. The pieces are generally one page long. Especially good for introducing contemporary trends to young students. (Presser.) Level 4.

entries.title: Petite Partita
entries.description: A six-movement suite in which the short movements display strongly contrasting characters. The writing is idiomatic to the piano. (Mills.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Preludio
entries.description: Scale figures in sixteenth notes abound. Etude-like, suitable for developing finger dexterity and strength. Level 6.

entries.title: Ninna-nanna
entries.description: This lyrical, one-page piece could be an upper-elementary student's first work in playing right-hand double notes, primarily double thirds. Atmospheric and expressive work which can be learned quickly. Requires attention to legato playing. Level 4.

entries.title: Carillon
entries.description: The entire piece is played in the treble register. Left-hand brokenchord accompaniment supports the high melody. Level 6.

entries.title: Scherzo
entries.description: Rapid pattern figures played in several registers provide the main theme for the concluding movement of the set. The performer will need a clean, well-articulated touch. Level 6.

entries.title: Colored Pictures
entries.description: Strong rhythms, biting dissonance, and highly contemporary writing based on non-functional harmonies. (PWM.) Level 7.

entries.title: Zoo Time
entries.description: These attractive rhythmic pieces each describe different animals in the zoo such as Two Baby Elephants, A Monkey lumping, A Fierce Lion and A Polar Bear. (PWM.) Level 8.

entries.title: Sonatina
entries.description: Linear writing and contemporary sounds in a three-movement work. See especially the last movement Vivace leggiero, with its finger patterns and witty demeanor. (PWM.) Level 9.

entries.title: Tema con variazioni
entries.description: Clear textures, biting dissonance, easy to read and features writing of high quality. (PWM.) Level 10.

entries.title: East European Folk Song Suite
entries.description: Nine elementary pieces from Slovakia, Austria, Hungary and Croatia. Straightforward writing and thin textures. (Southern Music.) Level 6.

entries.title: Puppets, Books 1, 2 and 3 (1912-1914)
entries.description: Delightful early-advanced teaching pieces that should be better known. Titles describe various aspects of a puppet's life or personality. Each movement portrays the puppet in a new characterization (such as The Shy Puppet or The Puppet's Dance). The writing is strongly tonal with some traces of chromatic progressions. Many of these will have strong student appeal. Book 1 is perhaps the best. (Masters Music, Supraphon.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: Columbine Dance. Valse
entries.description: This work needs to be played with a suave and fun-loving character. In ABA form, fits the hand well and is easily learned. Columbine was the sweetheart of Harlequin in Italian Renaissance popular comedy, which was often presented in pantomime. One of the most interesting and most amusing movements to play in all of the books. Level 8.

entries.title: The New Puppet. Shimmy
entries.description: Study in voicing double notes within a hand. Sarcastic character. Effective. Level 8.

entries.title: The Shy Puppet. Chanson
entries.description: Lyrical, chordal texture. Allegretto section adds vitality before the return to the Andante moderato. Level 8.

entries.title: Fairy-Tale
entries.description: Requires flair and suave phrasing. A student who plays Chopin well would also enjoy this selection. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: The Puppet's Dance
entries.description: Perhaps the most brilliant movement of the set, concluding with an especially effective Coda. This work fits the hand well, especially in the chromatic passages. This selection should be better known. Level 9.

entries.title: Spring in the Garden (1922)
entries.description: These pieces are similar to the Dello Joio Lyric Pieces for the Young in that they are longer than many intermediate teaching pieces (three to four pages), and contain strong character definitions. Some of Martini's better material for teaching. (Masters Music.) Levels 6-7.

entries.title: Fables (1924)
entries.description: Five extended character pieces, uneven in quality, for upper-intermediate students. See especially The Poor Rabbit, with its use of consecutive seconds; The Angry Bear, featuring percussive sounds on the piano; and The Monkeys, with its use of consecutive fourths. These pieces are appropriate for festivals or competitions. (Masters Music.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Film en Miniature (1925)
entries.description: Six titled pieces, from one to three pages, featuring interesting dissonances. (Masters Music.) Level 8.

entries.title: No. 1 Tango
entries.description: "Wrong-note" writing over a tango bass. Isolated double-note passages will need careful attention. Interesting and different. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 2 Scherzo
entries.description: Strong rhythms and biting dissonance. Needs sparkle and energy. Effective in performance. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 3 Berceuse
entries.description: Expressive writing in a contemporary idiom. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: No. 4 false
entries.description: Animated and humorous. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 5 Chanson
entries.description: The shortest work of the set, this effective selection with its economy of material could be learned quickly. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 6 Carillon
entries.description: Strong rhythms and a sing-song effect lend character and flair to this appealing movement. Level 8.

entries.title: Fenetre sur le Jardin (1938)
entries.description: Contemporary sounds are more pronounced than in the Martini Puppets but these pieces lack the character definition of the Puppets. (Leduc.) Level 8.

entries.title: Dumka (1941)
entries.description: Two-page piece based on quartal harmonies. (Max Eschig.) Level 8.

entries.title: Les Bouquinistes du quai Malaquais (1948)
entries.description: Strongly rhythmic, almost folk-like writing. Much of the writing is in unison at the octave. This work is especially appropriate for a student who wants a contemporary piece that requires energy and flair, but which is not technically demanding. This should be better known. (Heugel.) Level 8.

entries.title: Four Movements
entries.description: Four two-page pieces which are especially convincing performed as a set. Good recital fare. (Supraphon.) Level 10.

entries.title: 11 Cigno Blu (The Blue Swan)
entries.description: Designed for beginning piano study and co-authored with Carlo Panetta, this volume of pieces contains 12 four-measure exercises for reading and 50 short pieces. The hands play together throughout except in the preliminary short exercises, which are all written on the treble staff only and are to be played half with the right hand and half with the left. The rate of progression within the single volume is rapid. A strong emphasis is placed on developing contrapuntal playing skills (14 canons are included). Most of the pieces are in C Major and many finger numbers are included. This book reflects a dated approach to teaching beginners. (Berben.) Levels 1-2.

entries.title: Children's Album (1950)
entries.description: This set of pieces holds an important place in the Russian teaching literature. The works are mildly contemporary, highly pianistic and worth investigating. Titles include Fairy Tale, Dance, In the Garden and Morse Code. (G. Schirmer.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Toccata alla Danza
entries.description: Short, brilliant toccata requiring strong agile fingerwork and featuring driving rhythms. This highly pianistic work in a tonal framework is effective as an encore or program closer. Three minutes long. (Oxford.) Level 10.

entries.title: Kleine Geschichten, Op. 8 (Little Novelettes)
entries.description: The rhythms are somewhat complex for the technical level of these 18 Romantic character pieces. Intricate coordination between hands. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: No. I Toccatina
entries.description: Needs a performer with good fingers since it calls for clarity of articulation and evenness of passagework. Level 8.

entries.title: Trifles (Biriulki), Op. 28
entries.description: Many of these 26 character pieces are brilliant. The key progression in the set is from major to the relative minor. A significant teaching collection. See especially The Shepherd, Passing Fancy, Short Story, The Moth (Butterfly), In the Garden, The Music Box, Funeral March, Sailors' Song, Prelude and Fughetta and Echo in the Mountains. (Associated, PWM, Soviet State Publishing and others.) Levels 2-5.

entries.title: No. 1 In the Garden
entries.description: Built on rhythmic patterns, repeated tones and sparkling staccatos. Appropriate as an early-level chord study. Level 2.

entries.title: No. 2 The Orphan
entries.description: Expressive work primarily in the upper register of the piano. Triplet accompaniment in the left hand and lyrical right-hand melody. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 12 The Butterfly
entries.description: Programmatic work filled with patterns that can facilitate the learning. Levels 2-3.

entries.title: No. 13 The Music Box
entries.description: Uses very high register of the piano. The repetition of the theme, with variation in the accompaniment, renders this piece easy to learn. Levels 2-3.

entries.title: No. I The Turbulent Waters
entries.description: An impassioned, perpetual-motion work that needs an even technique for the sixteenth notes. Level 7.

entries.title: Pedal Preludes
entries.description: Twenty brief Romantic pieces composed for the development of pedal technique. Captivating and enchanting writing that should be better known. Highly patterned throughout and sound harder than they are. See especially Nos. 1, 4, 5, 11, 12, 17 and 19. (Summy-Birchard.) Level 6.

entries.title: Selected Miniatures from Op. 33
entries.description: Several are brilliant and demand agile fingerwork. (Soviet State Publishing.) Level 7.

entries.title: 18 Selected Pieces for Students, ed. Mirovitch
entries.description: Most of the pieces included here are from the collection Trifles, Op. 28, although works from other volumes also are included. Brief notes are presented for many of the pieces. (MCA.) Levels 6-7.

entries.title: Adventskalender
entries.description: Twenty-four pieces for Advent, with strong character definition. High quality. (Hansen.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Bagpipers
entries.description: Allegretto one-page piece. Melody with an ostinato accompaniment. Level 4.

entries.title: The Brook
entries.description: An energetic piece with many right-hand double-notes and twonote phrases. A light touch is needed. Level 4.

entries.title: The Stranger's Dance
entries.description: Featuring diatonic right-hand triads in root position and in inversions, this piece is a good study in chord playing, especially appropriate for the student with a strong hand and a good rhythmic sense. Various rhythmic complications. More difficult than it appears on the page. Level 5.

entries.title: Winter Wind
entries.description: Rapid, swirling sixteenths provide this piece with its distinctive rush of sound. Shifts of hand position and hand crossings also may need attention. Effective writing. Level 6.

entries.title: War Song
entries.description: Filled with rich sonorities and octaves. Sounds difficult. Effective writing. Level 6.

entries.title: Amahl and the Night Visitors
entries.description: Excerpts from Menotti's well-known opera, which he arranged for piano solo. Words are included. (G. Schirmer.) Levels 7-9.

entries.title: H. Regard de l'etoile (View of the Star)
entries.description: Based on the theme of the star and the cross and expressing the shock of grace. Levels 9-10.

entries.title: VII. Regard de la Croix (View of the Cross)
entries.description: Based on the theme of the star and the cross. Texture contains open octaves and four-note chords in both hands. Levels 9-10.

entries.title: IX. Regard du Temps (View of Time)
entries.description: Based on the mystery of the fullness of time. Primarily chordal writing in a moderate tempo. Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Les Animaux Magiques
entries.description: Clever teaching pieces for elementary students. The pieces concern animals and their escapades. The writing is tonal and pianistic. Titles include The cat's lullaby, The millepede who had sore feet, The elephant who wanted to be slim, The crocodile who had a tooth-ache and The giraffe with a bow-tie. (Lemoine.) Level 2.

entries.title: Le Chateau du Temps Perdu
entries.description: All of the pieces invoke the imagination of tales from a castle. Titles include Mists, The Guard's Hall, The Troubadour, Moon Reflection, Merry Jests and Nostalgia. Impressionistic and colorful sounds in the best of Meunier's pedagogical writing. (Lemoine.) Level 8.

entries.title: Four Little Fugues, Op. 43, 78
entries.description: Linear works in two voices that can provide excellent study in contrapuntal playing using Romantic sounds. Idiomatic to the keyboard. (Kalmus.) Level 7.

entries.title: Yellowed Pages, Op. 31
entries.description: Seven short pieces, mostly two pages long. Mildly contemporary writing with a dark Russian Romantic sound. Abundant use of the lower register of the piano. See especially No. 1 in E Minor and No. 2 in C Minor. (Soviet State Publishing.) Level 8.

entries.title: Sonata in F Major, Op. 84
entries.description: Appropriate for junior high or high school students, this pleasant sonata is tuneful and pianistic. Much of the writing in the first movement is chordal, but it fits a small hand well. The third movement is an energetic rondo in 6/8. (Kalmus, Peters.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: Seven Piano Pieces for Children
entries.description: Each two pages long. Although they are similar in character, style and inspiration to the Pinto Scenas Infantis, they are substantially easier. The strong rhythms and dance-like characters will appeal to many students. Titles include Sleep, Little Baby Doll, Little Japanese Toy and Country Dance. (Marks.) Level 6.

entries.title: Four Sonatinas
entries.description: Generally in two movements, primarily slow-fast. The writing is highly rhythmic and sometimes driving. Published separately. The performer needs a strong rhythmic sense and good sense of flair. (Ricordi.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Sonatine sur les touches blances
entries.description: Three short movements, Prelude, Berceuse and Fugue. Strong lyrical writing in a thin and mildly dissonant linear texture. Literature of high quality. (Leduc.) Level 5.

entries.title: Images from Count Berenguer IV of Catalonia's Court, Op. 39 (1982)
entries.description: Combines a mildly contemporary harmonic idiom with an Iberian flair. Representative titles from the 14 selections include Peronella, My Land! and Catalans, Let's Fight for Liberty. (International.) Level 8.

entries.title: Accueil Amical
entries.description: Seventeen very easy one-page contemporary teaching pieces that do not require a large hand. Wide variety of rhythmic values and wide range of the piano used in some of the selections. This collection should be better known. (Heugel.) Level 2.

entries.title: I. Dawn
entries.description: Requires a delicate touch, lyrical phrasing and refined shading. Octaves appear in the right hand. Level 4.

entries.title: II. Morning
entries.description: Broken-chord pattern in the left hand with some contrary motion playing and polymeters. An excellent study in phrasing. Level 4.

entries.title: III. Noon
entries.description: Requires expressive playing. Some double thirds. Level 3.

entries.title: IV. Afternoon
entries.description: Exciting and cheerful work requiring some virtuoso playing. Sixteenth-note figurations and short two-note slurs. Level 4.

entries.title: V. Twilight
entries.description: Lullaby feeling. Features polytonality combined with traditional sound. Level 3.

entries.title: Touches Blanches
entries.description: Simple melody and broken chord accompaniment, all on the white keys. Short phrases throughout in a short piece for the sophisticated student. Level 4.

entries.title: Touches Noires
entries.description: Played on the black keys. Appropriate for the musically sensitive student. Requires simple balance between the hands, syncopated pedaling, and shading of phrases. In the mood of Debussy's The Girl with the Flaxen Hair. Level 4.

entries.title: L'Album de Madame Bovary
entries.description: Set of 17 short character pieces, ranging from two lines to eight lines. Some of Milhaud's most tonal writing. (Enoch.) Level 6.

entries.title: The Household Muse (La Muse Menagere) Op. 245
entries.description: These 15 short pieces depict "household" activities such as The Awakening, Household Cares, Poetry, Cooking, Flowers in the House, Laundry, Music Together, The Son Who Paints, The Cat, Fortune Telling, Nursing the Sick, Reading at Night and Gratitude to the Muse. Restrained and lyrical writing with mildly dissonant sounds. Most are in ternary form and only one page long. Phrase lengths are often irregular. A work often referred to but seldom heard. Worth investigating. (Elkan-Vogel.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Four Romances Without Words (1933)
entries.description: Primarily lyrical and linear writing, perhaps best suited for the mature student with limited facility. Emotionally akin to the Mendelssohn Songs Without Words. The first and second are lyrical, the third is fast and playful and the fourth features frequent right-hand octave work. Highly syncopated, many large chords and numerous repeated eighth-notes impart the feeling of drive. The entire set encompasses four pages of music. (Salabert.) Level 8.

entries.title: The Joys of Life
entries.description: Set of six titled pieces composed in "Homage to Watteau" and taking its title from Watteau's painting "Les Charmes de la Vie." The set portrays scenes suggested by six of the artist's paintings. Titles of the pieces are Pastoral, The Indifferent, Rustic Pleasures, Serenade, Bagpipe and Masquerade. The writing is pleasant and accessible to the student able to play the easiest Chopin nocturnes. These pieces deserve to be heard more often. They would be especially appropriate as competition repertoire. (Belwin.) Level 9.

entries.title: Four Sketches
entries.description: See especially Alameda, which is given the indication "mouvement de habanera" and Sobre la Loma, "mouvement de rumba." The strong rhythms and dance-like flavor enhance their appeal to the earlyadvanced performer and their brevity makes them easily accessible. (Mercury.) Level 9.

entries.title: Le Printemps (1915-1920)
entries.description: Two volumes, each containing three untitled pieces that are lyrical, atmospheric and intriguing. These sophisticated pieces would be especially appropriate for a mature musician and would work well as part of a recital program. (Eschig.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: No. 5. Sad Birds
entries.description: Imitates the sounds of birds warbling. Harmonized with seventh, ninth and eleventh chords. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 8. Secret
entries.description: Monothematic work in AAA' form requiring melodic projection and a strong legato. Expansive accompaniment with wide stretches in the left hand. The key signature, C-sharp Major, makes the score appear difficult to read. Technical demands are minimal. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 9. Gypsy
entries.description: Close in some ways to the atmosphere of Albeniz and Granados. Mixolydian mode. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Pessebres (1914-16)
entries.description: The three pieces depict the rustic Catalan landscape. The first, Danza, is highly rhythmic with many modulations. The second, L'Ermita (The Hermitage), a lyrical intermediate "impressionistic-sounding" work, is in a moderate tempo. The final movement, El pastor (The Shepherd), is modal and contains some wide reaches. A folk-like rhythmic ostinato prevails. (Union Musical.) Level 8.

entries.title: Cris dans la rue
entries.description: Strongly rhythmic, effective in performance and seldom heard. Contains the notable indication midway through to "sing a little vulgarly." Level 9.

entries.title: Jeunes filles au jardin
entries.description: Highly Impressionistic writing in which the melody stems from a popular song in Barcelona. It has been conjectured that Mompou wanted to show that a cheap melody could become extremely refined. Some accompaniment chords span a ninth and a tenth. The second melody is extremely beautiful and also appears in Cris dans la rue. Level 9.

entries.title: Suburbis (1916-17)
entries.description: Contains five pieces which depict scenes from the outskirts of Barcelona. The first, titled The Street, The Guitar Player and The Old Horse, infuses Spanish melodies and brilliant passages in cadenza-like passages. See also the fifth piece, The Man from Ariston, which is a Spanish dance that contains some freely barred passages. (Salabert.) Level 10.

entries.title: Fetes Lointaines (Distant Festivals) (1920)
entries.description: Contains six untitled pieces. Most of them are dance-like Spanish music. Very few dynamic markings are provided. (Salabert.) Level 8.

entries.title: Three Variations (1921)
entries.description: Theme is stated simply (only half and quarter note values are used) and without accompaniment. Modest technical demands, strong contrast between the short variations. Especially appropriate for the older student who enjoys creating imaginative interpretations. (Eschig.) Level 7.

entries.title: Preludes 5-10 (193014)
entries.description: See especially Prelude No. 5, the most accessible of the preludes, with its slow lyrical introduction and animato dance-like postlude. Prelude No. 6, a moderato-cantabile espressivo work displaying an absence of barlines and composed for the left hand alone, would be appropriate for a creative performer. Prelude No. 9 is a thin-textured and highly lyrical work. It especially deserves note as a beautiful miniature of warm emotions and elegance. (Salabert.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: Paisajes (Landscapes) (1957)
entries.description: Set of beautiful character pieces. See especially I. The Fountain and the Bell which is a slow and hauntingly beautiful work. This could precede a student's study of the Chopin nocturnes. H. The Lake is a lyrical work with a sustained melody on top and broken chords divided between the hands throughout. It is highly programmatic in content and easy to read. The third and final piece is titled Carts of Galicia. (Salabert.) Level 7.

entries.title: Musica Callada, Books 1-4 (pub. 1959, 1962, 1966, 1974)
entries.description: Set of 28 pieces, mostly one or two pages long. These works are eminently accessible and would be fine choices for students wanting to play Impressionistic music but not yet ready for the piano works of Debussy. See especially Book 4, perhaps the best. (Salabert.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Cancion y danza No. 1 (1921)
entries.description: Perhaps Mompou's most famous piano piece. An earlier edition of this piece does not include barlines or time signatures. In the first edition the composer had indicated the words, "In the mist of darkness." (Union Musical.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Cancion y danza No. 2 (1924)
entries.description: The lento song is created from two-note lyrical figures. The texture of the dance is thin, and features primarily sustained chords in the left hand while the right hand plays the rhythmic dance passages. This is one of the easiest of the Mompou Songs and Dances. (Union Musical.) Level 6.

entries.title: Cancion y danza No. 3 (1926)
entries.description: More sophisticated musically than the Cancion y danza No. 2, this Song and Dance is unbarred, as is much of the piano music of Mompou. Noted for the dotted rhythms that lend a lilting effect. (Union Musical.) Level 8.

entries.title: Cancion y danza No. 4 (1928)
entries.description: More straightforward than the earlier Songs and Dances. The Song is based on a Spanish folk melody. Requires a strong rhythmic sense in both movements. Many right-hand double notes and chords. Students who play chords well would be attracted to this work. (Union Musical.) Level 8.

entries.title: Cancion y danza No. 8 (1946)
entries.description: Along with No. 2, this is one of the most accessible of the Songs and Dances. Consists of a lyrical Song portion, sentimental in character, followed by a two-page Dance featuring diatonic chords. Displays economy of material, fortissimo climax, and predictable phrasing. One of the most appealing of these works for teaching. (Salabert.) Level 7.

entries.title: Prelude
entries.description: Permeated with a rhythmic, vivacious melody supported by a staccato supporting bass. Reminiscent of American folk music. (Carl Fischer.) Level 10.

entries.title: Dancing School
entries.description: Humorous, flowing, enjoyable for the listener. Displays jumping bass in left hand, left-hand octaves, and changing meters. (Carl Fischer.) Level 10.

entries.title: Barn Dance
entries.description: Spirited writing that is primarily chordal and features jumping bass. Forthright and rhythmic writing. (Carl Fischer.) Level 10.

entries.title: Procession
entries.description: Chordal writing with more dissonance than other movements. Marked to be played "with steady menacing beat throughout." Double notes and full chords in the right hand appear. (Carl Fischer.) Level 10.

entries.title: No. 2 (Andante moderato)
entries.description: Expressive and mysterious. The angular melody needs careful shading. The feeling of two pulses per measure should be maintained. Level 5.

entries.title: No. 3 (Allegro molto)
entries.description: Spunky piece with strongly defined rhythmic patterns requiring fine coordination between the hands. Surprise ending with forte double thirds in contrary motion. One of the most attractive in this set. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 5 (Adagio)
entries.description: Haunting work that needs skill in voicing melody over chordal texture. Highly expressive writing. Level 5.

entries.title: No. 6 (Presto)
entries.description: Lively, dancelike work, especially good for students with a strong rhythmic sense. One of the most interesting pieces in the set due to the changing metric accents. Difficult ending. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 8 (Allegretto)
entries.description: Sprightly and folklike, with changing meters. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 9 (Allegretto)
entries.description: This 5/8 work is exciting and driving. Students with a strong sense of pulse will enjoy this assertive work. Appropriate perhaps for an older student who is not as advanced as his contemporaries but who needs a flashy piece. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 4 (Allegro moderato)
entries.description: Whimsical and lyric writing in 3/8 for the musically and rhythmically secure student. Sparse texture but sophisticated rhythms. Level 7.

entries.title: I. (Vivace)
entries.description: Short lively ABA work with Coda, needing tight rhythm and musical vitality. The leaps and the wide dynamic range used here make this movement sound difficult. Based on a triadic melody in right hand and left-hand staccato chords played in middle and upper registers. Features triads with added sixths, seventh chords and quartal sonorities.
