Forceful crescendo to the end. Needs crisp rhythm and some finger substitution. Level 10.

entries.title: II. (Lento)
entries.description: Expansive and nocturne-like work with wide left-hand leaps. The only truly slow prelude of the set. Hauntingly reflective work that requires deliberate control of rubato, matching of tones and the sustaining of a long melodic line. Angular right-hand melody that distorts the regular pulse. Mysterious. Level 9.

entries.title: III. (Allegro giocoso)
entries.description: Playful work requiring a crisp sound and fast fingerwork. Changes of meter and rhythmic patterns add interest. Features scale passages in the right hand against staccato eighths in the left hand. The close hand positions are not as difficult as they appear initially. Level 10.

entries.title: IV. (Allegretto meno mosso)
entries.description: Bright and toccata-like, with five-note clusters forming an almost constant tremolo in the right hand. Perpetual motion sixteenth-note chords alternating with single notes in the right hand against a lefthand syncopated melody and quick hand crossings. Level 10.

entries.title: V. (Moderato)
entries.description: More freely expressive than the other preludes, with portions of the work notated on three staves. Level 9.

entries.title: VI. (Allegro marcato)
entries.description: Needs stark intensity. Marcato writing within an ABA form. Low lefthand octaves add sonority. A student favorite. Level 10.

entries.title: A Summer Journal, Op. 19
entries.description: Suite of seven pieces concerning Muczynski's recollections of a warm summer's day in the West. (G. Schirmer.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: Morning Promenade
entries.description: Originally conceived for harpsichord. Lyric melodic passages in the first part with a chorale-like central section. Level 8.

entries.title: Park Scene
entries.description: Work in 7/8 featuring a left-hand ostinato and a right-hand isorhythmic pattern. Level 8.

entries.title: Midday
entries.description: An ABA blues. Levels 8-9.

entries.title: Birds
entries.description: An effective leggiero perpetual motion piece. The central motive contains repeated notes. Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Solitude
entries.description: Reflective work with an ambiguous harmonic structure and changing meters. Level 9.

entries.title: Night Rain
entries.description: Muczynski calls this "a Japanese watercolor." Ostinato in the left hand throughout, perhaps suggesting the sound of droplets falling after a night's shower. The music uses only three different pitches in the left hand. Level 10.

entries.title: Jubilee
entries.description: Exuberant work with almost constant running sixteenth notes. Level 10.

entries.title: Sonatina
entries.description: A three-movement work in a spirited and vivacious style. The first movement calls for large hands to avoid rolling the many tenths in the accompaniment at the opening. The writing is mildly dissonant. See especially the last movement. (Associated.) Level 9.

entries.title: Japanese Festival
entries.description: Set of 17 attractive teaching pieces with titles that appeal to children, such as Lively Children, Ballet by the Little Flower, The Speedy Car and so on. Selections are progressive in difficulty throughout the volume. In addition to movements annotated here, see Five Lively Children, Children's Song and Etude Moderato. (MCA.) Levels 4-6.

entries.title: Etude Allegro
entries.description: The most popular selection of the set. Bravura toccata that requires agile fingerwork and rapid alternation of the hands. Sounds more difficult than it is. Level 6.

entries.title: Suite for Piano "Time" (1952)
entries.description: Six-movement work. The composer titled it Time to express a progression from the Classicism in the Prelude and Harpsichord through Romanticism to Modernism. This work should be better known. Movements include I. Prelude, II. Harpsichord, III. Piano, IV. Etude, V. Romanticist and VI. Toyopet. Toyopet is the nickname of an automobile produced by Toyota in Japan. (Ogaku No Tomo Sha.) Level 8.

entries.title: Suite for Piano "Light and Shadow" (1957)
entries.description: Six-movement work, found in the same volume with the Suite for Piano "Time." First, third and fifth movements are tonal or modal while the second and fourth are 12-tone or atonal. The sixth features both tonal and atonal passages and the contrast between the two is indicated in the title "Light and Shadow." Titles of movements are I. Highlight, 11. A Story of Ocean, III. The Girl Playing the Koto, IV. Computer, V. Dirge and VI. Labor. Found in the second movement, A Story of Ocean, are fragmentary melodies from the Japanese national anthem and several naval marches. (Ongaku No Tomo Sha.) Level 8.

entries.title: Kaleidoscope for Young Pianists, Volumes 1 and 2
entries.description: These untitled 133 tiny pieces are mildly contemporary in idiom. Designed to introduce elementary pianists to contemporary sounds. Less dissonant than some of Bartok's teaching music and on the order of the Pal Kadosa 51 Piano Pieces. High-quality writing, easy to read, generally diatonic. Several selections would be effective as contest repertoire for a young student. (General Music.) Level 3.

entries.title: The Children's Delight, Op. 58, Volume 1
entries.description: Subtitled Easy Melodic and Instructive Pieces for Piano, these pieces are short upper-elementary character pieces for the young student. Twelve selections, traditional writing. (Kahnt.) Levels 2-3.

entries.title: The Children's Delight, Op. 58, Volume 2
entries.description: Continuation of the style of pieces in Volume 1. Slightly more difficult and slightly longer. See especially Chasing the Butterfly, Ride a Cock-horse to Banbury Cross, The Magic Little Bell and The Hunt's Up! Twelve selections. (Kahnt.) Levels 3-4.

entries.title: Table-Music, Op. 125
entries.description: Charming collection of neo-Romantic teaching pieces, most of them one page. Titles include Intrada, Pavane, Inventione Fugate, Allemande, Courante, Sarabande, Rigaudon and Gigue. (Kahnt.) Level 6.

entries.title: Children's Miniatures
entries.description: Sophisticated and imaginative writing. Strongly idiomatic for the piano. Might also appeal to adults. (PWM.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Tonadas, Volumes 1-4
entries.description: Four volumes of 48 short titled pieces (12 per volume). A tonada is a Spanish word meaning anything sung, played or danced. Rhythmic writing, mild dissonances, at the same approximate level as Turina Miniatures. The works are tonal and frequently use ostinatos containing tonal and modal variety. The performance of an entire volume by an advancing pianist would be interesting. They evoke the richness of Spanish folk music. These pieces might substitute for the teaching works of Granados, de Falla, Monpou or Turina. (Rongwen.) Level 8.

entries.title: 12 Cuban Dances
entries.description: Strong syncopations add to the appeal of these delightful short selections. The writing is mildly contemporary, and the dance flavor is pervasive. (Max Eschig.) Level 8.

entries.title: Hommage a Jelinek
entries.description: Five short, thin-textured works based on a tone row. Few technical difficulties inherent in the writing. Less complex than the Schoenberg Six Little Piano Pieces, Op. 19. The writing is technically accessible. Movement titles include Invention, English Waltz, Siciliano, Bolero and Epilog. (Moeck.) Level 8.

entries.title: Klavier-iibung
entries.description: Short pieces for beginners. The first pieces in the collection have moving notes in one hand and sustained notes in the other. No titles or tempo indications are provided although Nos. 22-40 contain metronome indications. (Schott.) Levels 1-2.

entries.title: A Tale from the Moon
entries.description: Wistful, languid melody is played by both hands two octaves apart. Level 4.

entries.title: The Organgrinder and the Monkey
entries.description: A fine example of the use of dissonance to create humor in music. Some bitonal writing in an appealing waltz-like work. Level 3.

entries.title: In Grandpapa's Big Rocker
entries.description: Dissonant chords are set in a rocking ostinato rhythm reflecting the character of the title. Level 4.

entries.title: The Funny Story
entries.description: Open fifths in the right hand are set against a swaying dissonant accompaniment. Level 3.

entries.title: An Arabian Fable
entries.description: Bitonal writing, ostinato accompaniment against a chromatic melody. Level 3.

entries.title: In the Swing
entries.description: Swaying accompaniment against linear, dissonant melody in the right hand. Level 4.

entries.title: The Sleeping Doll
entries.description: Legato melody over flowing left-hand accompaniment. Dissonant. Level 3.

entries.title: March of the Tin Soldiers
entries.description: Strong rhythmic motive. Level 3.

entries.title: Diez Piezas Simples, Op. 31
entries.description: Two volumes of intriguing character pieces from the South American school. (Barry.) Level 4.

entries.title: Four Pieces for Piano
entries.description: Interesting, mildly contemporary writing. These pieces would be appropriate for the sophisticated high school or early-level college pianist. Lullaby, March, Air, Waltz. (EMB.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Five Sketches from Finland, Op. 31
entries.description: Pleasant character pieces. Titles include Karelian Dance, Minuet, A Guilty Conscience, Waltz and Finlandish Dance. (Boston.) Level 7.

entries.title: May Night, Op. 27, No. 4
entries.description: Impressionistic writing requiring melodic projection in the left hand. Parallelism and rolled chords are present. Requires color in the playing. Level 8.

entries.title: Palmgren Album
entries.description: Source of some of Palmgren's best-known works including Prelude, Intermezzo, Waltz, Berceuse, Sarabande, The Sea and May Night. (Boston.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Six Miniature Studies, Volumes I and II
entries.description: Contemporary well-crafted etudes. One or several would provide fine repertoire choices for a contemporary music festival. (Boosey.) Level 10.

entries.title: Discoveries, Volumes 1-3
entries.description: Graded contemporary studies featuring a fascinating variety of influences. One piece is based on a 14th-century Alleluia, another on an ancient Chinese song, another on a Hindu raga, etc. The pieces are short and exhibit distinctively individual characters. (Peer International.) Levels 2-8.

entries.title: 27 Small Piano Pieces
entries.description: Mildly contemporary writing for early elementary students. Most of the short pieces remain in five-finger positions. Tuneful, with the underlying folk element always present. These pieces would be especially good for teaching independence of the hands and contrapuntal playing skills to an average elementary student. (EMB.) Levels 1-2.

See especially The Gold-Ball in the Well, which calls for left-hand notes to be silently depressed and held while the right-hand melody is played. Other notable selections include Rumpelstiltskin, The Dragon, The Frog and Rumpelstiltskin Spins Gold out of Straw. (EMB.) Levels 2-4.

entries.title: Six Bagatelles
entries.description: Highly contemporary and pointillistic writing. Accessible avant-garde writing. (Boosey.) Level 10.

entries.title: Portraits d'Enfants
entries.description: Twelve one-page pieces in a contemporary idiom. The writing is primarily linear and these pieces can help develop the independence of the hands. Mild dissonances. Bold writing. (Durand.) Levels 2-3.

entries.title: 10 Bagatelles, Op. 88
entries.description: Short, fresh pieces in varied styles. Dissonant, but tonal writing. Many are in neo-Romantic styles. (Peters.) Level 6.

entries.title: No. 1 Intrada
entries.description: Many nonfunctional triads in block position. Energetic, this piece requires a performer with a strong rhythmic sense. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 2 Menuet
entries.description: Graceful and one of the easiest of the set. Palatable contemporary sonorities in a work that is especially good for a student who plays classical articulations well. Rhythmically and musically straightforward. Level 5.

entries.title: No. 4 Tarantella
entries.description: Single notes and root position triads in the right hand. Repeated and patterned figures in the left hand. Appropriate for the student with fast fingers. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 5 Valse lente
entries.description: Neo-Romantic writing that fits the hand well and contains few lefthand skips. Rhythmically straightforward writing. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 9 Tango
entries.description: The catchy tango rhythm pervades the simple accompaniment in a modal work. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 10 Rondo
entries.description: Right-hand scale passages are played against a simple eighth-note accompaniment in the outer A sections. One of the most brilliant selections in the volume. Level 6.

entries.title: Music of Now, Books 1-3
entries.description: A piano method based on contemporary techniques, with highly organized presentation. Steps for practice and for learning pieces are given for many selections. Several ways of performing the various selections are given. The author has a strong concern for the development of a sensitivity to rhythm and line. Best used with a gifted or older piano student. Book I introduces intervals, rhythms and playing in relatively stationary hand positions. Book 2 features additional use of the black keys, dotted notes, tone clusters, more complex rhythms and meters. Fixed finger positions remain in this volume. Book 3 introduces some simple part playing, some double notes and triads. The hand positions shift slightly. (Waterloo.) Levels 1-4.

entries.title: Little Piano Book, Op. 60 (1953)
entries.description: These 14 short pieces are all dedicated to different students, and feature displaced harmonies, pandiatonicism and irregular phrase lengths. Requires hand extensions beyond a major sixth. Neither fingering nor pedaling is given. These works have become a classic in the standard teaching repertory and provide a good introduction to 20th-century sonorities and writing styles. Persichetti has said that these pieces may be performed separately or in groups. They were premiered by Persichetti's 10-year-old daughter Lauren in 1954. (Elkan-Vogel.) Levels 2-3.

entries.title: No. I Berceuse
entries.description: Lyrical writing with motivic interest and counter-melodies in the texture. Simple two-part accompaniment with many inner ledger lines. The nontraditional harmonies may provide a challenge. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 2 Capriccio
entries.description: Repeated staccato notes give this quick and spirited piece vitality. Bitonal writing with recurring rhythmic motive. Texture features sixteenth-note passages in one hand while the other is silent or moves in slower rhythmic values. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 3 Dialogue
entries.description: The charm is in the irregularity of the 5/4 measures (2+3). Melodic work appears in the right hand and then in the left, creating a dialogue between the two hands. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 4 Masque
entries.description: Lively presto work of 10 measures in which the hands remain in relatively stationary positions. Slurred melody in sixteenths over accompaniment of second and seventh intervals. Level 2.

entries.title: No. 5 Statement
entries.description: Lively, declamatory writing built from a right-hand two-measure motive. Level 2.

entries.title: No. 6 Arietta
entries.description: The absence of barlines allows free interpretation of this recitative. Florid right hand is totally independent from the left-hand thirds that descend through an octave. An appropriate piece to introduce a student to the concept of recitative. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 7 Humoreske
entries.description: Zestful writing with off-beat accompaniment. Bizarre harmonies and a memorable melodic motive. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 8 Fanfare
entries.description: Polychordal writing with many root-position triads. Marked allegro con spirito, this selection is one of the most vigorous and winsome of the set. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 9 Interlude
entries.description: Lyrical writing, adagietto tempo and featuring the upper register of the piano. Level 2.

entries.title: No. 10 Prologue
entries.description: Adagio pesante writing in blocked triads. Fortissimo, bichordal and dissonant. Much of the notation is in half notes. Level 2.

entries.title: No. 11 Canon
entries.description: Strict two-voice canon with the same material used in C, then in B, then again in C. Dissonant counterpoint. Level 2.

entries.title: No. 12 Epilogue
entries.description: Chordal writing with melody in soprano and an absence of rhythmic complexities. Level 2.

entries.title: No. 13 Fugue
entries.description: Strong detached subject in quarter and eighth notes. Bold, dissonant writing with few rhythmic intricacies. Dissonant. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 14 Gloria
entries.description: Primarily chordal writing in a work marked allegro risoluto. Joyful and cheerful in character. One of the most effective of the entire set. Level 3.

entries.title: Little Mirror Book, Op. 139 (1983)
entries.description: Studies in symmetrical inversion. The five elementary solos are written as mirror music so that the black and white key patterns reflect each other. Strong dissonances, simple rhythms. The titles are Magnifying Mirror (broken chords and triads), Makeup Mirror (contrast between staccato and legato), Magic Mirror (staccato and legato on broken chords), Mirror Lake (water image created through grace notes) and Rearview Mirror (some offbeat rhythms). See especially Rearview Mirror. (Elkan-Vogel.) Level 3.

entries.title: March
entries.description: Staccato passages and dotted rhythms appear in this rhythmic and spirited work. Ternary form with a G tonal center and some bichordal passages. Melody and blocked root-position triads in right hand above repeated (quasi-ostinato) left-hand thirds. Level 3.

entries.title: Canter
entries.description: Allegretto, song-like writing with continuous left-hand staccato eighths in the accompaniment. The performer needs to subtly inflect the melody. From G Lydian through various modes to a conclusion in A Ionian. Level 2.

entries.title: Pomp
entries.description: Filled with root-position blocked and broken triads in both hands. Bichordal writing in a work that is bold and rhythmic. Level 2.

entries.title: Four Arabesques, Op. 141 (1979)
entries.description: Highly pianistic writing, primarily linear. Dissonant harmonic framework. No. 1, featuring major sevenths, is perhaps the most accessible. It features frequent clef changes for the left hand and elegant broken chords. Nos. 2-4 feature some imitative, linear textures. All require attention to nuance and careful pedaling. (Elkan-Vogel.) Level 3.

entries.title: Serenade No. 2, Op. 2 (1929)
entries.description: Three short pieces, Tune, Strum, Pluck, that are witty and interesting. Tonal, although the composer uses all 12 tones freely with occasional unexpected chords. Tune features double notes with major sevenths in the accompaniment. Pluck includes striking syncopations and dynamic changes. Six pages. (Elkan-Vogel.) Level 7.

entries.title: Walk
entries.description: Melody switches between Phrygian and Dorian modes on A. Level 2.

entries.title: Play
entries.description: Features quickly alternating hands with strong accelerando and crescendo. Level 3.

entries.title: Sleep
entries.description: Slow chromatic descent with a slow trill in the left hand. Level 3.

entries.title: Variations for an Album, Op. 32
entries.description: Theme and five short variations in a lyrical and convincing work. Strong character definition between variations. Similar to some of the Kabalevsky variation sets. (Elkan-Vogel.) Level 7.

entries.title: Sonatinas Volume 1, Nos. 1-3, Op. 38, 45 and 47 (1950)
entries.description: Dissonant writing, with an underlying rhythmic vitality. No. 1 is in three movements. The complete sonatinas are published in two books of three sonatinas. Nos. 4-6 are easier than the first three. (Elkan-Vogel.) Level 7.

entries.title: Sonatina No. 1
entries.description: In three movements, Flowing, Quietly and Briskly. See especially the last movement, with the sudden dynamic changes and strong rhythmic interest. Level 8.

entries.title: Sonatina No. 2
entries.description: Composed in one movement, this is the most difficult of the three sonatinas. The opening features a canon in double notes. The middle section is humorous and fast, featuring jazz and polyharmonies. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Sonatina No. 3
entries.description: In two movements. The first features two-voice texture and is marked "warmly" and "singing." The second movement is filled with double notes. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Sonatinas Volume 2, Nos. 4-6, Op. 63-65 (1957)
entries.description: Melodies are primarily diatonic with moderate chromaticism and tonal harmonies that often move in parallel motion. Traditional rhythms and textures. (Elkan-Vogel.) Levels 3-4.

entries.title: Sonatina No. 4
entries.description: In three movements with five-finger melody alternating between the hands in the opening Allegretto. Finger substitution required in the second movement. The third movement features descending broken seventh chords. Levels 3-4.

entries.title: Sonatina No. 5
entries.description: In two movements, Poco adagio-Moderato and Vivace. See especially the second movement, which contrasts marcato and dolce. Folk-like melody in the second section, with chordal accompaniment. Level 3.

entries.title: Sonatina No. 6
entries.description: Five sections in this one-movement work with changing meters. Levels 3-4.

entries.title: Reflective Keyboard Studies, Op. 138 (1981)
entries.description: Exercises designed to develop both hands simultaneously by using mirror music. D and G-sharp are used as pivotal points, and the black and white keys are distributed equally, graphically reflected between the hands. Three sets of 48 contemporary studies. Preparatory studies for the Mirror Etudes. (Elkan-Vogel.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Unroll the flicker's rousing drum
entries.description: March-like, marcato writing with asymmetrical rhythms. Wide dynamic range. Level 9.

entries.title: Soft is the collied night
entries.description: Long melodic lines with a wide range of the keyboard used. Andante work with some wide stretches. Level 8.

entries.title: Gather for the festival bright weed and purple shell
entries.description: One of the most appealing, marked to be played brightly. Repetition of an interesting rhythmic figure. Level 9.

entries.title: Wake subtler dreams, and touch me nigh to tears
entries.description: Expressive one-page work calling for attention to voicing, phrasing and inflection. Level 8.

entries.title: Ravished lute, sing to her virgin ears
entries.description: Con mosso work calling for the playing of three-against-two and alternating meters. Level 9.

entries.title: Whose thin fraud I wink at privily
entries.description: Playful Allegro with some changing meters, but otherwise straightforward rhythms. Many double notes and tertian harmonies spiced with many accidentals. Needs quick fingers. One of the most appealing. Level 9.

entries.title: And warm winds spilled fragrance into her solitudes
entries.description: Leaping melody with left-hand descending chords. Level 8.

entries.title: To whose more clear than crystal voice the frost had joined a crystal spell
entries.description: Frequent meter changes. Widely spaced melodic line with thick chordal accompaniment. Level 8.

entries.title: Sleep weary mind: dream heart's desire
entries.description: Crossing hands with melody featured in both hands. Melody spans a wide range with an accompaniment comprised of thirds and sixths. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Dust in sunlight, and memory in comers
entries.description: Wide leaping melody with seventh chords throughout. Dynamic range from p,-ff. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Make me drunken with deep red torrents of joy
entries.description: One of the most successful. Features disjunct melody in sixteenth notes against eighth notes. Wide dynamic range. Level 9.

entries.title: Poems for Piano, Volume 3, Op. 14 (1981)
entries.description: Set of five works with long musical lines requiring nuance. Somewhat more difficult than the previous volumes. Technical and interpretative challenges. (Elkan-Vogel.) Level 10.

entries.title: Three Toccatinas, Op. 142 (1979)
entries.description: Neoclassic style is prevalent in this brilliant short group that requires agile fingers. Passagework divided between the hands. (Elkan-Vogel.) Level 10.

entries.title: Voyage in Space
entries.description: According to the composer, these 20 evocative contemporary works are intended "to introduce some of the freedoms of contemporary notation, expression and sound production to the adventurous and imaginative piano student." (Boosey.) Levels 6-8.

entries.title: Evocations, Volume 1
entries.description: Linear writing for the beginning student. Not particularly interesting. (Lemoine.) Level 2.

entries.title: Evocations, Volume 2
entries.description: Linear writing in this set of six teaching pieces. (Lemoine.) Levels 3-4.

entries.title: Six Pieces, Volume 2
entries.description: Titles of these primarily lyrical pieces include Etude I, Invention 1, Final I, Etude II, Invention II and Final II. More interesting than the Evocations. (Billaudot.) Level 4.

entries.title: No. 1 Prelude
entries.description: Simple two-voice writing. Level 2.

entries.title: No. 2 Minuet
entries.description: Two-voice writing, slightly more complex rhythms. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 3 Little March
entries.description: Infectious motive, strong march rhythms. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 4 Serenade
entries.description: Lyrical melody with sixteenth-note five-finger flourishes over a lefthand broken-chord accompaniment alternating between C Minor and D-flat Major. Level 4.

entries.title: Run, Run!
entries.description: An attractive and flamboyant piece in which the rapid alternation of triads between the hands creates a flurry of sonority. The B section, much slower than the brilliant and rapid opening, needs careful voicing and phrasing. The concluding glissando needs careful pacing. Level 7.

entries.title: Ring Around the Rosy
entries.description: An energetic and spirited bitonal work with a playful melody line. Left hand changes registers quickly. Levels 6-7.

entries.title: March, Little Soldier
entries.description: Features left-hand moving triads in the bass while the melody uses grace notes, appoggiaturas and glissandos. Should be heard more frequently. Levels 6-7.

entries.title: Sleeping Time
entries.description: A descriptive work with many suggestions provided by the composer. Level 6.

entries.title: Hobby Horse
entries.description: Sectionalized work with changing meter. Uses the entire keyboard. Requires wide stretches and fine coordination. Level 7.

entries.title: Marcha do Pequena Polegar (Tom Thumb's March)
entries.description: This is an appealing piece for a performer with flair. The many register changes require the ability to move freely about the keyboard. (G. Schirmer.) Level 9.

entries.title: Passacaglia
entries.description: Builds to a sonorous climax. Powerful contemporary writing in a fourpage piece. (Mercury.) Level 10.

entries.title: Studies on an English Dance Tune
entries.description: Six mildly contemporary movements on the first section of "Jenny Plucks Pears." Rhythmic and inviting writing that is palatable and patterned. The entire set would be effective in performance. Worth investigating. (Elkin.) Level 9.

entries.title: 20 Easy Pieces
entries.description: Short tonal pieces, often slightly shorter than a page. Highly rhythmic and tuneful, many are based on Indian folk dances or songs. (Peer International.) Levels 2-4.

entries.title: Four Variations on a Mexican Theme
entries.description: Interesting writing that is pianistic and Romantic in sound. (Peer International.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Four Mexican Dances
entries.description: Generally animated and strongly rhythmic. Pianistic and accessible to the advancing performer. The dances are approximately two pages each. (Southern Music.) Level 10.

entries.title: Fleas
entries.description: Alternation of melody pitches between the hands, with jumps divided between the hands. Interesting. (Frederick Harris.) Level 2.

entries.title: Six Petites Pieces Recreatives
entries.description: Enticing writing in a strong rhythmic framework. Requires strong depiction of character. These mildly contemporary pieces should be better known. Titles include Joyeux Depart, Idylle, Soleil Printainer, Berceuse, Valse Lente and Parade. (Max Eschig.) Level 3.

entries.title: No. 3 On the Spanish Border
entries.description: Spirited rhythms lend an air of festivity. This piece would be effective on a contest list. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 4 From Omsk to Tomsk
entries.description: Strong, humorous writing with biting chords. Allegro violento with fortissimo cluster chords. Meter changes. Level 3.

entries.title: Mouvements Perpetuels (1918)
entries.description: A deservedly popular work that needs strong character differentiation between the movements. The first movement is the best known, featuring large skips in the melody and mild dissonances. The left-hand accompaniment is ostinato-like. The main idea of this movement is derived from the fifth piece in Beethoven's song cycle An die ferne Geliebte, Op. 98. The second movement is short and lyrical, comprised of many motives. The vigorous final movement is spirited, with tenths in the accompaniment. (Chester.) Level 9.

entries.title: Valse (1919)
entries.description: Appealing three-page work that can provide a fine introduction to Poulenc's style. Light, appealing and rhythmic. Waltz accompaniment. (Eschig.) Level 8.

entries.title: Suite in C (1920)
entries.description: Three short, contrasting and energetic movements, Presto, Andante, Vif. Appealing writing. Should be heard more often. (Chester.) Level 9.

entries.title: Pastourelle (1927)
entries.description: A lyrical transcription from the ballet L'Eventail de Jeanne. A folk-song-like quality pervades. (Heugel.) Level 8.

entries.title: Three Novelettes (1927-1959)
entries.description: Interesting and attractive set. The lyrical first piece is by far the most popular, displaying a pastoral quality. Students enjoying lush Romantic sonorities would be drawn to this work. The second movement is brilliant, filled with biting dissonances and surprising cadences. It is the most difficult of the three movements. The final movement was written 32 years later than the other movements and is lyrical, filled with pungent harmonies and widely spaced left-hand arpeggiations. (Chester.) Level 10.

entries.title: Valse Tyrolienne
entries.description: Lively and melodic waltz. The wide left-hand jump bass can present problems. Level 7.

entries.title: Staccato
entries.description: Sharp staccato chords requiring a loose wrist. Lively work in a march-like tempo. Level 7.

entries.title: Rustique
entries.description: The tempo makes this more difficult than expected. A haunting melody permeates. Level 7.

entries.title: Polka
entries.description: Fast repeated notes in a bouncy and appealing movement. Level 7.

entries.title: Petite Ronde
entries.description: Although this is the simplest technically of the set, it is in a way the most difficult musically. The many repetitions in this rhythmic work call for variety in interpretation. Level 6.

entries.title: Coda
entries.description: Presents material from the earlier three movements in a kind of a summation of the suite. Another spirited and effective movement. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Badinage (1934)
entries.description: Playful writing. Spans a ninth and requires a fine legato. (Salabert.) Level 9.

entries.title: Suite Francaise (1935)
entries.description: Reflection of the idiom of French Renaissance composer Claude Gervaise is seen through Poulenc's use of modal cadences and progressions, 16thcentury dance rhythms and Renaissance dance forms. Pentatonic writing and dissonant harmonies. (Durand.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Bransle de Bourgogne
entries.description: Appealing repeated cadential figures in a lively and dissonant work. Highly rhythmic. Level 10.

entries.title: Pavane
entries.description: Simple chordal work, consisting predominantly of blocked chords. Sophisticated writing. Level 7.

entries.title: Petite marche militaire
entries.description: Primarily chordal. Repeated rhythmic figures. Level 8.

entries.title: Complainte
entries.description: Calm and melancholy writing in a simple texture. Level 8.

entries.title: Bransle de Champagne
entries.description: Sing-song effect with appealing repeated rhythms, especially appropriate for the student with larger hands. Level 10.

entries.title: Sicilienne
entries.description: Elegant work, primarily chordal. Performer will need an acute sense of a long line. Level 8.

entries.title: Carillon
entries.description: Bright and energetic chordal work. Level 9.

entries.title: Improvisations
entries.description: Nos. 2 and 12 are the most accessible and are both influenced by Schubert: No. 2 begins like a Schubert waltz, and No. 12 is a waltz subtitled "Hommage a Schubert." No. 12 is especially brilliant. (Salabert.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Album of Six Pieces for Piano
entries.description: Contains such works as Mouvement Perpetuel No. 1, Presto from Suite in C, Impromptu No. 3, Novelette No. I in C and others. (Chester.) Levels 8-10.

entries.title: Matthew's Piano Book
entries.description: Subtitled "10 Piano Pieces for Advanced Students," these are somewhat abstract character pieces in a mildly contemporary idiom. (Hansen/ Chester.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: No. I Good Morning
entries.description: A one-page waltz featuring "wrong-note" writing. Wide melodic range. Cheerful and pianistic. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 2 Polar Bear Dance
entries.description: Bitonal thirds are heard from register to register in a humorous work that is a vivid depiction of the title. A wide range of keyboard is used. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 3 Promenade in the Park
entries.description: Graceful lyrical writing. 5/4 meter. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 4 Poodles
entries.description: Scale passages and alternation of motives between the hands. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 6 Parade of the Penguins
entries.description: Changing meters add interest and unpredictability. Repeated notes and two-part writing in a work that requires finger independence. Level 5.

entries.title: No. 7 Trees at Twilight
entries.description: Simple and lyrical writing that is based on seventh and ninth chords. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 8 Mechanical Toy
entries.description: Simple but syncopated right-hand melody against constant lefthand accompaniment that outlines open fifths. Engaging writing. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 9 Procession with Lanterns
entries.description: Dotted rhythms permeate the texture and offer stateliness. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 10 In Perpetual Motion
entries.description: Brilliant and effective work that needs good fingers, but sounds more difficult than it is. Based on triads and scales and written primarily in sixteenth notes. Should be better known. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 11 Roundup
entries.description: Energetic work with a syncopated melody and accompaniment that give it a "western" flair. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 12 A Piece of Lace
entries.description: Melodic writing with double notes in the right hand. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 13 The Out-of-Tune Band
entries.description: Effective and humorous work with "wrong-note" writing. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 15 Miniature March
entries.description: Bold writing that calls for the playing of octaves, strong depiction of the march theme and flair. Level 5.

entries.title: No. 17 Mimicry
entries.description: Needs agile fingers. Level 5.

entries.title: No. 20 Scherzo
entries.description: Spirited boisterous writing with skips of a ninth and tenth. Level 5.

entries.title: No. 1 Morning
entries.description: A dreamy, impressionistic study using a wide range of sonorities on the piano. Deservedly popular as a study in color. Appropriate for a student drawn to the sound of Debussy. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 2 Promenade
entries.description: Primarily chordal textures. Contains "wrong-note" writing and melodic passages with many unusual turns. Requires the ability to inflect short motifs. More difficult than it appears on the page. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 3 A Little Story
entries.description: Less effective than most of the other pieces in the set, perhaps due to the awkward rhythmic framework. Levels 6-7.

entries.title: No. 4 Tarantella
entries.description: A fine finger study that is highly effective in performance. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 5 Regrets
entries.description: A true Impressionistic study, especially appropriate for the sensitive and musical performer. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 6 Waltz
entries.description: Highly effective in performance and strongly typical of Prokofiev's style. The displacement of the melody between various octaves produces humor. For the sophisticated musician who can move with agility on the keyboard. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 7 March of the Grasshoppers
entries.description: Popular and effective due to the graphic depiction of the title. Represented in the music are the hopping movements of a grasshopper as well as a kind of promenade. Since this work moves rapidly around the keyboard, it is best performed by an individual with a strong kinesthetic feeling. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 8 The Rain and the Rainbow
entries.description: Another atmospheric and Impressionistic work, shorter than most of the other pieces in this collection. Uses the entire keyboard and requires the reading of ledger lines. Many clef changes and hand crossings. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 9 Playing Tag
entries.description: A vigorous finger study that dances up and down the keyboard. Requires agile fingerwork and a strong rhythmic sense. Less known. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 10 March
entries.description: Most immediately familiar work in the set. Somewhat more difficult than it appears on the page due to the rapid crossing of hands, shifting of registers for the octave displacements and the detailed attention required for the articulation. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 11 Evening
entries.description: A hauntingly beautiful work. Requires a fine sense of the flexibility of a line and a good legato. Tender, lyrical writing in a highly expressive and sophisticated work. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 12 The Moon Strolls in the Meadows
entries.description: Less familiar than many of the selections in this collection. The andantino melody is exchanged between the hands. Requires agile movement about the keyboard. Primarily lyrical in nature. Level 7.

entries.title: Sonatina Pastorale, Op. 59, No. 3 (1934)
entries.description: A sonatina in one movement with a "musette" bass. Strict form with a Lydian melody. Moderate tempo, primarily lyrical writing. (Boosey, Kalmus and others.) Level 8.

entries.title: No. 1 Moderato
entries.description: Expansive hand crossings and sophisticated pedaling are required. Somewhat involved harmonic framework. One of the best in the set. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 4 Sostenuto
entries.description: Complex dotted-rhythm ostinato over a left-hand melody. Harmonically static. Level 9.

entries.title: No. I Dance
entries.description: "Wrong-note" writing in a work with crisp staccato and leaps. Easier than it sounds. This piece journeys through several unlikely keys with an organized rhythmic pattern that permeates the entire work. The opening is rhythmically similar to Debussy's Minstrels. Level 10.

entries.title: No. 2 Minuet
entries.description: Two-page ABA' form with off-beat rhythms. Levels 8-9.

entries.title: No. 3 Gavotte
entries.description: The best known work in this set with a wry, march-like feeling. Constructed harmonically almost completely with triads. The trio is primarily in two voices with each of the voices doubled at the octave. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 2 Gavotte
entries.description: Charming and intriguing writing. The piece calls for a large lefthand span. Use of chromatic scale is frequent here. Level 10.

entries.title: No. 4 Waltz
entries.description: More Impressionistic than neo-Classic. It is a slow waltz that abounds in augmented-sixth chords. Level 10.

entries.title: No. 6 Legende
entries.description: Scriabinesque harmonies in a lyrical work that requires mature interpretative skills. Essentially a dramatic recitative, and especially suited for the person with an affinity toward sensitive lyrical writing. Dynamic range drops to ppp. Many parallel fifths. Levels 9-10.

entries.title: No. 7 Prelude
entries.description: This perpetual-motion work, filled with right-hand broken chords, is somewhat reminiscent of the Chopin "Harp" Etude. Composed by Prokofiev for harp or piano. Contains effective glissandi passages. Level 10.

entries.title: Prokofiev: Selected Works, ed. Baylor
entries.description: Contains an interesting cross section of works by Prokofiev including Tales of the Old Grandmother, Op. 31, Pastoral Sonatina, Op. 59, No. 3, Episodes from Romeo and Juliet, Op. 75 and Prelude, Op. 12, No. 7. (Alfred.) Levels 8-10.

entries.title: Toccata
entries.description: Energetic 14-page work, for the talented upper-intermediate student. Driving rhythms, large repeated fortissimo chords, and some glissandi all help create excitement. Highly pianistic and effective in performance. (Associated.) Level 8.

entries.title: Prelude in G Minor, Op. 23, No. 5
entries.description: Ternary-form work that suggests the sound of a military march in the opening and closing sections. The central section contains a broad lyrical melody and expansive arpeggiations. This piece is more difficult than it initially appears. Vigorous and aggressive writing with wide jumps. Note the countermelodies throughout the central section. Level 10.

entries.title: Prelude in G Major, Op. 32, No. 5
entries.description: Serene and elegant work that focuses on the upper register of the piano. A murmuring accompaniment is heard against a contemplative and lyrical melody that needs careful dynamic control. Performance of this work requires rhythmic flexibility. Left-hand quintuplets permeate the languid texture. Level 10.

entries.title: Prelude in G-sharp Minor, Op. 32, No. 12
entries.description: The continuous obligato should remind the listener of bells. Contains a melody in the baritone register that needs careful voicing and shaping. For the mature performer. Level 10.

entries.title: No. I Elegie
entries.description: Rhapsodic work constructed on a passionate theme. Uses dark, rich sonorities and a wide expressive range. Level 10.

entries.title: No. 2 Prelude in C-sharp Minor
entries.description: Rachmaninoff was motivated to write this immensely popular prelude by the thought of the profit of forty rubles (about $20 at that time)! Requires skilled control of sonority. Rachmaninoff said that he did not have a descriptive mood in mind when he wrote this work. The music is melancholy, with an impassioned central section and a grand climax. Avoid achieving loudness without resonance. Level 10.

entries.title: No. 3 Melodie
entries.description: Contains a cello-like melody, typical of Rachmaninoff, presented successively in four voices. This work was later revised into a much more elaborate setting. Levels 9-10.

entries.title: No. 4 Polichinelle
entries.description: Represents a puppet's dance, full of humor and irony. The name refers to Punch, a character in the Punch and Judy puppet shows. An ABA' work that is more difficult than many realize. Brilliant writing with wide leaps and thick octave chords. Dramatic with varying textures and many ideas. Makes a large splash. Level 10.

entries.title: No. 5 Serenade
entries.description: Opens with a statement of the theme and four bars of introductory material. Seems to depict the atmosphere of Spain (perhaps a love song sung to guitar accompaniment). Level 10.

entries.title: Salon Pieces, Op. 10
entries.description: See especially No. 1, Nocturne; No. 2, Valse; and No. 5, Humoresque. (Many standard editions.) Level 10.

entries.title: No. 2 Valse
entries.description: Beautiful writing in a work with a lengthy Viennese theme and lush suspensions. Level 10.

entries.title: No. 5 Humoresque
entries.description: A delightful work with abundant jesting. The dotted rhythms and sudden loud chords make it inviting and unusual. Its opening theme echoes the First Symphony, and the wit and light-hearted character found here are unusual for Rachmaninoff. Level 10.

entries.title: Two Fantasy Pieces
entries.description: Published in 1951 without opus number. The first is a passionate work with rapid right-hand passagework. The second piece dates from the early 1890s and probably was dropped from Op. 3. (Hinshaw.) Level 10.

entries.title: Fantasy Piece No. 1
entries.description: A two-page selection with rapid passagework in sixteenths found usually in the right hand over a rocking accompaniment in 6/8. Level 10.

entries.title: Fantasy Piece No. 2
entries.description: Marked moderato, this work is a 12/8 study with continuous eighth notes in both hands. Requires careful attention to fingering. Not Rachmaninoff's most profound writing. Unlike its companion, this work was left untitled by the composer. Level 9.

entries.title: Three Nocturnes
entries.description: The first contains elements of real dramatic flair. Nocturne No. 3 can be seen as a forerunner to the Prelude in C-sharp Minor. (Hal Leonard.) Level 9.

entries.title: Four Improvisations
entries.description: These pieces are based on melodic fragments of Arensky, Glazunov, Taneyev and Rachmaninoff, respectively. This is some of Rachmaninoff's most accessible writing. Each of the improvisations is one page in length. (Hinshaw.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Rachmaninoff Album of Piano Works, ed. Hinson
entries.description: Contains the Four Improvisations. Interesting choices for alternative literature. (Hinshaw.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: Rachmaninoff Album for the Piano
entries.description: For many years, this was one of the most popular anthologies of Rachmaninoff's works due to the presence of the Op. 3 pieces such as the Polichinelle, Elegie and Melodie. An arrangement of the third movement of the Second Piano Concerto is also included here. (G. Schirmer.) Level 10.

entries.title: From Morning to Evening
entries.description: This collection contains 24 pieces in all keys with widely varying levels of difficulty represented. Mildly contemporary writing. See especially No. I Morning, No. 5 Promenade, No. 13 Swallow, No. 19 Trumpeters. The first piece in the collection is titled Morning and the last, Evening. (Associated.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: Six Tiny Sonatas
entries.description: Short works in three or four movements each characterized by sparse textures and appealing contemporary sounds. Especially appropriate for talented young elementary-school students. Highly pianistic. (General Music.) Level 3.

entries.title: 12 Indiscretions
entries.description: Woven into these short character pieces are themes from well-known classical melodies such as the main theme of the Chopin Polonaise in A Major and the habanera theme from Carmen. These pieces might be used well with an adult who likes light music and has a well-based knowledge of classical music. (General Music.) Level 8.

entries.title: Seven Mobiles
entries.description: Dissonant, rhythmic selections especially appropriate for the contemporary enthusiast or the sophisticated performer. Excellent writing. (General Music.) Level 9.

entries.title: American Album
entries.description: A fine collection of "Americana" in eight mildly contemporary solos. This collection is somewhat reflective of the Barber Excursions, but is less advanced. It is a collection that surely should be better known. See especially Square Dance, Harmonica and Jitterbug. (Mercury.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Prelude (1913)
entries.description: Fine introduction to Impressionistic style in one of Ravel's most accessible works. It was composed as a sight-reading piece for the Paris Conservatory competitions. Contains some unexpected harmonies, hand crossings and interlocking passages, and can work well as a bridge between intermediate and advanced literature. (Alfred, Durand and others.) Level 8.
The second page becomes much more complex than the opening. Thick textures. Not Ravel's best writing. (Durand.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Pavane pour une Infante defunte (1899) (Pavane for a Dead Princess)
entries.description: This was the first of Ravel's works to achieve real popularity. Ravel told the Princess Polignac, to whom it was dedicated, that it ". . . is not the funeral mourning for a girl who has just died, but the evocation of a pavane which could have been danced by a small princess in days of old, at the court of Spain." The accompaniment resembles a lute at times. Ravel preferred that it be played calmly and without too much passion or sentimental rubato. It is in a rondo form. (Alfred and others.) Level 10.

entries.title: At the Piano With Ravel, ed. Hinson
entries.description: Excellent foreword and notes on the individual pieces precede the musical text. Works included are the Menuet Antique, Pavane for a Dead Princess, Jeux d'eau, Sonatine, Menuet on the Name of Haydn, Sad Birds and The Valley of the Bells. Some of these works are quite difficult. (Alfred.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Ravel: Selected Favorites, ed. Hinson
entries.description: Contains many less well-known pieces of Ravel as well as the more commonly heard works. Includes In the Style of Borodin-Valse, In the Style of Chabrier and several of the Noble and Sentimental Waltzes. (Alfred.) Level 10.

entries.title: The Christmas Gifts
entries.description: Significant collection of 14 short elementary pieces. The sounds are often Romantic and the writing fits the hand well. These pieces are filled with strong characterization and are probably Rebikov's easiest pieces for students. They are only slightly greater in difficulty than the Kabalevsky Music for Children, Op. 39. See especially No. 1 Children Gather Around the Christmas Tree and No. 4 The Bear. (Beiwin.) Levels 2-3.

entries.title: Pictures for Children, Op. 37
entries.description: Seven Impressionistic character pieces. See especially: Preparing the Lesson, which is a take off on someone practicing the first movement of the Mozart Sonata in C Major, K. 545; Promenade of the Gnomes, which depicts two gnomes taking a walk and meeting a frog; and A Joyous Moment. These brief works should be better known. (International.) Level 4.

entries.title: Silhouettes, Op. 31
entries.description: Subtitled "Nine Pictures from Childhood," this opus provides a kind of children's album in a mildly contemporary vein. (Alfred, International and others.) Levels 4-6.

entries.title: No. 4 Playing Soldiers
entries.description: Ostinato right-hand melody of two measures gives this motivating one-page solo with mild dissonance its martial character. The hands play in close proximity. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 7 Rocking
entries.description: Impressionistic writing for the expressive intermediate student. Four layers of sound need voicing. Cantabile melody in the soprano. Level 6.

entries.title: Pieces for Piano
entries.description: Miscellaneous and accessible teaching pieces from Rebikov's collections Opp. 2, 5, 8, 9, 21, 28 and 29. Most of these pieces are unknown, but many are interesting. (Soviet State Publishing.) Levels 6-7.

entries.title: Rebikov: Miscellaneous Short Pieces, ed. Johnson
entries.description: Provides an overview of Rebikov's extensive output and a source for some of his best teaching pieces. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music.) Levels 3-6.

entries.title: Portraits
entries.description: Set of eight pieces composed for various friends who are only identified by their initials. Contemporary harmonies, thin textures, readable score. Brief sketches, similar to those by Virgil Thompson and Leonard Bernstein except that these selections are more accessible. (Weinberger.) Level 8.

entries.title: No. I The Augmented Triad
entries.description: Based on the B-flat augmented triad and its inversions. Complex rhythms, strong dissonance and frequent imitation between the hands. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 2 The Major Second
entries.description: Seconds alternate between hands (almost in toccata style) within this one-page piece that sounds more difficult than it is. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 3 The Tritone
entries.description: A patterned figuration is the basis of the progressions. No key signature. Fits the hand well. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 4 The 12 Tones
entries.description: Lyrical one-page work featuring simple use of the 12-tone scale. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 5 Shifted Rhythm
entries.description: Built on the same row as in No. 4 but in original and inverted forms. A rhythmic pattern shifts position in later measures. Primarily linear writing and angular melodies in a two-page piece. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 6 12 Upside Down
entries.description: The tone series used in earlier pieces is inverted. Features melodic writing over an ostinato-like accompaniment. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 7 Seven Times Seven
entries.description: Patterned figurations fit the hand well in this 7/4 selection. The central recitative gives variety from the vivo perpetual motion. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 8 Chromatics
entries.description: Linear, chromatic writing with one line in each hand. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 9 Dissonant Counterpoint
entries.description: Begins as a two-voice fugue. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 10 Tone Clusters
entries.description: Probably the most immediately appealing and tuneful of the set. Strong rhythms, syncopated and frequent use of fourths. Central recitative needs careful timing and pacing. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 11 Polytonality
entries.description: Highly dissonant work that alternates between B Major and E-flat Major. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 12 Fourths and Fifths
entries.description: Toccata-like alternation between hands, with black notes played by the left hand and white notes by the right. Dissonant writing. Level 8.

entries.title: Petite Etude
entries.description: Polytonal writing with the left hand playing on the black keys and the right hand on the white keys. Rapidly alternating patterns of eighths in each hand. Three pages. (Merion.) Level 7.

entries.title: Six Short Pieces
entries.description: Neo-Classical tendencies within a mildly contemporary idiom are portrayed in these pieces. The music is alternately rhythmic and lyrical, holding possibilities especially for the performer who has a strong tendency toward playing linear music. Titles are Preludio, Invenzione, Elegia, Momento Musica, Barcarola and Saltarello. (General Music.) Level 8.

entries.title: Five Pieces for Young Pianists
entries.description: See especially the Silly Polka, with its "wrong-note" writing and its carnival-like atmosphere. (General Music.) Level 7.

entries.title: Chironomos
entries.description: Short character pieces with strong character delineation. Primarily linear writing in a dissonant idiom. Titles include Preludio, Allegro Volante, Intermezzo, Mazurka, Improviso and Epilogo. (General Music.) Level 9.

entries.title: 12 Preludes
entries.description: Thin-textured, accessible works. Many of the preludes are dance-like, and rhythmic buoyancy is inherent in Rieti's style. Phrase lengths are often symmetrical. See especially Nos. 2, 7 (Prokofiev-like), 10 (a witty and humorous work) and 11 (a brilliant toccata requiring some bravura). (General Music.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Contrasts (1967)
entries.description: Five titled movements in a mildly contemporary idiom. Movements are Preludio, Variazioni, Bagatella, Elegia and Girandola. Perhaps his most popular collection. (General Music.) Level 9.

entries.title: Arioso
entries.description: A two-page solo written in three voices with the middle voice alternating between the hands. It is built around G as a tonal center using a Phrygian mode with the lowered second step. Rather esoteric writing. (Presser.) Level 8.

entries.title: Sonada de Adios
entries.description: Five-page lyrical work marked Andante sostenuto and featuring a dramatic climax. Appropriate for the student playing Chopin nocturnes. Mournful writing calling for a fine concept of rich sonority. (Eschig.) Level 10.

entries.title: A Quiet Afternoon (1948)
entries.description: Set of nine short character pieces. Sensitive, lyrical writing in a simple and communicative work perhaps best performed by more mature performers. Key signatures through five sharps and three flats. See especially No. 8 A Trick. (Peer International.) Levels 6-7.

entries.title: Barcarolles (1949)
entries.description: Three movements ("Romances sans paroles") in 6/8 and dedicated to American pianist Leon Fleisher. Rorem states that they were written in Morocco in 1949 during a very wet season and that he was playing a great deal of Chopin's 6/8 music at the time. (Peters.) Level 10.

entries.title: Six Shades of Blue. Preludes for Piano
entries.description: Written to be a set of piano works similar to the Gershwin Preludes. Blues character is maintained throughout the six preludes. According to the composer in the preface: "the first and fourth are 'cool,' while the third and fifth are 'hot.' The second is 'slick' and the last is 'down."' (Boosey.) Level 9.

entries.title: Elves and Fairies
entries.description: Fourteen titled pieces in five-finger positions with complex coordination required between the hands despite the lack of movement about the keyboard. Dated writing without profundity. (Peters.) Levels 1-2.

entries.title: Five Miniature Preludes and Fugues
entries.description: Probably Rowley's most important and useful teaching collection. The highly patterned selections fit the hand well, making them especially appropriate for students with small hands. Each prelude features a single figuration throughout such as broken-chord arpeggiation. Easy, twovoice fugues. (Chester.) Level 2.

entries.title: From My Sketch Book, Op. 39
entries.description: Ten short pieces, primarily diatonic. Dated. (Peters.) Level 2.

entries.title: 12 Little Fantasy Studies, Op. 13
entries.description: A specific technical challenge is emphasized in each study. See Study in C, which features broken third patterns, Songs Without Words, which employs broken chords and Little Stream, which exploits the trill. Most pieces are in the keys of C, G, F and D. Predictable writing. (Boosey.) Levels 2-3.

entries.title: 10 Miniatures From a Paris Window
entries.description: Tuneful writing, thin texture and harmonically conservative in two books each of five pieces. (Chester.) Level 4.

entries.title: Happenings
entries.description: These 17 studies feature five-finger patterns in major and minor keys. Wasp is bitonal with the right hand playing in G and the left in F-sharp. Other titles include All On a Summer's Day, Marching, In the Aeroplane, Chase Me, Imitation and Sailor Bold. (Banks.) Level 4.

entries.title: Sonatinas, Op. 40
entries.description: The entire set is subtitled Seasons and the four individual sonatinas are given subtitles which are the names of the seasons of the year: Op. 40, No. 1 (Spring), Op. 40, No. 2 (Summer), Op. 40, No. 3 (Autumn), Op. 40, No. 4 (Winter). Most of the first movements are in sonata form and three of the four sonatas conclude with rondos. See especially the finale of Winter, which interpolates the themes of "The Mulberry Bush" and "The First Noel." Limited harmonic language, conservative writing. (Peters.) Level 6.

entries.title: Etudes in Tonality, Op. 44
entries.description: Eight selections in 20th-century idioms. Titles are Prologue (jazz influence), Modal, Pentatonic, Atonal, Diatonic, Whole-Tone, Chromatic and Polytonal. Each etude features a specific technical skill. (Peters.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Polyrhythms, Op. 50
entries.description: Seven titled selections featuring a different meter for each hand: Canzonetta, two notes against three; Idylle, three against two; Vignette, six against three; Preambule, 12 against six; Impromptu-Appassionato, three against four; Nocturne, five against three; and Tango, irregular groupings. (Peters.) Level 10.

entries.title: Les Miroirs de Miro
entries.description: Four pieces for piano based on four paintings by Mir6. See especially Spanish Dancer, a one-page contemporary flamenco piece. (United.) Level 7.

entries.title: Bagatellen, Op. 12
entries.description: Pianistic and mildly dissonant. Strong rhythmic feeling. See especially Kleiner Marsch and Capriccietto. (Breitkopf.) Level 8.

entries.title: Kaleidoscope, Op. 19 (1945)
entries.description: Subtitled Six Brilliant Recital Pieces. Kaleidoscope presents contemporary writing for the elementary student. The style is highly rhythmic and generally sparkling. Titles include 1. March, 2. Zingara, 3. Musette, 4. Berceuse, 5. Chinese Carillon and 6. Burlesque. See especially the vigorous March. It would be especially good for a student with strong hands and a vibrant rhythmic sense. Also effective is the Chinese Carillon with its left-hand ostinato, played high in the treble register. (Associated.) Level 6.

entries.title: Pieces Enfantines, Volume 1
entries.description: Little-known suite of six interesting teaching pieces filled with vitality. Movements include Tendre Souvenir, Promenade, Sans histoire, /ouet mecanique, Princesse lointaine and Sur les chevaux de boil. (Durand.) Level 7.

entries.title: Imagenes Infantiles (Childhood Images)
entries.description: Two sets of four pieces each found under one cover and presenting sophisticated contemporary writing for the upper-intermediate student. Bitonal selections. See especially Na Pancha's Song and The Mischievous Boy. Level 8.

entries.title: Sonatine No. 2
entries.description: Attractive and highly rhythmic writing. Thin textures make this suitable for the advancing student with small hands. Requires energy and strong rhythmic sense. An unusual and yet readable contemporary work. Level 10.

entries.title: Sarabandes (1887)
entries.description: These works anticipated a harmonic language that was later adopted and developed by Debussy and Ravel. These three works initially must have sounded strange with their unprepared seventh chords and unresolved ninths. (Salabert.) Level 7.

entries.title: First Gymnopedie
entries.description: Lyrical work, opening with a four-bar introduction. Calls for the sustaining of a melodic line with long note values and a left-hand accompaniment with wide jumps. Extremely popular. Level 8.

entries.title: Second Gymnopedie
entries.description: Similar in texture to the First Gymnopedie. Lyrical melody over bass chords presenting a dreamy character. Relaxed writing in five sections. Level 7.

entries.title: Third Gymnopedie
entries.description: Compelling lyrical writing in a work with a relaxed tempo. Very well known. Level 8.

entries.title: Gnossiennes (1890)
entries.description: Three two-to-three-page works that were inspired by archaeological excavations at Knossos' palace in Crete. The orientalism might be a result of Japanese and other exotic music Satie heard at the Paris Exhibition in 1889. Barlines and key signatures are absent. These are the first compositions in which he gives humorous directions to the performer. Although they are often published with the Three Gymnopedie, these works are different in sound and style. Reading them as if they were in 4/4 time may facilitate the initial learning. (Alfred, Salabert, G. Schirmer and others.) Level 8.

entries.title: Sonneries de la Rose-Croix (Rosicrucian Fanfares) (1892)
entries.description: Movements include Air of the Order, Air of the Grand Marshal and Prelude of the Heroic Gate of Heaven. (Dover, Salabert.) Level 8.

entries.title: Le fils des etoiles (The Song of the Stars) (1892)
entries.description: In three movements, First Act Prelude (The Calling), Second Act (The Initiation) and Third Act (The Incantation). (Dover, Salabert.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Poudre d'or (Gold Dust) (c. 1901)
entries.description: Waltz with numerous artificial ritards. Not as successful as the other waltz le to veux. (Dover.) Level 8.

entries.title: Veritables preludes flasques (Pour un chien) (Genuine Flabby Preludes [for a dog]) (1912)
entries.description: No barlines or key signatures in these Flabby Preludes with humorous Latin instructions. Movements are Severe reprimand, All alone at home and Playing. (Eschig.) Level 6.

entries.title: Nine Children's Pieces (1913)
entries.description: Includes three groups of three pieces, all written in 1913. The works included are Childish Small Talk (Menus Propos Enfantins), A Child's Quaint Ways (Enfantillages Pittoresques) and Tiresome Pranks (Peccadilles Importunes). Sparse textures and uncomplicated pieces with running commentary on events concerning the life of a child such as "Get used to seeing a slice of bread and jam without wanting to steal it." Sophisticated writing, most appropriate for adult humor. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music, Novello.) Level 2.
Titles are Mountain Song of the Turkish Tryol, Skinny Dance and Espanana. No barlines or key signatures. (Cramer.) Level 7.

entries.title: Descriptions automatiques (Automatic Descriptions) (1913)
entries.description: These pieces begin to show Satie's imitation of Impressionism. Movements are About a boat, About a lantern and About a helmet. Part of Voici les tambours imitates a portion of Debussy's Minstrels. (Eschig.) Level 8.

entries.title: Chapitres tournes en tons sens (Matters Thoroughly Discussed) (1913)
entries.description: Movements are titled The Woman Who Talks too Much, The Rock Carrier and The Shut-Ins' Laments. Sophisticated writing, musically challenging. (Eschig.) Level 8.

entries.title: Embryons desseches (Dried Up Embryoes) (1913)
entries.description: An imaginary crustacean described in a preface to each movement is portrayed in the three movements. The second piece d'Edriophtalma includes a version of the Chopin Funeral March. (Eschig.) Level 10.

entries.title: Enfantillages pittoresques (Picturesque Child's Play) (1913)
entries.description: In three movements, Little Prelude to the Day, Lullaby and March of the Great Staircase. (Eschig.) Level 3.

entries.title: Je to veux (I Long for You)
entries.description: A waltz, traditionally notated and moving through many keys. Conservative and appropriately sentimental. Level 8.

entries.title: Menus propos enfantis (Childish Small Talk) (1913)
entries.description: More sophisticated musically than technically. Movements include The War Chant of the King of Beans, What the Little Tulip Princess Says and Waltz of the Chocolate with Almonds. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music, Eschig.) Level 3.

entries.title: Peccadilles importunes (Annoying Offenses) (1913)
entries.description: Titles of movements are Being jealous of your friend who has a swelled head, Eating up your friends piece of bread and Taking advantage of the corns on his feet to grab his hoop. (Eschig.) Level 3.

entries.title: Heures seculaires and instantanees (Times of Day, Then and Now) (1914)
entries.description: Musically sophisticated. Movements include Venomous obstacles, Morning light [from noon] and Stampeding boulders. (Eschig.) Level 9.

entries.title: Vieu sequins et vielles cuirasses (Antique Gold and Ancient Armor) (1914)
entries.description: Musically sophisticated. Movements include At the Gold Merchant's House, Armored Dance and The Defeat of the Cimbres. (Eschig.) Level 8.

entries.title: Sports and Divertissements (1914)
entries.description: Set of 20 one-page miniatures with witty commentary and no barlines and preceded by a mock chorale. Masterpieces of wit and irony. Topics deal with such diversions as golf and tennis. Much more difficult musically than technically. Needs a sophisticated performer. (Dover, Salabert and others.) Level 10.

entries.title: Sonatine bureaucratique (1917)
entries.description: The last of Satie's humorous pieces, this sonatina is closely based on the Clementi Sonatina in C Major, Op. 36, No. 1. Teachers and adults especially will enjoy the parody on this well-known teaching piece of Clementi. (Many standard editions.) Level 8.

entries.title: Five Nocturnes (1919)
entries.description: These short pieces represent Satie's return to formal writing. Absent are the humorous titles and unconventional commentary and directions to the performer. He intended the set of five to be his final solo piano works. These selections reflect the simplicity of Satie that is often overlooked amidst his need to shock or amuse. The first three are most frequently heard. (Eschig, Salabert.) Level 10.

entries.title: Inci's Book
entries.description: Short teaching pieces for children in which much of the writing is on the white keys and is sometimes modal. Well-crafted with titles that include Ind, Playful Kitten, A Tale, The Giant Puppet, A Joke, Lullaby and A Dream. (Southern Music.) Level 4.

entries.title: Small Serenade
entries.description: Suite of six movements titled Song, Riff, Rumba, Tango, Dream Waltz and Stomp. Intermediate level with thin textures and an easy-to-read score. Song is modal while Stomp and Riff touch on a popular style. A diverse set of pieces. (Elkan-Vogel.) Level 5.

entries.title: Orlando di Lasso
entries.description: The composer notes this as "a transfigured memory of two-part motets." Level 5.

entries.title: Domenico Scarlatti
entries.description: The figuration that opens and predominates in this selection is clearly an offshoot of a well-known Scarlatti sonata with its rapid, descending five-note step-wise figures. The most difficult of the set, but reflects the exuberance of Scarlatti's music. Level 8.

entries.title: In My Nine Lives
entries.description: All of these elementary contemporary pieces are based on themes concerning cats. The writing is some of Schickele's best. See especially With Closed Eyes, Kittenhood, Good Hunting, Parenthood and Coming of Age. (Elkan-Vogel.) Levels 3-5.

entries.title: Little Suite for Susan
entries.description: Originally conceived as a birthday gift of little piano pieces that Schickele's wife might be able to play. Seven short titled pieces with changing meters and some tricky passages. Titles include Dulcimer Tune, Blues Etude, Tango, Strut, Hymn, Fanfare and Dusk Song. (Elkan-Vogel.) Level 7.

entries.title: Razzle-Dazzle Triptych
entries.description: The three movements Prelude, Razzle-Dazzle and Afterthought are all written with a humorous spirit. The entire set takes only three minutes. These would be most appropriate for the adult student with sophisticated taste and a strong rhythmic sense. (Elkan-Vogel.) Level 7.

entries.title: Hollers, Hymns and Dirges
entries.description: Although subtitled by the composer as Eight Folk Song Settings, these pieces are anything but traditional settings. Textures are lean and sparse, and this contemporary writing is sophisticated. For each old American folk song, Schickele describes his thoughts or feelings concerning a time in his past when the folk song moved him. Adult students may be interested in this collection. Titles include Go Tell Aunt Rhody, Amazing Grace, Darlin' Corey and Ruby. (Elkan-Vogel.) Level 7.

entries.title: 12-Tone Suite
entries.description: Strong character definition between movements. The rhythmic framework is neo-Classical but the harmonic language is 12-tone. Sophisticated writing that an early-advanced student should handle well. The writing reflects the influence of Schloss' teacher, Alban Berg. The movement titles in this short suite are Prologue, Scherzo, March, Interlude, Air, Chaconne, Etude and Epilogue. (Peer International.) Level 10.

entries.title: Pupazzi, Op. 36
entries.description: Subtitled Petite Suite pour Piano, this work contains eight early-advanced works. (Mathot.) Level 8.

entries.title: Six Little Piano Pieces, Op. 19
entries.description: Found here are short, compact works that are thin in texture. The longest of the six atonal pieces comprises 17 measures, and three of the pieces are only nine measures long. The last is perhaps the most moving, with its two bell-like chords that sound throughout. (Universal.) Level 9.

entries.title: Potpourri
entries.description: A collection of solos for the elementary student, featuring contrasting characters and primarily thin textures. (Carl Fischer.) Level 3.

entries.title: Near and Far
entries.description: Subtitled "13 Musical Scenes for the Older Student," these pieces might be best for older students who are interested in contemporary writing. Sonority often takes precedence over melody, and students who enjoy atmospheric literature will be interested in these pieces. (Carl Fischer.) Level 3.

entries.title: Sonata Breve
entries.description: An eight-minute work in one movement with many tempo and mood changes. It is a study in warm sonorities and color from the tranquillo opening through large climaxes to the final decay of sound resulting from indeterminate repetitions of a motive at the end. (Oxford.) Level 10.

entries.title: Lyrical
entries.description: An ostinato bass consisting of a descending four-note motive pervades most of this movement. The right-hand melody is freely tonal. For the sophisticated ear. Level 9.

entries.title: Pensive
entries.description: Highly chromatic lyrical writing in a primarily chordal work. Calls for a mature musician. Level 8.

entries.title: Dynamic
entries.description: In three sections, each starting piano and growing to an intense forte, this work exhibits strong rhythms and much syncopation, with mirror construction between the two voices in the middle section. Dramatic writing with a tinge of jazz flavor makes it the most appealing movement of this set. Level 8.

entries.title: Three Score Set
entries.description: Three-movement work. The first is gigue-like and is in 6/8 with a moving melody. The second is a bitonal chord study, written almost entirely in blocked triads. The final movement is a dance, highly rhythmic, bringing the work to a rousing close. Excellent contemporary literature for the early-intermediate student. (G. Schirmer.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Zoo
entries.description: Subtitled "Animals for Piano," this work is a collection of highly descriptive pieces about the various animals at the zoo. Engaging writing, easy to listen to and highly descriptive. These pieces need imaginative interpretations and should be better known. All selections are intriguing, but see especially The Elephant, The Bear, The Monkey and The Giraffe. (Schott.) Level 4.

entries.title: Lotus Land, Op. 47, No. 1
entries.description: Perhaps his best-known piano work and rewarding to play. Lotus Land is filled with atmospheric passages, rich and sonorous chords, brilliant cascades and arpeggiations, all of which contribute to its effectiveness. (Many standard editions.) Level 9.

entries.title: A Song from the East, Op. 54, No. 2
entries.description: Appealing mostly for its evocative atmosphere. A staccato broken-chord bass in eighth notes stretching over a tenth forms the accompaniment during much of this Romantic piece. (Many standard editions.) Level 9.

entries.title: Danse Ncgre, Op. 58, No. 5
entries.description: A tonal and tuneful piece, filled with energy and brilliant right-hand passagework. Useful as supplementary literature to a student's standard fare. (Galaxy.) Level 10.

entries.title: Prelude in B Major, Op. 2, No. 2 (from Trois Morceaux)
entries.description: Subdued work with three-voice textures and triplet figurations. Highly expressive writing. Level 8.

entries.title: Prelude in A Minor, Op. 11, No. 2
entries.description: Calls for skillful use of rubato. Linear writing, highly Romantic. Level 9.

entries.title: Prelude in E Minor, Op. 11, No. 4
entries.description: Lyrical work with enchanting left-hand descending melodic motives. Calls for refined voicing and a good sense of rubato. Enchanting melancholy work. Scriabin first began this as a ballade. Level 8.

entries.title: Prelude in D Major, Op. 11, No. 5
entries.description: One of the most elegantly beautiful of the early works of Scriabin. Calls for a flexible left hand and a refined sense of pacing rolled chords. Level 10.

entries.title: Prelude in E Major, Op. 11, No. 9
entries.description: Andantino work, highly lyrical. Left hand contains interesting countermelodies to the chordal right hand. Level 9.

entries.title: Prelude in G-flat Major, Op. 11, No. 13
entries.description: Long lines prevail throughout this lyrical work that calls for a refined ability to voice the busy texture. Level 10.

entries.title: Prelude in D-flat Major, Op. 11, No. 15
entries.description: A lovely and simple work with a melody over a slowly moving accompaniment. The performer must sustain the forward movement of the phrases. Level 8.

entries.title: Prelude in G Major, Op. 13, No. 3
entries.description: Beautiful harmonic colors in a work with triplet accompaniment figures and two- and three-note chords in the right hand. Expressive writing. Level 8.

entries.title: Prelude in G Minor, Op. 27, No. 1
entries.description: Marked patetico, this resonant and sonorous work uses augmented sixths and many delayed resolutions of nonchord tones. Level 10.

entries.title: Three Pieces, Op. 45
entries.description: Titled Feuillet d'album, Poeme fantasque and Prelude, these selections display chromatic writing within a tonal framework. More difficult musi cally than technically. Level 8.

entries.title: 2 Preludes for the Intermediate Pianist
entries.description: A highly practical collection of works from throughout Scriabin's life for the upper-intermediate student pianist. (Presser.) Levels 8-10.

entries.title: Scriabin: Selected Works, ed. Baylor
entries.description: A practical performing edition containing a compilation of many genres including several poems, preludes from Op. 11, Op. 16, Op. 48, Op. 74, several etudes from Op. 42, Op. 65, the Fourth Sonata, an album leaf, several poems and other works. The variety of works found here makes it especially interesting. (Alfred.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: The Gnomes (Krasnoludki)
entries.description: A set of seven significant children's miniatures featuring strong writing that is mildly contemporary and quite pianistic. These works should be much better known and more frequently performed. Vividly illustrated. See especially Dance, Mazurka, Cradle-song and Oberek. (PWM.) Level 7.

entries.title: Waltz
entries.description: Wide skips and a disjunct melody in this dissonant three-page work for early-advanced pianists. The sometimes complex rhythms in the accompaniment will need careful study. (Merion.) Level 9.

entries.title: Seven Little Pieces
entries.description: Brief, untitled movements in which each piece presents certain pianistic problems, noted at the top of the piece. Dissonant writing for the sophisticated musician. (Presser.) Level 10.

entries.title: Notebook for Young People
entries.description: Shchedrin consciously wrote teaching pieces based on Soviet character and content.
