This work, a piano cycle for youth, much in the same vein as the Schumann Album for the Young and the Tchaikovsky Children's Album, contains 15 character pieces in a contemporary idiom. Highly pianistic. Interesting writing, of high quality. See especially No. 9 Fanfares, No. 13 Chase and No. 15 Etude in A. This Notebook is widely heard in Russia. (Sikorski.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Six Children's Pieces, Op. 69 (194445)
entries.description: This set of pieces was composed in one day by Shostakovich for his daughter Gayla when she was eight years old. Generally in two voices, these pieces can be highly effective in performance. Unfortunately many performers may shy away from them due to their disjunct lines and numerous accidentals. (Many standard editions.) Level 3.

entries.title: No. 1 March
entries.description: The best-known movement of the set, with "wrong-note" writing that pervades the texture and adds interest. The last three measures need careful work in fingering. Level 2.

entries.title: No. 2 Waltz
entries.description: Two-voice waltz in which both hands rarely move simultaneously. Many shifts of hand position. Level 2.

entries.title: No. 3 The Bear
entries.description: Again, two-voice writing, with predominantly disjunct melodic lines. The non-legato playing of quarter notes can create a lumbering effect while the big gestures help develop freedom of arms. Some unison playing. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 5 A Sad Story
entries.description: Lyrical writing. Provides a fine study in voicing and inflection of melodic lines. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 6 The Mechanical Doll
entries.description: This selection and the March are the best-known movements. Rapid passagework for both hands, "wrong-note" writing, and thin textures. Calls for fine attention to articulation. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 1 Lyrical Waltz
entries.description: Graceful work with an oom-pah-pah accompaniment. Provides preparation for Chopin playing. Not especially easy to read, calling for many different kinds of articulation within the piece. Harmonies change frequently and unpredictably, and the fingering is sometimes tricky in the right hand. For the sophisticated musician. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: No. 7 Dance
entries.description: Giocoso work for the student with good fingers and a strong sense of pulse. Quirky. Level 6.

entries.title: Events of a Day
entries.description: This set includes 21 pieces. Titles include The Break For Rest, Making Peace, At the River, Dance, Evening Country, Lullaby and Dream. Effective writing. (Sikorski.) Levels 6-8.

entries.title: 25 Pieces for Piano
entries.description: Collection of intermediate character pieces. Infrequently heard, but a gold mine for the performer looking for fresh modern literature in a conservative vein. (Sikorski.) Levels 7-9.

entries.title: I. (Allegretto)
entries.description: A saucy march in ABA form with dotted rhythms and sarcastic melodic phrases. Level 8.

entries.title: 11. (Andantino)
entries.description: A "wrong-note" waltz in ABA form. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: III. (Allegretto)
entries.description: A humorous polka with strong changes of mood and interesting key changes. This jovial work requires careful voicing and technical control in the double-note passages. Level 8.

entries.title: Prelude in F Minor
entries.description: For the musically sophisticated student, this folk-like piece with a haunting mysterious effect provides preparation for fugal playing. Highly contrapuntal with many accidentals. Levels 8-9.

entries.title: Aphorisms, Op. 13
entries.description: A little-known collection of 10 titled pieces, seldom heard, but providing substantive fare for the performer able to play the Bach Inventions. Primarily linear writing and thin textures characterize a work that should be better known. (VAAP.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: No. 1 (Moderato)
entries.description: A haunting and somewhat nostalgic prelude that opens with an Alberti figure in the right hand that then moves to the left. Wide keyboard range and contrapuntal interplay in this linear and moving work. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 2 (Allegretto)
entries.description: Brilliant chromatic scales and a light, dance-like nature mark this work. The rhythmic interest often falls on the third beat of the measure in the accompaniment. Cheerful. Level 10.

entries.title: No. 3 (Andante)
entries.description: Contains an almost Impressionistic effect in a serenely lyrical piece. Nocturne-like two-page piece with a dramatic intrusion near the close. Three-voice texture. Worth investigating. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 4. (Moderato)
entries.description: A serious three-voice fugue in 5/4 that is on the technical and musical level of some of the Bach Sinfonias. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 6 (Allegretto)
entries.description: Found here is an appealing satiric polka featuring dissonance, bitonality, wide interval skips, halting accompaniment and abrupt shifts of key. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 9 (Presto)
entries.description: Perpetual-motion piece with biting dissonances in two voices and featuring a whirling accompaniment in 6/8. Continuous triplet rhythms, bitonal dissonant effects and use of the extreme upper register of the piano characterize this effective tarantella. Two printed pages. Level 10.

entries.title: No. 10 (Moderato non troppo)
entries.description: A lyric processional with a typically Russian melody in a tender, poignant mood. The melody is made of some wide stretches. Requires a sensitive performer and a delicate touch and subtle dynamic control. Level 10.

entries.title: No. 13 (Moderato)
entries.description: A tiny but highly effective march with fife and drum effects. Uses the wide ranges of the keyboard. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 14 (Adagio)
entries.description: A weighty and somber work, perhaps the best of the set. Orchestral writing, requiring resonant sonorities, rich tone and featuring wide melodic intervals. Rises to a ,pq' climax before ending ppp within the 36-measure piece. This effective work has been orchestrated by Stokowski. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 15 (Allegretto)
entries.description: One of the most humorous and appealing of the Preludes, this waltz-like work is filled with "wrong-note" writing. The oom-pahpah accompaniment appears in the right hand and the melody in the left in the opening. Thin textured, witty. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 16 (Andantino)
entries.description: A stately march with amusing "wrong-note" writing and humorous twists of the phrase. The dotted rhythms in the accompaniment give this work its unique character. Bold and interesting. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 19 (Andantino)
entries.description: Lyrical writing in a 6/8 work, similar to one of Mendelssohn's barcarolle-like pieces from the Songs Without Words. Filled with harmonic richness. Level 10.

entries.title: No. 23 (Moderato)
entries.description: An Impressionistic piece with a chant-like melody appearing in the middle register of the piano and a triplet accompaniment figure in the upper part. Level 10.

entries.title: No. 24 (Allegretto)
entries.description: A satiric gavotte, highly effective as a close to this set of pieces. Level 10.

entries.title: Folk-Ways U.S.A., Volumes 1-5
entries.description: Siegmeister wrote a series of volumes subtitled "A Progressive Series of American Song Scenes and Sketches" as a corrective to his own uninteresting childhood experience studying piano with unmotivating literature. He tried to think of his own children in writing these volumes and to imagine the kind of music they would like to play. According to the preface, the composer bases this series on American folk and popular sources due to "the beauty of the music; because of its strong appeal to the American imagination; because it presents certain technical and musical features of great interest to the modern mind (i.e., modal scales, changing meters, syncopation, etc.); and because it is a means of introducing the child to the point of view of the American composer." Volume 1 is designed for the beginner and the pieces are based on folk songs such as Ev're Night, Down in the Valley, The Mocking Bird and One, Two, Three O'Leary. The arrangements are tonal. (Presser.) Levels 1-7.

entries.title: A Set of Houses
entries.description: Descriptive elementary pieces that depict different kinds of houses including No. 2 Two-Story House, No. 3 City House, No. 5 Monkey House, No. 6 Old Stone House, No. 7 Penthouse, as well as others. This set of pieces should be much better known. The writing is mildly contemporary and quite pianistic. (Carl Fischer.) Levels 3-4.

entries.title: The Children's Day
entries.description: Strong writing appears in these six character pieces. The harmonic palette is mildly contemporary and the works will be attractive to the performer and audience alike. This collection should be better known. See especially No. 1 Sunny Morning, No. 2 Skipping Rope, No. 3 Playing Clown and No. 5 Catching Butterflies. (MCA.) Levels 4-5.

entries.title: American Kaleidoscope
entries.description: Twenty strong pieces in a variety of moods, especially appropriate for small hands. See especially No. 3 Song of the Dark Woods, No. 5 Street Games, No. 15 The Chase, No. 17 Marching and No. 19 Sunny Day. (Sam Fox.) Levels 4-5.

entries.title: 51 Minitudes for Piano
entries.description: Mini-etudes for piano, often humorous. Each one illustrates some quirk of thematic, melodic, canonic or harmonic manipulation. They are, according to the composer, "curt, terse and quick" with some of them only four measures long. The unusual titles include Bach x 2 = Debussy, A National Anthem in Search of a Country, Czerny Shmemy and Blitzpartie. Novelties. (G. Schirmer.) Level 8.

entries.title: 28 Easy Pieces
entries.description: These tonal, pianistic pieces are progressive in difficulty, spanning the elementary to intermediate levels. The character differences between movements are distinctive and these works fit the hand well. Titles include Conversation, Czech Folksong, Jolly Etude, Dance, Morning Prelude and Mazurka. Fine writing from the Russian school. (Associated.) Levels 4-6.

entries.title: Five Pieces for Young People
entries.description: Primarily linear writing, often modal, in a set of pieces that sound more difficult than they are, perhaps as a result of the interesting use of rhythms. See especially Finger Play and Little Fanfare. (Carl Fischer.) Level 2.

entries.title: Martha Through the Looking Glass
entries.description: Three pieces with contemporary sonorities and angular writing. Titles are The White Knight, It's My Own Invention and The Upside-Down Deal Box. (Boosey.) Level 8.

entries.title: A Visitor's Album
entries.description: Four short pieces in a highly contemporary style. Titles are In Sohmertime, The Dog Star (Lullaby), The Dancing Lesson (Rondo) and Madrigal. (Boosey.) Level 9.

entries.title: Dance Card
entries.description: The four movements are neo-Romantic takeoffs on dances. This is music that would be especially appropriate as "light American music" to end a program. Traditional harmonic framework with some dissonance. Titles include Tango Bolshoi, Diabelli Polka, Valse Tristan and Prater Rag. (Merion.) Level 10.

entries.title: Faces of Jazz
entries.description: Twelve titled pieces in original jazz idioms for the intermediate pianist. See especially Off-Beat Shorty, That's Mike and Goin' in a Hurry. Should be better known. (Marks.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Evocation
entries.description: Twelve-tone work with strong character, flexible rhythms and full sonorities. Level 9.

entries.title: Episodic Suite
entries.description: See especially the movements titled Yellow and Blue, March and Toccata. Biting contemporary sounds in a palatable style. (Mowbray/Presser.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Note Pages, Volume I (1974)
entries.description: This set of 44 brief avant-garde piano pieces contain titles such as Chasing About, Beckoning and Autumn Poem. Contemporary techniques include notation for free repetitions, clusters, free accelerandos and ritards and playing with the side of the hand. Should be better known. (EMB.) Levels 5-7.

entries.title: Note Pages, Volume II (1976)
entries.description: These 23 avant-garde pieces are slightly more difficult than those in Volume I. The range of works extends from slow, one- or two-line com positions to longer pieces that are rapid, lively and transparent. Each piece is based on a single musical thought and the forms are concise and clear. (EMB.) Levels 7-9.

entries.title: 12 Pieces for Ten Fingers (1963)
entries.description: Literature for beginners by a major contemporary American composer. The first ones use only quarter and half notes, and all are progressive in difficulty. Contemporary idioms are explored at the beginning level, including pentatonic writing and changing meters. All selections are titled. (Sam Fox.) Levels 1-2.

entries.title: ABE, GABE, ADA, FAE and ED
entries.description: All of the notes in the piece (the letter-names of the notes) consist of the five names in the title. Level 1.

entries.title: In the Mirror
entries.description: Each hand plays a mirror image of the other. Only quarter and half notes are used and hands remain in relatively stationary positions. Somewhat academic, but the form is clear. Level 1.

entries.title: Echo-Chamber
entries.description: Sustaining pedal is held for long periods causing blurring of sonorities. Written primarily with long sustained note values. Level 1.

entries.title: Turn-Me-Round
entries.description: A short piece that can be played upside down or in retrograde. Exact mirror images are used. Starer states in his preface that this is "a game for people who like puzzles ... [like] 'Madam I'm Adam."' No eighth notes are used. Level 1.

entries.title: Countdown
entries.description: Each consecutive bar has one beat less than the one before. The player is to speak a number, from nine to one, to "blastoff" at the beginning of each measure as the piece progresses. No eighths are used. Level 1.

entries.title: Evens and Odds
entries.description: The meter changes in each measure from even to odd numbers of pulses such as from 4/4 to 3/4 to 4/4 to 5/4 and so on. Blocked thirds in the left hand. Level 1.

entries.title: Up and Down, Right and Left, Over and Across
entries.description: This selection asks the student to change fingers on the same note and to cross hands. Level 2.

entries.title: Darkness and Light
entries.description: The music contrasts low with high, threatening sounds with pleasing sounds, dissonance with consonance. Many sustained sonorities. Level 2.

entries.title: Adding and Taking Away
entries.description: Notes are added to form clusters and are then taken away, one by one, to return to a single note. One of the most effective selections in this volume. Level 2.

entries.title: Walking with Two Fingers
entries.description: Played with the second and third fingers only. Many 3-2 and 2-3-2 eighth-note fragments appear between the hands. Level 2.

entries.title: Sliding Into the Keys
entries.description: Demonstrates the concept of modulation. Attractive bouncing rhythm throughout. Level 2.

entries.title: 12 Notes 12 Times
entries.description: This one-page selection shows 12 ways of presenting all of the 12 notes: in fourths, fifths, the chromatic and the whole-tone scale and in chords. Dense texture. Level 2.

entries.title: Seven Vignettes (1950)
entries.description: Short character pieces in contemporary idiom, filled with color. See especially Fanfare, Chorale and Toccata. (MCA.) Level 7.

entries.title: Four Seasonal Pieces (1985)
entries.description: Contemporary reflections. Titles include As the Gentle Wind, Orange Sun, Leaves are Falling and Leaves on the Frozen Pond. (MCA.) Level 7.

entries.title: 1. Purple
entries.description: Expressive, polytonal work to be played slow with intensity. Subtle dynamics are required. Levels 6-7.

entries.title: 4. Bright Orange
entries.description: This jazzy piece, good for a student with flair, is marked to be played fast and light. The use of jazz syncopations makes it especially appealing. Many students may be inclined to rush the rhythm. Levels 6-7.

entries.title: 5. Grey
entries.description: Built on four forms of a 12-tone row and marked slow, without expression. Generally soft dynamic level with some patterns (and many intervals of the seventh). Level 6.

entries.title: 6. Pink
entries.description: Tonal writing that is song-like and sentimental, especially appropriate for the expressive performer. The melodic leaps should be played expressively. A favorite. Level 6.

entries.title: 7. Crimson
entries.description: Based on asymmetrical divisions of 7/8 meter. This fast and driving work contains dynamic ranges from pp to if. Appealing writing for the energetic performer with fast fingers and a light touch. Level 7.

entries.title: 1. Maroon
entries.description: This piece is almost pure color; the melody is difficult to find, if not absent, and the rhythm is not especially memorable. Cluster chords, register shifts and sudden dynamic changes. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: 2. Aluminum
entries.description: Rhythmical work marked to be played fast and even. Combines added-note chords in parallel motion with polytonality. Level 8.

entries.title: 3. Silver and Gold
entries.description: A slow, reflective and expressive work with left-hand ostinato. "Silver, the ostinato accompaniment in the left hand, constantly repeats its 12 notes, like a row," Starer said. "Gold is threaded against it, with D as tonal center-a point of departure and return." Levels 7-8.

entries.title: 4. Khaki
entries.description: Sounds of drum rolls, bugles and rifles. Crisp and energetic writing featuring cluster chords, changing rhythmic patterns and a wide dynamic range. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: S. Pepper and Salt
entries.description: Chords built of identical intervals playing against clusters. The three basic ideas on which this work is built include the widely spaced eighth notes alternating between hands, the repeated-note rhythmic pattern and the cluster chords in several ranges. Unpredictable and non-melodic. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: 6. Aquamarine
entries.description: Bluesy and mellow, this work features quintuple meter, jazz rhythms, a left-hand accompaniment with blocked and broken chords and a right-hand melody with subtle dynamic changes. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: 7. Chrome Yellow
entries.description: Highly rhythmic with nonsymmetrical rhythms, marked to be played in a "brittle" manner. Level 8.

entries.title: 1. Pastorale
entries.description: Marked to be played lento, un poco rubato, this work is highly improvisatory and features a quasi-oriental flavor. Students with a vivid imagination will enjoy this work. Level 8.

entries.title: 2. Little White Sheep
entries.description: Opens with seven staccatos, repeated treble Cs, in a two-page piece with much repetition. Effective rhythms and thick texture. This work is in a playful vein. Level 7.

entries.title: 3. Dance
entries.description: This Allegro giocoso work uses a wide keyboard range and features interesting and unexpected syncopations in a contemporary harmonic framework. Interesting writing and quite pianistic. Level 8.

entries.title: 1. Dialogue with the Self
entries.description: Marked Deliberate. Many different tempo and character changes are present. The different registers suggest two different personalities. Level 8.

entries.title: 2. Opening Petals
entries.description: Flowing and elegant writing. Clusters expand to wider intervals in this lyrical two-page piece which should be played with slight rubato. Level 7.

entries.title: 3. Dreams of Glory
entries.description: A march featuring triplets, chordal accompaniment and dotted rhythms. Sharp dynamic contrasts and use of all registers of the keyboard. Level 8.

entries.title: 4. In the Birdcage
entries.description: Imitations of bird songs in the right hand. Dreamy work requiring freedom and rubato. Level 8.

entries.title: 5. A Faded Old Photograph
entries.description: An elegant waltz in a contemporary idiom, with changing meters. Level 8.

entries.title: 6. Pop-Time
entries.description: Energetic work in a jazzy idiom. One of the favorites from this collection. Needs sharp rhythms and attention to articulation. Level 9.

entries.title: 7. Herman The Brown Mouse
entries.description: A one-page scherzo, delicate and impish. Marked to be played cautiously, delicately. Level 8.

entries.title: 8. A Small Oriental Vase
entries.description: Emphasizes lyrical playing and tone production. Marked gently flowing, with intensity. Level 8.

entries.title: 9. Steps to the Attic
entries.description: Tone clusters in a quick, march tempo. Needs sharp staccatos and bite. Level 8.

entries.title: 10. Shadows on the Wall
entries.description: Marked slowly, creepily. Changing meters and mysterious writing. Level 8.

entries.title: 11. Deep Down the Soul
entries.description: Serious chordal piece to be played with dignity. A somber, probing work. Level 8.

entries.title: 12. Dancing Next Door
entries.description: Quick and light piece in mixed meters, shifting from 10/8 to 7/8 to 9/8 and so on. Effective in performance and good for the performer who enjoys scherzando works and has a strong sense of rhythm. Level 9.

entries.title: Hexahedron (A Figure Having Six Faces) (1971)
entries.description: Six contrasting pieces presenting fine contemporary writing for the advancing performer. Titles are 1 Musingly, 2 Gurglingly, 3 Coolly but not chilly, 4 Doggedly, mulishly, almost pig-headedly, 5 Philosophically and 6 Frantically. These pieces should be heard more often. (MCA.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: Robert Starer Piano Solos
entries.description: A volume containing a wealth of solo piano music by Starer including Games With Notes and Numbers, At Home Alone, both sets of Sketches in Color and Four Seasonal Pieces. (MCA.) Levels 1-10.

entries.title: Six Modal Miniatures
entries.description: Different and little-known teaching pieces, each built on a different mode. The modes are explained at the beginning of the volume. This collection could also be used for analysis in a student theory class. (Oliver Ditson.) Level 4.

entries.title: Six Little Pieces for the Piano
entries.description: Two-voice modal writing, totally on the white keys, with most of the pieces approximately eight measures long. The score is published in manuscript form. (American Composers Alliance.) Level 1.

entries.title: Five Little Five-Finger Pieces (1954)
entries.description: Hands remain in stationary positions. The writing is modal or bitonal, and predominantly contrapuntal. Several are canonic. No note values smaller than the quarter note are used. For the sophisticated beginner. (Helios.) Level 1.

entries.title: Music for Ann (1953)
entries.description: Prelude, Trumpet Tune, Chorale, Quiet Song and Piece for Skipping. Modal writing, sophisticated tonal framework. (Helios.) Level 2.

entries.title: Five Portuguese Folksongs (1968)
entries.description: Folk songs transcribed by Halsey Stevens for piano and placed in simple arrangements for the elementary student. Interesting settings, with some octaves and ninths. Worthy competition repertoire for elementary performers. (Peer International.) Levels 3-4.

entries.title: 17 Piano Pieces
entries.description: Brief character pieces for the intermediate student. Dissonant writing best suited for the sophisticated musical ear. Representative titles include Erratic Rhythms, Palindrome II and From a Roman Sketchbook. (Westwood.) Level 8.

entries.title: Sonatina No. 1 (revised version, 1959-67)
entries.description: Neo-Classical linear writing that is pianistic and mildly contemporary. Adult students might be more drawn to this literature. Allegro giusto, Tempo di menuetto and Allegro. (American Composers' Alliance, Helios.) Level 8.

entries.title: Sonatina No. 2 (1959)
entries.description: Absolute music, better played by seasoned musicians than by young students. The interpretative considerations outweigh the technical problems. Allegro moderato, Canon, Rondo. (Helios.) Level 9.

entries.title: Sonatina No. 3 (1950)
entries.description: Linear writing that is more dissonant than the other sonatinas. The second movement is an Elegy that concludes the sonatina quietly. (Helios.) Level 9.

entries.title: Notturno-Bellagio Adagio
entries.description: According to Maurice Hinson, this piece is "characterized by beautiful singing lines, concise and clear form (ABA), eloquent chromatic motifs, and logical transfer of the line between the hands. Dynamic indications are sparse but the natural dynamic fluctuation is highly expressive." Three pages. (Alfred.) Level 8.

entries.title: Fireworks
entries.description: Chordal outlines often prevail in this contemporary nonfunctional harmonic writing for the liberated musical ear. Titles include Sky Rocket, lumping lacks, Volcano, Silver Spray and Roman Candle. (Fentone.) Level 7.

entries.title: 20 Piano Pieces for the Young
entries.description: Vibrant, engaging writing and generally Romantic sonorities. These would be interesting to investigate for a contest listing. (Peters.) Level 6.

entries.title: No. 1 Andantino
entries.description: An excellent study in legato, with both hands remaining in the C position. Also stresses independence between the hands. A justifiably well-known beginner selection to work on phrasing and musicianship skills. Level 1.

entries.title: No. 2 Allegro
entries.description: Helps develop coordination between the hands and facility in passage playing in the right hand. No black keys are used, but the left hand must voice lower notes of accompaniment figures as melodies. Level 1.

entries.title: No. 3 Allegretto
entries.description: Comes across almost as a drone since the range of pitches is so narrow. Modal sounds. Only white keys are used. The coordination between the hands is somewhat difficult here. Level 2.

entries.title: No. 4 Larghetto
entries.description: A siciliano, this work is a study in playing a legato melody and in voicing a melody above double notes in a single hand. Difficult musically and technically compared to the other pieces in the volume. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 5 Moderato
entries.description: This short piece also requires voicing a melody above double notes in the right hand as well as "voicing down" an insistent repeatedchord accompaniment. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 6 Lento
entries.description: Strongly modal writing with some shifting hand positions. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 7 Vivo
entries.description: Modal writing. Broken-chord accompaniment. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 8 Pesante
entries.description: Syncopated chords, though the harmonic language is not that of jazz. Perhaps the most immediately appealing of the set. Level 3.

entries.title: Three Fairy Tales
entries.description: Three suites of pieces on the fairy tales of Cinderella, jack and the Beanstalk and The Sleeping Beauty, all contained under one cover. The movements of the various pieces, featuring thin textures and mild dissonances are titled and indicate the progress of the story through the music. Sophisticated writing and effective characterization of the stories. These pieces should be heard more often. (Peters.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: 15 Character Pieces for Piano
entries.description: While these pieces are not technically difficult, the musical understanding necessary to play and enjoy them far surpasses the technique required. Linear dissonant writing, thin textures. (Peters.) Level 8.

entries.title: Sonatina No. 1
entries.description: The last of the three movements, Rondo, with unexpected rhythmic shifts and bucolic character, make it among the most appealing of Soulima Stravinsky's works. Students with small hands could play this work. Level 8.

entries.title: Sonatina No. 2
entries.description: This sprightly work consists of two fast movements, Allegro moderato and Tarantella (vivace). Agile fingerwork is needed for the fast figures in the tarantella. May be played by a student with small hands and strong fingers. Level 7.

entries.title: Sonatina No. 3
entries.description: The first movement, Fantasia piccola corrente, is a solo movement for the right hand, written on one staff and consisting of a single melody. The last movement, Epilogue tranquillo, is a left-hand solo, also written on one staff and containing only a melody. A contemporary march with linear writing and strong rigorous rhythms joins the two movements. Musical sophistication is called for in interpreting this sonatina. Level 8.

entries.title: Sonatina No. 4
entries.description: Fanfare, Plainchant, Bourree d'Auvergne. The Fanfare has the most immediate appeal with its strong, energetic rhythms and tuneful motives. Level 7.

entries.title: Sonatina No. 5
entries.description: The first movement, Quodlibet, features melodic dialogue between the hands. The tuneful final Rondino is perhaps the most immediately appealing movement of the set. Level 7.

entries.title: Sonatina No. 6
entries.description: Composed on themes by the 14th-century composer Machaut. The first movement is a theme and variation, the second an inviting Fughetta and the third a lively rondo, obviously modal in character. One of the best of the set. Level 8.

entries.title: Piano Variations, First Series
entries.description: Several sets of variations appear within each volume. Linear writing, contemporary harmonic framework. The dissonance would be appreciated by an experienced musician whose musical aptitude might be ahead of his technical ability. Each movement is in a specific variation form such as sectional variations, continuous variations or ground bass variations. Movements include Prelude, Forlane, Stanza, Piccolo Divertimento, In Modo Russo and Les Valses. (Peters.) Level 8.

entries.title: Piano Variations, Second Series
entries.description: Similar to the First Series of variations by S. Stravinsky. Titles include 11 Tones, Metrics, 12 Tones and Pavana. See especially the Pavana. (Peters.) Level 8.

entries.title: The Art of Scales
entries.description: According to the composer, this volume contains 24 "exercises set in the form of short preludes, whereby the phrasing of a scale in one hand is always governed by the musical content of the other hand." More of intellectual interest. (Peters.) Level 9.

entries.title: Three 3-part Inventions
entries.description: Contemporary contrapuntal studies in a mildly dissonant fashion. (Peters.) Level 10.

entries.title: Hungarian Children's Songs
entries.description: These 25 Hungarian melodies are arranged for the elementary pianist. Melodies were taken from the Collection of the Hungarian Folk Music, edited by BartOk and Kodaly. Fine arrangements, should be more widely used. (Boosey.) Levels 3-5.

entries.title: Sonatine fur Klavier, No. 1 (1919)
entries.description: The tuneful first movement is highly motivic while the andante molto third movement is folk-like and features interesting syncopations. (EMB.) Level 6.

entries.title: Album Secondo Per Daniela
entries.description: Highly contemporary writing with new notation utilized for several of the short pieces. Accessible avant-garde literature. (Berben.) Level 7.

entries.title: The One Who Is Wiser Concedes
entries.description: Rollicking vivo in 6/8 requiring agile fingerwork and a strong rhythmic sense. Level 7.

entries.title: The Sad Toreador
entries.description: An amusing takeoff on the Toreadors Song from Carmen with "wrongnote" accompaniment. Level 8.

entries.title: The Butterfly Who Woke Up in Winter
entries.description: Reflective, swaying piece with delicate figurations in the right hand. Marked larghetto. Level 8.

entries.title: A Bird, Op. 1
entries.description: Two-page solo with mixed meters. An accompanimental motive depicting a mournful bird recurs under a sorrowful melody played by the right hand. (Strangeland.) Level 8.

entries.title: Prelude in G Minor, Op. 3a
entries.description: Quasi-Baroque. A good study for a student to precede the Bach inventions or Handel fugues. (Strangeland.) Level 7.

entries.title: Children's Treasure Box, Volume 1
entries.description: This group of 20 short pieces is written to introduce various aspects of contemporary piano technique to the first- or second-year student. Both hands remain in stationary five-finger patterns throughout each piece. (Strangeland.) Level 1.

entries.title: Children's Treasure Box, Volume 2
entries.description: The 17 pieces found here continue to develop the various aspects of contemporary piano technique introduced in Volume 1. The level of difficulty is slightly greater, and the student is required to play outside stationary hand positions. These are less contrapuntally oriented than in Volume 1. (Strangeland.) Level 2.

entries.title: Children's Treasure Box, Volume 3
entries.description: Additional group of 12 piano pieces. The works feature broader contrasts in tempos, key signatures and dynamics and more extended hand positions. (Strangeland.) Level 3.

entries.title: Children's Treasure Box, Volume 4
entries.description: This is the last in the series. (Strangeland.) Levels 3-4.

entries.title: Work and Play
entries.description: Short character pieces for beginners, each tuneful and enchanting. True musical miniatures with few notes for the beginner. Traditional harmonies. (Associated Board of the Royal Schools of Music.) Level 2.

entries.title: Selected Piano Pieces, I
entries.description: Pedagogically sound, musically inviting and exciting character pieces of high quality. Similar in quality to the Bartok teaching pieces in For Children but these would be even more accessible musically. The folk tune basis is not as strong here as in the Bartok pieces. Volume I includes 11 short pieces. (EMB.) Levels 4-5.

entries.title: Selected Piano Pieces, II
entries.description: These pieces are slightly more difficult than those in Volume I. Titles include Sonatina, All' Concerto, Prelude and Fugue, Rondo and Spring Wind. (EMB.) Levels 6-7.

entries.title: Piano Compendium
entries.description: During his life, it appears that the composer planned to write a number of practice-oriented books on the theory of composition. lstvan Barna and Matyas Kovacs have compiled this Piano Compendium from sketchbooks found after Szervanskzy's death. The writing is based on the modern Hungarian school of composition of Kodaly and Bartok. These 33 selections are primarily of historical interest. (EMB.) Levels 6-8.

entries.title: 3 Sonatines pour piano
entries.description: All three are in three movements of one page each and the writing is sparse in texture and straightforward. See especially the third movement of Sonatina No. 1 based on children's playground mocking themes. Little-known and interesting writing. The manuscripts of these works were discovered in the 1970s. (Lemoine.) Levels 2-3.

entries.title: Fleurs de France
entries.description: Pleasant if dated elementary pieces depicting various flowers of different regions of France in one- or two-page vignettes. Primarily linear writing with a tonal basis. (Lemoine.) Level 6.

entries.title: Lieder von der Murinsel
entries.description: Included here are folksongs from the Medumurje region in Yugoslavia, arranged for piano by the composer. The melodies are based primarily on modes, most often the Dorian, and the settings are similar to some of Bartok's. Rhythmic and inviting. No titles are given for the folksongs. (Henle.) Levels 3-4.

entries.title: Serbian Dances
entries.description: Similar in concept to the Bartok Rumanian Folk Dances. Folk melody basis, rhythmic, modal with a harmonic underpinning. (Rongwen.) Level 8.

entries.title: Little Sonata, Op. 51
entries.description: Pianistic and interesting contemporary two-movement sonatina at the intermediate level that is worth investigating. (Doblinger.) Levels 5-6.

entries.title: Sounds and Colours, Op. 95
entries.description: This collection of 15 intriguing miniatures which extend from the tonal sounds of Liszt and Bartok to more avant-garde colors. Selections include Study in Sounds (muted strings, free notation), Sounds and Colours (spacial notation), Echo (spacial notation, clusters, cardboard muting of white and black keys), Chinese Chimes (new notation, improvisation within a note group, free ordering of sections) and Sounds of the Night (spacial notation, free repetitions, strumming inside the piano). (Doblinger.) Levels 7-9.

entries.title: No. 3 In a Great Hurry
entries.description: Changing meter in this presto scherzando and humorous work that calls for a staccato technique in a fast tempo. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 12 Toccatina
entries.description: Patterned piece featuring black keys in one hand and white in the other. Requires playing with alternating hands and rapid hand-tohand scale passages. This staccato study needs fast fingers and attention to even sounds. Level 9.

entries.title: For Me, Op. 76
entries.description: Delightful little recital pieces for the upper-elementary student. The harmonic palette is mildly contemporary and the writing especially fine. See especially No. 14 Raindrops and No. 21 The Little Fly. Should be heard more often. (Doblinger.) Levels 3-4.
Highly recommended 20th-century pedagogical literature. (Universal.) Levels 5-6.

entries.title: When the Frog Wandering Goes
entries.description: Delightful and little-known set of six imaginative and energetic pieces. All movements are strong, but see especially Merry Andrew, Tsheremis Dance, When the Frog Goes Wandering and March. (Doblinger.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Sonatine
entries.description: The second movement is a one-page ornamented Nocturne and the final movement is especially effective with lively folk-like writing. (Doblinger.) Level 10.

entries.title: Toccata, Op. 54
entries.description: Primarily continuous sixteenth notes in a rousing and effective toccata. A Lento rubato, quasi Fantasia section immediately prior to the final Coda breaks the momentum slightly. Requires attention to evenness of passagework and pacing of large crescendos. Convincing and brilliant writing. (Doblinger.) Level 10.

entries.title: Soundshots
entries.description: Twenty intriguing character pieces in a biting contemporary style. Titles include Run, Rabbit, Run!, Strolling, Pitter-Patter, Jumping High, End of Day and The Clocks. Strong writing by an American female composer, suitable for festivals of American and contemporary music. Selections are generally one to three pages. (Hinshaw.) Levels 5-7.

entries.title: Do-Re-Mi de coucours
entries.description: Eight short pieces, several of which are of strong musical interest. See especially Recit, Fanfare and Silhouette. Appropriate for the student playing his or her first "classics." (Lemoine.) Level 3.

entries.title: Concert en famille
entries.description: Twelve-tonal teaching pieces, tuneful and varied. See especially Promenade dans les bois, a march-like piece that builds to a brilliant climax before subsiding. Especially suitable choices for contest repertoire. Strong writing. (Musicales Transatlantiques.) Level 6.

entries.title: Happy Time, Book 1 (Primary)
entries.description: The three volumes in Happy Time include some of the most accessible contemporary literature available by a major composer for an earlyelementary pianist. Often the pieces are somewhat contrapuntal, making them especially suitable for students needing to develop independence between the hands. All books provide original music that is interesting and musically alive while teaching technique progressively. Book 1 includes 15 pieces for the early-elementary student and the level is approximately the same level as the Kabalevsky Music for Children, Op. 39. (MCA.) Level 2.

entries.title: Both Ways
entries.description: Slow lyrical piece that is a good repertoire choice to work on developing legato in a young student, and on developing independence of the hands. The melody shifts to the left hand in the middle of the piece while the accompaniment is comprised primarily of a descending chromatic scale in whole notes. Level 2.

entries.title: Little Gavotte
entries.description: Animated and decisive, appropriate especially to help students work on moving out of stationary hand positions. Correct fingering and finger crossings need special attention. Level 2.

entries.title: Common Tones
entries.description: Early hands-together playing in a piece that could serve as a student's first work in bringing out a left-hand melody "over" a right-hand accompaniment. Excellent material for study or for sight-reading. Level 2.

entries.title: Arabia
entries.description: The exotic flavor of Arabia renders it perhaps the most popular selection in this volume. A legato melody sounds above a bass two-bar ostinato. The modal writing may catch a student's interest. Level 2.

entries.title: Shadow
entries.description: Contrapuntal writing in this canon at the octave. Level 2.

entries.title: Sailors' Dance
entries.description: One of the most tuneful and energetic pieces in the volume. Coordination between hands will be a primary technical consideration. Level 3.

entries.title: Melody
entries.description: Highly lyrical work comprised of broken descending chords divided between the hands. Requires voicing within a single hand. Melody sounds more difficult than it is. Level 2.

entries.title: Popular Air
entries.description: The patterned left hand will make this one-page piece accessible to most young students. Appealing melody with nontraditional accompaniment, all within a tonal framework. The first section features a simple melody and the second features double notes. Level 3.

entries.title: Happy Time, Book 2 (Elementary)
entries.description: The selections here are decidedly more difficult than those in Book 1. Included are 13 selections, most of which are one page. (MCA.) Levels 4-5.

entries.title: Caravan
entries.description: Left-hand ostinato might depict the slow lumbering of a caravan in the midst of its trek. Lyrical melody is stated first in single notes and then as a duet. Level 4.

entries.title: Perpetual Motion
entries.description: This work is in some ways a single long crescendo. Melody in left hand in half notes and quarters occurs underneath the perpetual motion patterned sixteenths in the right hand. Contains little musical value, but could be dazzling. Level 4.

entries.title: Pursuit
entries.description: Marked allegro con moto, this brief selection provides a fine study in coordination between the hands. Highly motivic, vigorous music. Level 5.

entries.title: Chorale and Variation
entries.description: Three-voice chorale requiring skillful pedaling and providing good preparation for the playing of four-part writing such as that in modern hymnals. The fingering is well-marked but not easy. Levels 3-4.

entries.title: Happy Time, Book 3 (Intermediate)
entries.description: Perhaps the strongest book in the series. Many of the pieces are reminiscent of other composers' styles. It seems strange that this exceptionally fine collection of pieces is so little known. (MCA.) Level 6.

entries.title: A la Schumann
entries.description: One-page piece with a thick texture and voice leading moving from hand to hand as in the writing of Schumann. Voicing of melodies and even control of the texture is needed here. Level 7.

entries.title: Organ
entries.description: Primarily chordal writing, especially good for practice in playing in four-part harmony and in voicing thick textures. Exceptionally effective melodic writing. Level 6.

entries.title: Light Waltz
entries.description: Perhaps appropriate for the student who wishes to play Debussy and is not ready for that music. Level 7.

entries.title: Night Mood
entries.description: Another Impressionistic one-page vignette, expressive and accessible for the average student. Worth investigating. Level 6.

entries.title: Arioso, alla J. S. Bach
entries.description: Largo movement in overture style with dotted rhythms and full chords. Sounds big. Level 6.

entries.title: Oriental Dance
entries.description: Rhythmic and driving with repeated tone clusters. Not as effective as some of the other selections in this set. Level 7.

entries.title: Iberian Mood
entries.description: Short, Spanish-sounding piece. Little movement on the keyboard in the left hand. Level 6.

entries.title: In Memory of George Gershwin "1925"
entries.description: Shows Gershwin's international influence at the time when the Tansman pieces were composed. Blues tempo, ornamented righthand melody. Level 7.

entries.title: Finale, Solo-Piece
entries.description: Lively work that could serve as a recital piece for a talented elementary school student. Level 7.

entries.title: Piano in Progress, Volume 1
entries.description: Tansman intended the two volumes in the series to be a kind of "Gradus ad Parnassum" geared to the early years of piano study. Volume 1 is easier than Volume 2, but the selections within each book are not necessarily arranged in progressive order. The writing is pedagogically strong, using patterns that appear commonly in standard piano literature. See especially No. 2 Romance, No. 4 Modulation, No. 7 Tango, No. 9 Etude and No. 10 Joking Mood. (Marks.) Levels 2-3.

entries.title: Piano in Progress, Volume 2
entries.description: Excellent elementary literature in a mildly contemporary, almost neoRomantic idiom. Selections Nos. 11-19 are contained in this volume. See especially No. 12 Descending, No. 13 Rustic Dance, No. 16 Intermezzo, No. 18 Spleen (Tempo di Blues) and No. 19 Going Ahead. (Marks.) Level 4.

entries.title: No. 1 Old Song
entries.description: Right-hand double notes with a lyrical left-hand melody. NeoRomantic writing in a beautiful work that should be better known. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 3 The Bouncing Ball
entries.description: One-page perpetual motion figure with continuous sixteenths in the right hand and a bouncing eighth-note figure in the left hand. Energetic and highly effective in performance. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 4 The Dancing Bear
entries.description: Pesante work with a lugubrious left hand and heavy chords. The rhythm of the right hand adds interest. Level 2.

entries.title: No. 6 Russian Dance
entries.description: Needs rhythmic vitality without rushing and contains several offbeat accents. One of the most effective in this collection, sounding more difficult than it really is. Level 3.

entries.title: No. 7 Dresden China Figures
entries.description: Suitable for a performer who needs to work on playing with finesse and polished articulation in the context of a short piece. Needs a "proper" minuet style, graceful slurs and gentle staccatos. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 9 Skating
entries.description: The performer who needs to develop scale facility might benefit from working on this short piece that features both ascending and descending scales as well as broken thirds and octave stretches. Level 3.

entries.title: No. I Stroll
entries.description: Could be mistaken for one of the easier pieces by Gurlitt. The writing is highly lyrical and requires a fine legato. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 2 In the Garden
entries.description: Song-like, with melody in the right hand and accompaniment in the left. The disjunct melody makes the matching of the sound of each note to the one previously played especially important. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 3 Mazurka
entries.description: Melody with many directional changes above repeated two-note accompaniment chords. Tuneful, lively and appealing work. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 4 The Arithmetic Lesson
entries.description: The attractive melody with its catchy tune may camouflage the fingering difficulties in the left-hand crossings. The writing is almost sing-song. Should be appealing to many. Level 5.

entries.title: No. 10 Arabian Nights
entries.description: Requires imagination in interpretation for this slow, mysterious work. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 12 Parade
entries.description: March-like with repeated notes in both hands. Somewhat repetitive, but the rhythmic patterns are enticing to both performer and listener. The performer needs a strong rhythmic sense. Level 5.

entries.title: No. 1 Awakening
entries.description: Most of the playing is done in C Major and in the center of the keyboard, which gives it a bright feeling with the close voicings. The right hand features a good bit of double-note passagework. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 2 The Warbler
entries.description: One of the most effective pieces in the four sets, this work is a perpetual-motion piece with a right-hand melody depicting a warbler and a left-hand accompaniment in repeated chords, necessitating a light touch. Less difficult than it sounds. Level 5.

entries.title: No. 3 Noel
entries.description: Naive simplicity in a melody heard against a repeated-chord accompaniment. Fits the hand well. This is the most accessible selection in this set. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 6 Rest
entries.description: Calm mood depicted through a repeating left-hand accompaniment and a lyrical melody. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 9 A Difficult Problem
entries.description: A fast finger-twister, especially appropriate for a student who likes to decipher patterns. This piece fits the hand well, but the key of E Major makes it appear more difficult than it is. Level 6.

entries.title: No. 12 Ping-Pong
entries.description: A quick but short perpetual-motion work that depicts the bouncing back and forth of a ping-pong ball. Requires fine coordination of double-note passages that nevertheless fit the hand well. Effective writing. Level 7.

entries.title: I. An Old Tale
entries.description: Slow and melancholy writing with some chromaticism and dissonance. A fine piece for developing voicing and for studying continuity of line and phrasing. Can easily be over-pedaled. Levels 6-7.

entries.title: II. Rocking Horse
entries.description: Highly pianistic work in A Minor with a left-hand accompaniment spanning a tenth. Musical writing, energetic tempo and easier than it looks on the page. The first and third phrases feature the Natural Minor mode while the second and fourth phrases feature the harmonic and Melodic Minor forms. Can help in developing left-hand wrist rotation, coordination between two hands, clarity and quick chordal changes. Level 7.

entries.title: III. A Serious Moment
entries.description: An expressive piece with delightfully colorful harmonies, especially good for developing chordal playing in the right hand and for voicing melody over thick texture. Should be better known. Level 6.

entries.title: IV. Hide and Seek
entries.description: The energy in this spunky piece is generated from the repeated chords and figures. Right-hand passages in double-fourths need a supple wrist. Most of the piece is staccato. Large fortissimo climactic ending. Level 7.

entries.title: V. In a Venetian Gondola
entries.description: Quiet legato left-hand figure in an ostinato rhythm that spans an eleventh and requires careful fingering in the crossings and attention to shaping. The right hand frequently sustains one voice while another is moving. More difficult than it sounds. Level 7.

entries.title: VI. Blues Record
entries.description: The most accessible work in this volume. Bluesy and short, with fairly stable hand positions. Suitable for a student needing motivation. Level 6.

entries.title: VII. Valse lente
entries.description: Slow chromatic waltz with an unpredictable harmonic framework. An appropriate early 20th-century choice for the student who enjoys Romantic styles and textures. Short and effective. Level 7.

entries.title: IX. Berceuse
entries.description: Mostly played on the black keys, this delicate and graceful piece fea tures various recurrence of the main theme in variation. Each fourbar phrase adds or subtracts countermelodies or chordal reinforcement in the right hand. Excellent study in voicing for both hands. Level 7.

entries.title: X. Marche Militaire
entries.description: Dry and rhythmic, especially appropriate for a performer who can play large chords. Descending left-hand octaves provide a full effect. Theme and variations with the theme reappearing in different guises against the left-hand ostinato accompaniment. Level 7.

entries.title: 10 Diversions for the Young Pianist
entries.description: See especially Spanish Mood, Merry Go Round, Rainy Day, Speeding Along, Mischief and Toccata. For the student with flair. (Associated.) Level 7.

entries.title: Five Impressions (1934)
entries.description: Especially suitable for the student who enjoys playing works in the style of Debussy. Interesting harmonies, pianistically conceived. Entire set of strongly contrasting pieces consists of six pages. Titles include Calm, Burlesque, Triste, Anime and Nocturne. (Eschig/Associated.) Level 9.

entries.title: Quatre Danses Polonaises
entries.description: Little-known. See especially No. IV Oberek. (Eschig.) Level 10.

entries.title: Toccata
entries.description: Dedicated to Ginastera, this bold work sounds more difficult than it is. Especially suitable for the performer with flair and the ability to move quickly about the keyboard. May be considered as a precursor to the BartOk Allegro Barbaro. (Barry.) Level 9.

entries.title: No. 1 (Allegro marziale)
entries.description: The best known and most popular of the set. Martial, driving rhythm and martellato octaves with the hands in close range. Effective ending. Level 7.

entries.title: No. 2 (Con vivacita)
entries.description: Alternating meters, and staccato playing with parallel eighth-note motion between both hands. Some left-hand tenths may need to be rolled. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 3 (Vivo)
entries.description: A staccato dance with biting vitality. Parallel melody and staccato chords. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 4 (Lento con tristezza)
entries.description: A sad work with a left-hand ostinato that pulsates through much of the piece. Incorporates an aria from one of Tcherepnin's own works. Expressive and effective writing, especially appropriate for the performer who enjoys Romantic literature. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 5 (Dolce)
entries.description: Sensitive harmonic coloring within a nocturne-like work. Tcherepnin stated that the extended chords in tenths may be played instead in closed position to avoid the rolls. Needs refined voicing of tops. One of the best in the collection and especially good for the sensitive student. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 6 (Allegro con spirito)
entries.description: Easier than it sounds or appears on the page. The left-hand brokenchord accompaniment creates a swirling effect beneath a slower melody in this perpetual-motion work. Tcherepnin says that this was composed on a cold Russian winter day when, in the middle of winter, the sky was blue, it was sunny and his thoughts turned to spring. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 7 (Prestissimo)
entries.description: Effective in performance for a performer with competent facility. Calls for strong fingers. Much of the staccato playing is highly patterned. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 8 (Allegro)
entries.description: A seldom-heard chordal march filled with zest and energy. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 9 (Allegretto)
entries.description: Calls for fine fingerwork in playing the staccato passages. One of the more popular selections from this volume. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 10 (Presto)
entries.description: Exciting work built on chromatic scales and other passages in eighths against a chordal accompaniment. Fits the hand well. Level 8.

entries.title: Pieces Without Title, Op. 7, W8
entries.description: Eight character pieces, most of them in three-part forms. See especially 1 Allegro, 5 Allegro molto, 6 Sostenuto and 8 Impetuoso. (Durand.) Level 9.

entries.title: Canzona, Op. 28
entries.description: The sections of this four-page work are slow, fast, slow, fast in a subdued piece with thin textures. Interesting writing that uses extreme registers. (Simrock.) Level 8.

entries.title: Four Romances, Op. 31
entries.description: Pieces are in free form, with each dedicated to a friend. Piano writing is based on an invented nine-step scale. These short pieces are to be considered as four movements of one work. (Universal.) Level 8.

In 1925 while in Liseux, France, Tcherepnin was impressed by the crowds praying that Theresa would be made a saint. He read her book and visited places connected with her. In this series of children's pieces, Tcherepnin wanted to show the stages of her life. The 13 pieces are written to show the simplicity of her faith. A folk-like tune, quoted at the beginning of the book, is used in several additional pieces including Nos. 3, 8, 9 and 13. (Durand.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Piano Method on the Pentatonic Scale, W141
entries.description: This was composed as a result of Tcherepnin's visit to China in 1934 during which he became aware of the need for a piano method in Chinese. The method uses the pentatonic scale and was completed in Beijing in 1935. (Shanghai-Commercial Press.) Levels 1-6.

entries.title: Chinese Bagatelles, Op. 51, No. 3
entries.description: Consists of 12 one- to two-page pieces with strong rhythmic vitality and open harmonies, reflecting an Oriental flavor. Dedicated to 10 young pianists who took part in a concert of the Bagatelles, Op. 5 in a concert in Beijing. This work is the third part of Tcherepnin's work Piano Etudes on the Pentatonic Scale. (Heugel.) Level 7.

entries.title: For Young and Old, Op. 65, Volumes 1 and 2 (Pour Petits et Grands)
entries.description: Twelve moderately difficult pieces in two volumes of six. Character pieces, generally in three-part forms. These pieces are notably popular in France. See especially 4 Les Contrastes, 6 La Babillarde, 7 L'A ffligee, 11 Les Plaisirs du Toutou and 12 Le Belle au bois dormant. (Durand.) Levels 5-7.

entries.title: Expressions, Op. 81
entries.description: Composed when an American publisher suggested that he wanted some pieces similar to the Bagatelles, Op. S. In the composer's explanatory remarks on the inside front cover of Expressions, Tcherepnin wrote, "The title 'Expressions' indicates that the music is subjective, dynamic and meant to give scope to a performer's expression of his own feeling in relation to the musical content of the pieces." Neo-Romantic and more rhythmically complex than the Bagatelles, Op. S. Ten pieces with titles printed at the ends of the pieces. See especially Entrance, which is Stravinsky-like and contains broken octaves and marcato playing; At the Fair, which is a witty polytonal work with changing meters; and At Dawn, which features shrill bird calls and bird chirps. (MCA.) Level 8.

entries.title: Episodes (Priskasi) (Short Stories)
entries.description: These pieces, written in his early childhood and youth, are in a mildly contemporary idiom, and would provide fine study material from the 20th century for a performer able to play the more advanced works from the Anna Magdalena Bach Notebook. Worth investigating. (Heugel.) Levels 5-7.

entries.title: A Sunny Day, Op. Posth. (1915)
entries.description: Composed in 1915, Tcherepnin found and recopied this work on the day of his death in 1977. This two-page solo is filled with parallel triads and is marked moderato tranquillo with several tempo changes. (Theodore Presser.) Level 4.

entries.title: Trois Preludes en forme de Blues
entries.description: The sophisticated blues feeling here makes these pieces more appropriate for adults who know swing style and popular music than for young students. Primarily chordal and a large span is required. The blues feeling is unmistakable. (Max Eschig.) Level 9.

entries.title: Songs Without Words, Op. 82, W 98
entries.description: Two- and three-page works in Romantic forms with a contemporary sound. Titles are at the ends of the pieces rather than at the beginning. These works avoid repetition and extremes of dynamics and range. See especially No. 4 Skomorokh which is based on syncopations. The final work, No. 5 Schmkhar-Venokhe is a kind of processional based on medieval chant and presenting a long crescendo from the beginning to the end in ff. (Peters.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: 17 Easy Pieces in Contemporary Piano Literature, W 175
entries.description: These pieces were composed in 1954 and are included in six volumes titled Contemporary Piano Literature, selected by Frances Clark and Louise Goss, and published by Summy-Birchard. They reveal Tcherepnin's talent for composing with limited technical resources. A strong contribution to the elementary contemporary teaching literature. The pieces are Book 1: March, Joy and Tears, Relays; Book 2: Melody, To and Fro, Chimes; Book 3: Prelude, The Clock, Hide and Seek; Book 4: Valse, Merry-Go-Round, Old Tale; Book 5: Escapade, Frolics, Ivan's Accordion; Book 6: Happy Stowaway, Mic and Mac. (Summy-Birchard.) Levels 3-5.

entries.title: Quatre Nocturnes
entries.description: These four gentle one-page nocturnes are wistful and nostalgic. The linear writing has a natural shape and flow. Generally featuring few technical difficulties. The first movement is lively and animated while the third movement, Allegretto, is reminiscent of a music box and contains a recurring melody. (Universal.) Level 9.

entries.title: Vingt Pieces Faciles
entries.description: These short untitled teaching pieces have thick textures with many accidentals. (Salabert.) Level 8.

entries.title: Album D'Amis
entries.description: The sophisticated harmonic language and overall style of this later work of Tansman make it most effective for the mature musician with a strong imagination. Nine miniatures are found here, requiring less in the way of technique than in musicianship. (Max Eschig.) Level 9.

entries.title: Four Pieces from Before
entries.description: Composed in 1957 by Ivan at the age of 19 as a Christmas gift for his father. Riding the Clouds is in a contemporary idiom and contains no barlines. (Boosey.) Level 7.

entries.title: Inventions (1961)
entries.description: Composed to represent three different treatments of the two-part invention. No. I builds on the idea of fluid chromatic horizontal continuity. No. II is in a 12-tone idiom and uses two five-note motives. No. III is constructed diagonally, and the writing is quite angular. (Belaieff.) Level 10.

entries.title: Tarka-Barka, A Microcosmic Collection of New and Extraordinary Pieces for Piano
entries.description: Collection of about 90 true avant-garde miniatures for piano, some only two lines long. The wealth of techniques used includes contemporary notation (a detailed key to the signs used is provided at the beginning of the collection), various techniques inside the piano, the use of the fist, palm and forearm, various cluster formations and a variety of pedal techniques. Excellent introduction to avant-garde music for the intermediate performer. (EMB.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Portraits, Album 2
entries.description: See especially Aria: A Portrait of Germaine Hugnet; Portrait of R. Kirk Askew; Portrait of Ramon Senabre; Sea Coast: A Portrait of Constance Askew; and Meditation: A Portrait of Jere Abbott. (Mercury.) Level 9.

entries.title: Portraits, Album 3
entries.description: See especially Toccata: A Portrait of Mary Widney, Pastorale: A Portrait of Jean Ozenne and Souvenir: A Portrait of Paul Bowles. (Mercury.) Level 10.

entries.title: Nine Portraits (1974)
entries.description: Sparse textures and easy to read. See especially Madame Dubost chez elle, Russell Hitchcock, Reading and Ettie Stettheimer. (Southern Music.) Level 8.

entries.title: 13 Portraits
entries.description: See especially Clair Leonard's Profile; Florine Stettheimer: Parades; Jamie Campbell: Stretching; Peter Monro Jack: Scottish Memories; and Persistently Pastoral: Aaron Copland. (Boosey.) Level 10.

entries.title: 19 Portraits for Piano (1981)
entries.description: Many of the portraits in this volume are one page long, slightly shorter than in other volumes. See especially Bill Katz: Wide Awake; Norma Flender: Waltzing; Richard Flender: Solid: Not Stolid; Barbara Upstein: Untiring; and Craig Rutenberg: Swinging. (Boosey.) Levels 9-10.

entries.title: 17 Portraits for Piano (1982-84)
entries.description: See especially Dennis Russell Daiver: In a Hammock; Rodney Lister: Music for a Merry-go-round; and Louis Rispoli: In a Boat. (G. Schirmer.) Level 10.

entries.title: Prelude
entries.description: Slow three-page work that builds to an effective climax. Many open chords and parallel fifths. (McAfee.) Level 8.

entries.title: Burlesques, Op. 31
entries.description: Set of three jovial pieces. The harmonic palette displays biting dissonances and the rhythms are generally exciting. The second is the most accessible, and only the third is titled. (Schott.) Level 10.

entries.title: No. 3 The Juggler
entries.description: By far the most popular. A sparkling concert piece calling for facile fingerwork and the ability to control a rapid tempo. Easier than it sounds and effective in performance. (Schott.) Level 10.

entries.title: Three Piano Pieces, Op. 32
entries.description: The first piece is delicate, almost Impressionistic at times. The second is a pastoral work, while the third moves about the keyboard using a full range of dynamics. This last work is a restless piece and the most difficult of the three. (Schott.) Level 10.

entries.title: Five Caprices, Op. 36
entries.description: Interesting works at the intermediate level. See especially No. 2 with running passagework and No. 5, an exuberant piece. (Associated.) Level 9.

entries.title: Echoes of a Small Town, Op. 49 (Kleinstadtbilder)
entries.description: Set of 14 attractive and relatively short intermediate character pieces, all of which require strong characterization and feature biting dissonances. This set could be considered as an excellent modern counterpart to the Debussy Children's Comer and Schumann Scenes from Childhood. See especially No. 5 The Organ-Grinder, which features changing meters and syncopated melodic lines, No. 6 Marching Geese in which the geese strut along in an almost mechanical manner, No. 12 In the Marketplace, which depicts movement and rushing people and No. 14 Street Song, the longest, and carefree. (Schott.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Diversions, Op. 78a
entries.description: Five sophisticated one- or two-page pieces that are primarily linear and which require detailed attention to articulation. No. 3 features changes in meter in a quiet duet while No. 4 is light and fast with touches of humor. (Leeds.) Level 7.

entries.title: Sonatinetta, Op. 78b
entries.description: Extroverted work, generally in two voices. The first movement, "allegro and gay," contains percussive added-note chords. See especially the witty third movement, a miniature march. (Leeds.) Level 9.

entries.title: Three Little Dances, Op. 85
entries.description: The first is written to be played only on the black keys, the second on the white keys and the third on both. See especially the third dance, notable for its lively, vigorous rhythm and biting sonorities. Dissonant and robust writing, even flamboyant. (Belwin-Mills.) Level 10.

entries.title: Reflections, Op. 86
entries.description: Five one-page pieces. These neoclassic pieces are relatively easy to read, but seem to lack the depth of some of his other collections. The textures are not thick. (Belwin.) Level 7.

entries.title: 10 Studies for Beginners, Op. 59
entries.description: Linear writing with many open intervals. Good studies in hand independence but the writing is somewhat dry. (Schott.) Levels 2-4.

entries.title: 10 Easy Studies, Op. 58
entries.description: Most are in two parts and highly linear. Excellent studies in 20thcentury contrapuntal writing. (Schott.) Levels 5-7.

entries.title: 10 Studies of Medium Difficulty, Books 1 and 2, Op. 57
entries.description: At times more in the vein of character pieces than etudes. Predominantly linear, with some fingerings provided. (Schott.) Levels 8-10.

entries.title: To the Rising Sun, Op. 4, No. 1
entries.description: Atmospheric character piece. It builds to a brilliant climax, portraying the noonday and afternoon sun, and then closes gently and quietly as the sun sets. Passionate and sustained writing. Melody is found first in the left hand and later in the right hand. (Alfred.) Level 8.

entries.title: Ninerias, Set 1 Op. 21, (1919)
entries.description: Eight titled selections that are slightly more difficult than those in the Gypsy Dances. Titles include Jeux, Berceuse, Danse des poupees and A la memoire dun bebe. Interesting and little-known works. (Salabert, Rouart.) Level 10.

entries.title: I. Caminando (Strolling)
entries.description: Rather brief Andantino movement appropriate for the performer needing additional experience playing chords and octaves. Requires careful voicing of chord tops and melody over chordal accompaniment in the same hand. The performer needs a strong inner sense of rhythm. Level 8.

entries.title: 11. Se acercan soldados (Soldiers coming)
entries.description: Fanfare-like work containing many different ideas. Appealing, but more difficult than it appears on the page. Essentially a march with low, rich basses and some double-note playing in the right hand. Level 8.

entries.title: ❑I. La aldea duerme (The village sleeps)
entries.description: Impressionistic work, appropriate for the performer able to play Debussy The Little Shepherd. Effective cadenza in the middle of this lento work. More difficult musically and interpretatively than technically. Level 7.

entries.title: IV. Amanecer (Dawn)
entries.description: One of the most effective pieces in this collection, beginning pp and growing to an effective, full-chorded , climax at the majestic close. Warm sonorities. Level 7.

entries.title: V El mercado (The Market Place)
entries.description: Bustling with energy portrayed in the continuous sixteenth notes heard throughout. Invention-like imitative writing. Requires careful attention to fingering. Level 7.

entries.title: VI. Duo Sentimental
entries.description: Flowing work that features dialogue between voices. Frequent shifts from eighths to triplets. Calls for finger independence. Dynamic range is primarily p to ppp. Level 7.

entries.title: VII. Fiesta
entries.description: The most effective work in the set. Highly rhythmic and festive in character. Should be heard more frequently. Level 7.

entries.title: VIII. La Vuelta (The Return)
entries.description: Recalls themes from other movements in a disjunct two-page work. Needs inherent musicality to bring it off. After several tempo changes, the work concludes with a quiet ending. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 1 Zambra
entries.description: Slow introduction followed by an energetic dance with percussive "strums" that suggest the guitar. Primarily chordal and rhythmic. Level 10.

entries.title: No. 2 Danza de la Seduccion
entries.description: Flowing and expressive ternary-form work that reveals the influence of the Impressionists. Many altered and seventh chords, sparse textures and changing rhythms. The most accessible movement in this volume. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 3 Danza ritual
entries.description: Repetitive work in minor with some ninth chords. Contains fewer skips than other movements in this set. Two pages. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 4 Generalife
entries.description: Describes what was originally a summer palace of the Moors in Granada, near the Alhambra. Based on an actual gypsy dance, a polo, this movement features frequent hemiola-type syncopations, fast broken arpeggios and parallel triads in imitation of a guitar. One of the best movements in the set. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 5 Sacro Monte
entries.description: Gypsy dance, opening with imitations of dancers clapping their hands and stomping their feet. This work depicts the fury of a dance from the famous caves at the Sacro Monte near Granada. The entire movement is a gradual crescendo through two-note figures, then triplets and finally sixteenths. Figuration fits the hand especially well but finger strength is required. Demands rapid movement about the keyboard. The guitar idiom is always present here. Level 8.

entries.title: Ninerias, Set 1, Op. 56
entries.description: A second set of eight titled works in the same idiom as the earlier set. Titles include Entree de Conchita, A l'ecole, Divertissement, Parade and Carnaval des enfants. (Salabert.) Level 10.

entries.title: Fanfare (Trompeteria)
entries.description: Filled with imagery including a march, trumpets and drum rolls. Introduction, ABA sections and Coda. Rapidly repeated triads, leaps and sequential writing. Levels 8-9.

entries.title: Jugglers (Equilibristas)
entries.description: Note repetition, broken-chord patterns and skips over the keyboard describing various juggling acts. Contains an eight-measure introduction followed by passagework in which alternating hands play repeated sixteenth notes in sequential figuration. Demands a light, bouncy sound. Level 9.

entries.title: The Bareback Rider (Amazona)
entries.description: Begins with introductory material from Fanfare. The music could be interpreted as suggesting a horseback rider in a trot, moving to a gallop represented by the continuous use of sixteenth-note figurations. Spirited writing. Level 9.

entries.title: The Trained Dog (El perro sabio)
entries.description: Technically the easiest movement of the set. Playful work, at times pentatonic and Impressionistic. Note the dog bark in measures 13-14 and 17-18. Exploits high registers and calls for a light touch overall. Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Clowns (Payasos)
entries.description: Perhaps the most effective movement of the work. The tempo changes several times between Allegro and Andante and returns to Allegro. A favorite with students, perhaps due to the quick shifts of mood and a highly effective glissando. The chord sequences suggest a boisterous mood. Level 9.

entries.title: The Aerialists
entries.description: Hand-over-hand arpeggios set the mood for this work. A later contrasting section with Debussy-like harmonic color over a slowly moving bass separates the lyrical statements of the theme. The theme of the Coda is from the Fanfare movement. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 1 Fiesta de las Calderas
entries.description: Tempo changes call for a fine improvisatory flair. Rhythmic allegretto middle section and strong Spanish flavor. Level 8.

entries.title: No. 4 Danza Ritmica
entries.description: Reminiscent of the popular Sacro Monte from Danses Gitanes, Op. 55. Dazzling. Level 9.

entries.title: No. 5 Seguiriva
entries.description: Rhythmic and exciting. The several measures of consecutive fourths in the right hand need attention. This movement requires flair and drive. Level 9.

entries.title: Viaje Maritimo (1930)
entries.description: Three highly descriptive character pieces that provide interesting writing for the advancing student wanting to play Spanish music. Titles are Luz en el mar, En fiesta and Llegada a! Puerto. (Schott.) Level 9.

entries.title: Radio Madrid (1931)
entries.description: Contains three sets consisting of a prologue and three pieces each. The pieces can be played separately. Strongly rhythmic writing and straightforward harmonies. Worth investigating. See especially Fetes a Seville and Carrentra Castellana. Shorter than the pieces in Viaje Maritimo. (Schott.) Levels 8-9.

entries.title: Postcards (1931)
entries.description: Set of five pieces, each representing a snapshot of a different place in Spain. See especially the suave Madrid and the Basque Dance, composed in 5/8 and chordal and strongly rhythmic. A fine collection of high quality and technically accessible Spanish music. (Schott.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: At the Shoemaker's (1933)
entries.description: Set of six character pieces with strong characterization. See especially The Peasant's Boots, The Shoes of the Ballet Dancer and Shoes of the Toreador. Should be better known. Intriguing works that require flair. (Schott.) Level 7.

entries.title: The Turina Collection, ed. Maurice Hinson
entries.description: Collection of 20 piano works including selections from At the Shoemaker's, The Circus, Miniatures, Postcards, Radio Madrid and Sea Voyage. An interesting, varied collection. (Schott.) Levels 6-9.

entries.title: Five Sonatinas
entries.description: Appealing works utilizing popular idioms in classical forms. References to ragtime, rock, blues and folk music. Should be better known. (Associated.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: A Little Piano Book
entries.description: Titles include Valse Lente, Nocturne, Canon, Two-part Invention in F, Twopart Invention in E-flat and Two-part Invention in G. In general the music is not particularly inspired. (Oxford University Press.) Level 4.

entries.title: Cirandinhas
entries.description: Twelve folk song arrangements that are published separately and in collection. The accompaniments are simple, and the scores are relatively easy to read. These harmonically agreeable works are filled with the spirit of Brazilian folk music. Excellent teaching pieces and ones that should appear more frequently on competition lists. (Eschig.) Level 7.

entries.title: Circle Game (Brinquedo de Roda)
entries.description: Simple piano adaptations of popular Brazilian children's songs. Titles include The Poor Little Country Girl and My Mother Used to Sing a Lullaby Like This. (Peer.) Level 6.

entries.title: Francette et Pia
entries.description: A group of 10 short piano pieces intended for the famous French pianist Marguerite Long to use in her teaching studio. In this set of pieces, the composer follows the adventures of a Brazilian boy and a French girl. The story encompasses the boy Pia's arrival in France, the moment when he first sees Francette, their initial conversation, their playing together (in the fourth piece) and their arguing in the fifth piece, Francette is Angry. In the sixth piece, Pia Went to War, he leaves, and in the eighth piece Francette shows how lonely she is without him. In the seventh piece, the opening is a Brazilian theme in march tempo, although as the work progresses, Francette's own French theme is heard. Pia returns and Francette is happy to see him again. In the ninth piece, Francette is Happy, the composer alters the previously used children's themes and depicts their happy reunion. The set closes with a four-hand duet titled Francette and Pia Play Together Forever. (Eschig.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Volume I
entries.description: Contains five well-written selections on Brazilian children's songs. Titles are Dawn, Full Tide, The Rose Bush, Little Lame Girl and On the Strings of a Viola. (Consolidated.) Levels 7-8.

entries.title: Volume III
entries.description: Five works. See especially I. 0 Pastorzihno with an ostinato and a highly rhythmic bass, III. A Freira and IV. Garibaldi foi a Missa. Effective writing. (Eschig.) Level 6.

entries.title: Volume XI
entries.description: Additional works from Villa Lobos' Practical Guide are in this volume. Strongly rhythmic, generally animated and require flair. Several require a wide span. Especially absorbing selections are Little Dove, Tiny Dove, Circle Dance, Constant and The Castle. (Southern Music.) Level 10.

entries.title: No. 4 The Little White Dress
entries.description: Ragtime style is recalled with the sixteenth-note motives and syncopated patterns. Features a predominance of broken thirds. For students with small hands. Level 4.

entries.title: No. 1 Alnitah
